companyDescription:
The US Census indicates Lubbock’s population is 254,565, making it the 11th largest City in Texas. It is anticipated that Lubbock’s population will be 275,000 by 2022.

The City of Lubbock has a diverse team of professionals. There are approximately 2,100 full time City employees performing various duties including engineers, park maintenance workers, accountants, police officers, attorneys, IT professionals, fire fighters, street maintenance workers, animal control officers, linemen and meter readers, building inspectors, librarians, and more.

Welcome to the Official City of Lubbock LinkedIn account. Follow us on Facebook & Twitter @cityoflubbock, & visit us at www.mylubbock.us

title: Videographer & Content Producer
descriptionHtml:
Pro Athlete is looking for a high-output Videographer & Content Producer to join our creative team. We aren't looking for a traditional film director; we need a "one-person-band" who thrives on speed and autonomy. You will be responsible for the end-to-end production of video content for JustBats, JustGloves, JustPaddles, and Field General - from setting up the lights and capturing audio to executing fast-paced edits with simple motion graphics.

The ideal candidate can walk into our studio, grab a new product, and turn around a high-quality, "social-ready" video in a matter of hours, not weeks.

Who we are:

We are Pro Athlete. We are an eCommerce company that specializes in creating a World Class customer and employee experience and we happen to sell sporting goods and apparel through various online platforms such as JustBats, JustGloves, JustPaddles, and Field General. We have a Major League caliber culture, we have a passion for what we do, and we love to have fun. No, really, we LOVE TO HAVE FUN!

Alignment with our Core Values is a must! Please do not apply if you don't believe in our Core Values or don't plan on living them out to their fullest. We receive a lot of applicants, so do your best to separate yourself from the rest. Do something to "wow" us and get us excited about the potential of you joining our team!

How We Play the Game:

  • We live out our Core Values
  • We have genuine curiosity
  • We make every detail matter
  • We have each other's backs
  • We take the high road
  • We are operationally predictable with no surprises
  • We stay connected
  • We hold each other accountable
  • We put our pride aside
  • We create a drama-free work zone
  • We never get complacent

Essential Roles & Responsibilities:

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • End-to-End Production: Manage the entire lifecycle of a video project, including setup, filming, audio recording, and post-production
  • Rapid Editing: Execute high-energy edits under tight deadlines to support our fast-moving e-commerce product launches
  • Studio Mastery: Expertly handle studio lighting and audio configurations to ensure all products (bats, gloves, paddles) are showcased with premium visual quality
  • Motion Graphics: Use After Effects to integrate text callouts, transitions, and brand elements that elevate the final product
  • Collaboration: Work alongside the content and marketing teams to brainstorm and execute videos that drive engagement and sales
  • Asset Management: Organize and archive raw footage and final exports to ensure a streamlined workflow for the entire team

Position Requirements:

  • Technical Portfolio: A reel or portfolio demonstrating strong lighting techniques, clean audio, and fast-paced editing
  • Software Proficiency: Expert knowledge of Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, Davinci Resolve, Capcut, or similar editing software
  • Equipment Knowledge: Comfortable working with professional DSLR cameras, lighting kits, and diversified audio setups depending on the situation
  • eCommerce Speed: Proven ability to work in a high-volume environment where quick turnarounds are the standard
  • Self-Starter Mentality: Ability to take a concept and run with it solo—you don't wait for a crew to get to work
  • Passion for the Game: A genuine interest in sports or the ability to quickly learn the nuances of baseball, softball, and pickleball gear

All Jobs at Pro Athlete Require:

  • Core Value alignment. Our Core Values are the driving force behind how we do business.
  • Self-driven work ethic. You must be a self-starter who is resourceful, loves taking initiative and seeing things through to completion
  • Genuine curiosity and the desire to learn. We are ever-changing and need employees who are innovative and adaptable

Position Type and Expected Hours of Work:

This is a full-time position that is expected to meet our hybrid model. This position needs to be located in the Kansas City metro area.You may be required to be on-site more than not due to content and product shoots.

Travel:

Travel may be expected for various shoots but mostly local.

Other Duties:

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

If we've just described your dream job, we invite you to browse through our company website to learn more about us. We think you'll like what you see.

E-Verify:

This organization participates in E-Verify to confirm employment eligibility. For more information, please view the information below by copying and pasting the URL into your browser or contact TOPS@proathleteinc.com.

  • https://www.e-verify.gov/employees/e-verify-overview

Pro Athlete is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran, or any other applicable protected classes. Pro Athlete prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind and is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all.

companyDescription:
At Pro Athlete, Inc. we strive to make your online shopping experience a grand slam every time. While doing so, we also work to make baseball and softball better games for all those involved.

We might look like we are a sports agency or a sports training facility because of our unique culture, but we aren't. title: Recruiter
descriptionHtml:
Senior / Principal Tech Recruiter

Fully Remote | United States


Only US-based 360 recruiters with a proven track record of winning new business will be considered.


Most remote recruitment roles promise flexibility.

Very few combine that with a genuinely high-performance environment.


This is one of those opportunities.


About the Opportunity

Our client is a specialist tech recruitment firm partnering with venture-backed startups and high-growth companies across the US.

They operate in markets where hiring is urgent, complex, and high impact, particularly across software engineering, cyber, and go-to-market functions.

You’ll be working with companies such as OpenAI, Coinbase, Reddit and Citadel, alongside a broader network of VC-backed startups.


The business has scaled rapidly, doubling in size last year, and continues to grow in a strong, candidate-led market.


Why This Role Appeals to Top Recruiters

  • Fully remote setup with the freedom to work from anywhere in the US
  • A collaborative team culture, not an isolated remote environment
  • Regular meet-ups, trips, and off-sites across cities like New York, San Francisco, London, and beyond
  • Heavy investment in client and candidate engagement through dinners, events, and conferences
  • A team that includes multiple $1M+ billers who actively share knowledge and best practice
  • No KPI-heavy culture, performance is measured on output and results


This is a business built for recruiters who want to operate like adults, with trust, autonomy, and the tools to perform at a high level.


The Role

You will build and own a market within software engineering recruitment, focusing on high-growth technology businesses.

This is a full 360 role where success is driven by your ability to:

  • Win and develop new client relationships
  • Position yourself in a competitive, fast-moving market
  • Deliver high-quality outcomes across the full recruitment lifecycle


What They’re Looking For

  • Proven 360 recruiters with strong business development capability
  • Consistent billing history, typically $300k+
  • Experience in tech, startups, or high-growth environments
  • Individuals who are self-sufficient, commercially aware, and driven
  • People who value collaboration but don’t rely on hand-holding


Why Now

The business has already experienced significant growth and is now focused on scaling intelligently.

They are hiring to strengthen key verticals, particularly software engineering, and to support existing team members who are currently operating solo in their niche.


The market is active, the client base is strong, and the timing is intentional.


If you are looking for a remote role that still gives you access to top clients, strong earnings potential, and a genuinely high-performing team, this is worth exploring.

The RoleYou will build and own a market within software engineering recruitment, focusing on high-growth technology businesses.This is a full 360 role where success is driven by your ability to:Win and develop new client relationshipsPosition yourself in a competitive, fast-moving marketDeliver high-quality outcomes across the full recruitment lifecycle
What They’re Looking ForProven 360 recruiters with strong business development capabilityConsistent billing history, typically $300k+Experience in tech, startups, or high-growth environmentsIndividuals who are self-sufficient, commercially aware, and drivenPeople who value collaboration but don’t rely on hand-holding
Why NowThe business has already experienced significant growth and is now focused on scaling intelligently.They are hiring to strengthen key verticals, particularly software engineering, and to support existing team members who are currently operating solo in their niche.
The market is active, the client base is strong, and the timing is intentional.
If you are looking for a remote role that still gives you access to top clients, strong earnings potential, and a genuinely high-performing team, this is worth exploring.

title: Case Manager ($17/hr)
descriptionHtml:
• Provide enrollment assistance and guidance to Medicaid-eligible recipients and their families regarding the HIPP / Premium Assistance program via phone or correspondence.

• Verify, document, and investigate existing health coverage for Medicaid recipients and their dependents (employer plans, COBRA, etc.).

• Assist in identifying members who may qualify for HIPP / Premium Assistance.

• Obtain and verify documents required for eligibility: insurance policies, employment verification, premium invoices, etc.

• Maintain active case files during open enrollment cycles, premium review, and payment processing.

• Process, review, and follow up on premium payments / reimbursements, ensuring timely and accurate payments, and tracking any discrepancies.

• Perform ongoing case maintenance and audits, updating records for status changes, renewals, terminations, or escalations.

• Complete periodic reporting (e.g. case metrics, enrollment trends, audit reports) as required by state or internal stakeholders.

• Respond to high volumes of inbound and outbound calls and inquiries about eligibility, coverage, program rules, and status.

• Conduct advanced document review, legal research or case escalation as needed, particularly on more complex cases.

• Prioritize case events, manage deadlines, escalate issues to supervisors or subject matter experts when appropriate.


Substantial customer service experience, ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment, basic knowledge of health insurance and health insurance terminology, Excel Microsoft knowledge

title: People & Culture Business Partner
descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Title:  People & Culture Business Partner

Location:  Remote – US

Reports to: Director, People & Culture Business Partner

Vacancy: This is an existing vacancy

The Role:   

The People & Culture Business Partner (P&CBP) is a strategic partner to senior leaders, supporting multiple P&L business units to achieve business objectives through effective people strategies. This role brings strong business acumen, trusted advisory capability, and a deep understanding of how talent, performance, and organizational design enable sustainable results.

Partnering across North America and collaborating with global People & Culture colleagues, the P&CBP translates business strategy into integrated people plans while supporting leaders through growth, change, and transformation.

What You’ll Be Doing in This Role: 

  • Partner with senior and executive leaders across multiple P&Ls to align people strategies with business goals, financial outcomes, and operating priorities.
  • Act as a trusted advisor, providing pragmatic counsel on leadership, talent, organizational effectiveness, and employee experience.
  • Translate business strategy into people cycle plans leveraging workforce planning, talent, performance, and capability levers.
  • Coach leaders on effective performance management, including goal setting, feedback, development conversations, and performance improvement.
  • Support consistent application of performance frameworks to drive accountability, engagement, and productivity.
  • Lead and support talent assessments for critical roles and skills, including talent identification, readiness assessment, and development planning.
  • Partner with leaders to align workforce plans, structure, and skills with current and future business needs.
  • Use data, trends, and business insight to inform talent recommendations and measure the impact of policies and people initiatives.
  • Provide strategic and hands‑on support for organizational design, role clarity, and restructuring initiatives.
  • Support change management by advising leaders on impacts, stakeholder communication, and engagement and adoption.
  • Assess organizational effectiveness and recommend improvements to structure, governance, and ways of working.
  • Manage and resolve complex employee relations issues, conducting objective and compliant investigations as required and partnering with Legal to mitigate risks.
  • Collaborate closely with specialist P&C teams (Talent Acquisition, L&D, Total Rewards, Employee Experience) to deliver solutions and support to the business.
  • Identify capability and development needs and partner on learning initiatives.
  • Provide policy guidance, including compensation, promotions, transfers, and role changes.
  • Contribute to broader P&C projects and support people workstream for M&A activity.

Required

Qualifications of this Role:

  • 8+ years of progressive HR experience, including at least 5 years as a People & Culture (HR) Business Partner supporting P&L business units.
  • Proven experience partnering with senior and executive leaders in complex, matrixed environments.
  • Demonstrated expertise in performance management, succession planning, workforce planning, and organizational design.
  • Experience supporting transformation initiatives with the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong business and financial acumen.
  • Excellent coaching, communication, and influencing skills.
  • Strong project management and initiative ownership capability.
  • Solid knowledge of employment legislation and regulatory requirements (e.g., ESA, FLSA).
  • Proficiency with O365 and Workday or comparable HRIS.
  • University degree in HR, Business, or equivalent experience.

Preferred

  • Master’s degree in HR, OD, or related field.
  • CHRL, SHRM CP/SCP, or equivalent designation.
  • Exposure to European employment legislation and practices is considered an asset.
  • Experience supporting technology, analytics, engineering, utilities, or similarly complex environments.

This position has a starting salary range of $130,000.00 – $140,000.00 USD per year. This is the range we reasonably and in good faith expect to pay for the role at the time of posting. An employee’s pay within the range is determined by a number of factors, including relevant skills, education, qualifications, experience, performance, business or organizational needs, and geographic location.

Our Values

We POWER Our Customer’s Success

We are Innovative, Collaborative and Grounded and in Data

We Make Things Easy

We Get It Done

We Start with Trust & Prove it Everyday

    

JD Power is committed to employing a diverse workforce. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.

Should you require accommodations during the recruitment and selection process, please reach out to tarecruitment@jdpa.com.

JD Power does not disclose your personal data to unauthorized third parties. However, as a global corporation consisting of multiple affiliated companies in various countries, JD Power has international sites and JD Power uses resources located throughout the world. JD Power may from time to time also use third parties to act on JD Power’s behalf. You agree to the fact that to the extent necessary your personal data may be transferred and/or disclosed to any company within JD Power group of companies as well as to third parties acting on JD Power’s behalf, including also transfers to servers and databases outside the country where you provided JD Power with your personal data. Such transfers may include for example transfers and/or disclosures outside the European Economic Area and in the United States of America. If you are a California or United Kingdom resident, additional disclosures about the information we collect and how we use that information can be found by clicking here.  

To all recruitment agencies: JD Power does not accept unsolicited agency resumes and we are not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Our mission in the market we serve is clear. To power every auto-related decision through proprietary data, advanced analytics, deep industry expertise, and seamless workflows that connect insight to action.

title: Executive Administrative Assistant
descriptionHtml:
Founded in 2003, ebs Recruiters is built on a foundation of family values and a passion for finding great opportunities for amazing individuals. With over 3,000 talented professionals successfully placed, we are dedicated to making dreams a reality. Networking is at the core of what we do, connecting candidates with their ideal roles and providing top-notch services to our clients.

Today, our leadership team boasts over 100 years of collective experience in technical recruitment. Their extensive expertise brings a unique blend of business acumen and interpersonal skills to every interaction. When you partner with ebs Recruiters, you can rely on us to offer continuity, expertise, and a dedicated contact for all your recruitment needs. We recruit a perfect fit, every time.


Executive Administrative Assistant

Position Overview: The Executive Administrative Assistant will provide high-level administrative and operational support to a senior executive, ensuring seamless day-to-day operations. This role requires strong organizational skills, discretion, and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. You will act as a key partner, supporting both daily execution and broader initiatives in a fast-paced setting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage a complex and evolving calendar, including scheduling across multiple time zones
  • Serve as a point of contact between executive leadership and internal/external stakeholders
  • Prepare meeting materials, coordinate logistics, and ensure timely communication
  • Organize agendas, presentations, and documentation for meetings
  • Track action items and follow up on deliverables
  • Manage high-volume email correspondence
  • Coordinate travel arrangements and detailed itineraries
  • Draft and edit communications on behalf of leadership
  • Support ongoing projects by tracking timelines and deliverables
  • Handle sensitive and confidential information with professionalism
  • Provide additional administrative support as needed

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree preferred
  • 5+ years of experience supporting senior executives in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong organizational and time-management skills with attention to detail
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and common business tools
  • Ability to work independently, anticipate needs, and take initiative
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to interact with senior stakeholders

At ebs Recruiters, we focus our vast experience in accounting, finance and human resources on one single mission – to find "the perfect fit"​ for you.

ebs recruits for Fortune 500 corporations, public accounting firms and small businesses in St. Louis and beyond. Our clients count on our superior service, our uncompromising ethics and our extensive network of accounting, finance, human resources and supply chain professionals.

We conduct customized searches at all levels, from entry level to executive, both permanent and temporary. Our approach is solution-oriented. We listen to our clients, clarify their needs and go the extra mile to ensure the right match. Every candidate and every search is an investment, and we never settle for less than perfect.

You can count on ebs for a perfect fit, every time.

title: Recruiter - Talent Acquisition Specialist
descriptionHtml:
Primary Responsibilities

 

Sourcing & Selection

• Build and develop effective referral sources with FRs/staff, develop productive centers of influence (COIs), and develop qualified lead strategies from a variety of sources (i.e. referrals, COIs, advertising, career fairs, social media, etc.)

• Actively participate in community clubs and organizations to promote and brand Northwestern Mutual

• Coordinate advertising and marketing strategies to include ads, direct mail, community events, etc.

• Develop relationships with other Northwestern Mutual recruiters to share leads and best practices

• Specific selection process accountability to include but not limited to screening candidates, conducting initial interviews, administering selection tools and guiding candidates through the selection process

• Review resumes and schedule qualified initial interviews

• Effectively communicate with candidates

 

Accountability, Tracking, and Analysis

• Report weekly recruiting activity to leadership team

• Develop proficiency and use of home office-supported software to maintain candidate databases and accurate records of prospects to ensure efficiency of the selection process

• Assist in the coordination of leadership team meetings and the preparation of activity/results reports

• Analyze recruiting ratios to identify inefficiencies in selection process; establish and implement improvements

• May include the oversight of contract and licensing responsibilities

 

Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree required

• Three to five years professional work experience, preferably in sales, business development, recruiting or the financial services industry required

• Demonstrated ability to work professionally as a team member and with all levels of people

• Experience with prospecting; generating leads via phone or face-to-face interactions preferred

• Strong organizational and interpersonal skills

• Strong personal network and/or community involvement to leverage from a recruiting perspective

• High-energy and passion along with ability to meet quotas

title: Customer Success Manager
descriptionHtml:
About Auror

At Auror, we're empowering the retail industry to tackle theft and Organised Retail Crime, a $150 Billion problem globally. It's high volume crime that's increasingly organised in nature and is putting people, retailers, and communities at risk every day.

Founded in New Zealand 12 years ago, we're working with some of the best and largest retailers in the world across the US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and the UK.

Auror is connecting people and intelligence to reduce crime. We're using technology for good.

In partnership with our leading retail partners, we need people with the passion, determination, and innovation required to overcome one of the world's largest problems. If you're looking to make a difference with and for the people dedicated to stopping crime, for good, then we want you on our team.

We're also embracing the potential of AI to supercharge our impact—whether that's enhancing the way we detect trends, support our customers, or improve internal workflows. As a company, we're committed to responsibly incorporating AI into how we work and what we build, and we encourage all Aurors to be curious about how AI can elevate their work, regardless of role or function.

The Role

As a Customer Success Manager (IC3), you will own the delivery of Customer pilots, and new + existing Customer implementations, ensuring enterprise customers reach value quickly and confidently. This is a true CS role with a focus on the early customer journey: building relationships, aligning on outcomes, and ensuring customers see measurable impact quickly.

You will operate with autonomy on defined work, guiding customers through onboarding, activation, and proof of value while collaborating closely with Sales, Customer Success Engagement, and Product.

This role is hands-on and outcome-driven. You'll connect customer goals to practical use cases, translate data into measurable outcomes, and support pilot conversion through strong execution and stakeholder engagement. Success is measured by adoption, time-to-value, and pilot outcomes — driven by strong relationships and commercial acumen, not just execution.

Practically this will involve:

  • Own the end-to-end customer experience across pilots and onboarding, ensuring customers reach value within agreed timeframes
  • Build trusted relationships with customer stakeholders across all levels, setting clear expectations and maintaining strong engagement throughout
  • Build and drive pilot and onboarding plans anchored in customer goals, operating realities, and success metrics.
  • Manage onboarding projects end-to-end, including scope, milestones, dependencies, risks, and timelines
  • Guide customers toward early, high-impact use cases and help them embed new ways of working with confidence
  • Translate platform data into clear, measurable outcomes such as shrink reduction, operational improvements, and community safety impact
  • Contribute meaningfully to pilot business cases by articulating ROI and value tied to customer use cases
  • Lead structured engagements across multiple customer stakeholders, keeping momentum, managing risks, and unblocking progress
  • Deliver customer training, onboarding sessions, and weekly performance updates covering adoption, engagement, insights, and next steps
  • Work closely with Sales, CS Engagement, and Product teams to unblock progress, support conversion, and ensure clean handoffs with comprehensive documentation, with health status and risks identified
  • Manage multiple pilots concurrently in a fast-paced environment, including customer travel at least monthly as needed
  • In smaller markets or during transitional phases as the activation model beds in, you may temporarily support a limited Customer Engagement portfolio by agreement. This is not a core expectation of the role and will be actively reviewed to ensure activation delivery remains the priority

This role reports to Ruby Arden, VP Global Customer Success

Requirements

About you

You're a relationship-first CS professional who thrives in the early stages of the customer journey. You bring commercial awareness, customer empathy, and the organisational discipline to manage complex engagements without losing sight of the bigger picture.

  • You have experience in enterprise SaaS Customer Success, with exposure to pilots, onboarding, or new customer journeys
  • You build trust quickly with customer stakeholders and adapt your communication to different roles and seniority levels
  • You operate independently, balancing urgency with quality — managing timelines, risks, and dependencies while keeping the customer experience front of mind
  • You communicate confidently with customers, explaining product value, outcomes, and ROI in clear, practical terms
  • You coach customers and users to embed new ways of working
  • You identify blockers to adoption and address them early
  • You translate data into insights that help customers make decisions and adopt the platform effectively
  • You are organised, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple workstreams at once
  • You are proactive, growth-minded, and comfortable travelling monthly or as needed to support customers
  • Fluency in English and Spanish a plus!

We are looking for people who demonstrate a strong alignment to our Guiding Principles (you can find these on our Careers page).

Auror is committed to providing an inclusive and accessible application process to all candidates and we are actively working to improve diversity within the tech industry. We celebrate diversity and inclusiveness at Auror, regardless of (but not limited to) race, gender, sexual orientation, family status, religion, ethnicity, national origin, physical disability, veteran status, or age.

If you need adjustments to any part of our recruitment process because of accessibility reasons, please do not hesitate to let us know during the application process. We will work with you to identify adjustments that will allow you to perform at your best.

Benefits

  • Competitive salary range: Depending on level of experience, between $85,000 - $105,000 (IC3)
  • Employee share scheme: You'll own part of a company making a real difference!
  • Flexibility: We are hard-working and outcome focused, but recognise there is more to life than work. We promote a healthy work/life blend
  • Shorter work weeks (at full pay): Everyone gets Friday afternoons off, so you can start your weekend early, and do more of whatever it is that makes you happy
  • Focus on mental and physical health: We understand how vital our health is and have policies to support your wellness, including Wellness Days, and up to $500 USD for expert sessions every year
  • Health care plan (Medical, Dental & Vision): Auror covers 100% of the cost of your individual health insurance plan with Anthem & Metlife.
  • Family-friendly: We offer comprehensive paid parental leave - 12 weeks for birth parents and 6 weeks for non-birth parents following birth, adoption, or surrogacy, available to all Aurors from day one
  • Personal growth: We support our team to participate in courses, conferences, or events that will help them develop their skills
  • Team love: We have regular team lunches and social events where most (if not all) activities are during work hours

Next steps:

If you're excited about our mission and you have the experience and a passion for this role, please hit "Apply".

If you're not sure that you tick all the boxes but feel you're close to what we're looking for, please apply anyway! We're proud that Auror is a place where everyone can learn and grow so we'd love to hear from you.

You'll be asked to submit a cover letter as part of your application. While this is optional we do encourage it, as we find cover letters can tell a story that resumes alone often cannot. Our hiring team love to understand what attracted you to this role and why you are excited about the opportunity to join Auror.

Once you apply, you'll hear from us to acknowledge your application. If you have questions about any of the above, or if you have any accessibility requirements, we'll be able to help you from there.
descriptionText:
About Auror

At Auror, we're empowering the retail industry to tackle theft and Organised Retail Crime, a $150 Billion problem globally. It's high volume crime that's increasingly organised in nature and is putting people, retailers, and communities at risk every day.

Founded in New Zealand 12 years ago, we're working with some of the best and largest retailers in the world across the US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and the UK.

Auror is connecting people and intelligence to reduce crime. We're using technology for good.

In partnership with our leading retail partners, we need people with the passion, determination, and innovation required to overcome one of the world's largest problems. If you're looking to make a difference with and for the people dedicated to stopping crime, for good, then we want you on our team.

We're also embracing the potential of AI to supercharge our impact—whether that's enhancing the way we detect trends, support our customers, or improve internal workflows. As a company, we're committed to responsibly incorporating AI into how we work and what we build, and we encourage all Aurors to be curious about how AI can elevate their work, regardless of role or function.

The Role 

As a Customer Success Manager (IC3), you will own the delivery of Customer pilots, and new + existing Customer implementations, ensuring enterprise customers reach value quickly and confidently. This is a true CS role with a focus on the early customer journey: building relationships, aligning on outcomes, and ensuring customers see measurable impact quickly.

You will operate with autonomy on defined work, guiding customers through onboarding, activation, and proof of value while collaborating closely with Sales, Customer Success Engagement, and Product.

This role is hands-on and outcome-driven. You'll connect customer goals to practical use cases, translate data into measurable outcomes, and support pilot conversion through strong execution and stakeholder engagement. Success is measured by adoption, time-to-value, and pilot outcomes — driven by strong relationships and commercial acumen, not just execution.

Practically this will involve:

Own the end-to-end customer experience across pilots and onboarding, ensuring customers reach value within agreed timeframesBuild trusted relationships with customer stakeholders across all levels, setting clear expectations and maintaining strong engagement throughoutBuild and drive pilot and onboarding plans anchored in customer goals, operating realities, and success metrics. Manage onboarding projects end-to-end, including scope, milestones, dependencies, risks, and timelinesGuide customers toward early, high-impact use cases and help them embed new ways of working with confidenceTranslate platform data into clear, measurable outcomes such as shrink reduction, operational improvements, and community safety impactContribute meaningfully to pilot business cases by articulating ROI and value tied to customer use casesLead structured engagements across multiple customer stakeholders, keeping momentum, managing risks, and unblocking progressDeliver customer training, onboarding sessions, and weekly performance updates covering adoption, engagement, insights, and next stepsWork closely with Sales, CS Engagement, and Product teams to unblock progress, support conversion, and ensure clean handoffs with comprehensive documentation, with health status and risks identifiedManage multiple pilots concurrently in a fast-paced environment, including customer travel at least monthly as neededIn smaller markets or during transitional phases as the activation model beds in, you may temporarily support a limited Customer Engagement portfolio by agreement. This is not a core expectation of the role and will be actively reviewed to ensure activation delivery remains the priority

This role reports to Ruby Arden, VP Global Customer Success

Requirements

About you 

You're a relationship-first CS professional who thrives in the early stages of the customer journey. You bring commercial awareness, customer empathy, and the organisational discipline to manage complex engagements without losing sight of the bigger picture.

You have experience in enterprise SaaS Customer Success, with exposure to pilots, onboarding, or new customer journeysYou build trust quickly with customer stakeholders and adapt your communication to different roles and seniority levelsYou operate independently, balancing urgency with quality — managing timelines, risks, and dependencies while keeping the customer experience front of mindYou communicate confidently with customers, explaining product value, outcomes, and ROI in clear, practical termsYou coach customers and users to embed new ways of workingYou identify blockers to adoption and address them earlyYou translate data into insights that help customers make decisions and adopt the platform effectivelyYou are organised, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple workstreams at onceYou are proactive, growth-minded, and comfortable travelling monthly or as needed to support customersFluency in English and Spanish a plus!

We are looking for people who demonstrate a strong alignment to our Guiding Principles (you can find these on our Careers page).

Auror is committed to providing an inclusive and accessible application process to all candidates and we are actively working to improve diversity within the tech industry. We celebrate diversity and inclusiveness at Auror, regardless of (but not limited to) race, gender, sexual orientation, family status, religion, ethnicity, national origin, physical disability, veteran status, or age.

If you need adjustments to any part of our recruitment process because of accessibility reasons, please do not hesitate to let us know during the application process. We will work with you to identify adjustments that will allow you to perform at your best.

Benefits

Competitive salary range: Depending on level of experience, between $85,000 - $105,000 (IC3) Employee share scheme: You'll own part of a company making a real difference!Flexibility: We are hard-working and outcome focused, but recognise there is more to life than work. We promote a healthy work/life blendShorter work weeks (at full pay): Everyone gets Friday afternoons off, so you can start your weekend early, and do more of whatever it is that makes you happyFocus on mental and physical health: We understand how vital our health is and have policies to support your wellness, including Wellness Days, and up to $500 USD for expert sessions every yearHealth care plan (Medical, Dental & Vision): Auror covers 100% of the cost of your individual health insurance plan with Anthem & Metlife. Family-friendly: We offer comprehensive paid parental leave - 12 weeks for birth parents and 6 weeks for non-birth parents following birth, adoption, or surrogacy, available to all Aurors from day onePersonal growth: We support our team to participate in courses, conferences, or events that will help them develop their skillsTeam love: We have regular team lunches and social events where most (if not all) activities are during work hours

Next steps:

If you're excited about our mission and you have the experience and a passion for this role, please hit "Apply".

If you're not sure that you tick all the boxes but feel you're close to what we're looking for, please apply anyway! We're proud that Auror is a place where everyone can learn and grow so we'd love to hear from you.

title: Sales Operations Coordinator
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Halfday was founded in 2021 to reimagine the functional beverage category. Inspired by our co-founder’s personal battle with digestive illnesses, we set out to make digestive health more approachable by bringing prebiotic fiber to iced tea. We believe functional doesn’t have to mean clinical; it can be delicious, craveable, and part of everyday life! Built on grit, strong partnerships, and long-term thinking, Halfday is scaling with a focus on disciplined execution and durable growth.


Job Summary

The Sales Operations & Logistics Coordinator plays a key role in supporting the sales team while ensuring the smooth coordination of order fulfillment, inventory management, and logistics processes. This position bridges the gap between Supply Chain, Sales, 3PLs, and distribution functions to ensure timely delivery, accurate order processing, and a high level of customer satisfaction.


Sales Operations Support

  • Process sales orders accurately and efficiently in internal systems
  • Coordinate with the sales team to track order status, pricing, and customer requirements
  • Assist with contract management and pricing updates
  • Manage EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions via SPS Commerce, including order intake, acknowledgments, and ASN (Advance Ship Notice) creation and submission
  • Oversee ASN accuracy and compliance, ensuring alignment with shipment details and retailer requirements
  • Track and report on key performance indicators (KPIs), including OTIF (On-Time In-Full), identifying trends and areas for improvement
  • Troubleshoot EDI errors and discrepancies, working directly with SPS Commerce and external EDI partners to resolve transmission, mapping, ASN validation, and data integrity issues


Logistics & Order Fulfillment

  • Coordinate shipment scheduling, tracking, and delivery with logistics partners
  • Ensure timely and cost-effective transportation of goods
  • Monitor inventory levels and collaborate with warehouse teams to prevent stockouts or overstocking
  • Assign and allocate inventory to purchase orders to ensure traceability and accurate order fulfillment
  • Support initiatives to improve OTIF performance through proactive planning, issue resolution, and cross-functional coordination
  • Handle returns, exchanges, and order discrepancies
  • Maintain documentation related to shipping, customs (if applicable), and compliance


Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Act as a liaison between sales, 3PLs, supply chain, and customer service teams
  • Communicate proactively regarding delays, issues, or changes in order status
  • Support process improvements to streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve service level KPIs


Customer Support

  • Respond to customer inquiries related to order status, delivery timelines, and product availability
  • Ensure a high level of customer satisfaction through clear and timely communication


Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or a related field (or equivalent experience)
  • 2+ years of experience in sales operations, logistics, EDI coordination, or supply chain roles
  • Hands-on experience with SPS Commerce or similar EDI platforms
  • Working knowledge of EDI processes, transaction sets (e.g., 850, 855, 810, 856/ASN), and data mapping concepts
  • Experience managing and troubleshooting EDI transactions, including ASN processing and communication with SPS Commerce and external trading partners
  • Experience tracking and analyzing KPIs such as OTIF (On-Time In-Full) and driving performance improvements
  • Proficiency in CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite)
  • Strong organizational and multitasking skills
  • Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis
  • Fluency in Google Suite/Google Sheets, Slack.
  • Willingness to use phone calls collaborating with brokers and agencies 


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in a fast-paced or high-growth environment
  • Knowledge of shipping regulations and international logistics (if applicable)
  • Familiarity with inventory management systems and traceability practices (lot, batch, or serial tracking)


Key Competencies

  • Attention to detail and accuracy
  • Time management and prioritization
  • Cross-functional collaboration
  • Adaptability and problem-solving
  • Customer-focused mindset


Working Conditions

  • Remote
  • Occasional coordination with warehouse or distribution centers
  • May require handling time-sensitive tasks outside standard hours

companySlogan: The prebiotic iced tea.
companyDescription:
When Co-founder Kayvon Jahanbakhsh was diagnosed with ulcerative colitis when he was a freshman in college he lost over 80 pounds, and he was forced to dramatically change his diet to focus on his gut health. 

It’s that moment when Kayvon realized how difficult it was to find products that would help him on this journey that he could enjoy. That’s where the idea for Halfday prebiotic iced tea was born - with the purpose to make digestive wellness more delicious, fun and accessible. 

As a first generation Iranian-American, Kayvon’s family served tea with breakfast, lunch and dinner each day. At school he was introduced to sugary bottles of iced tea that were readily available in cafeterias. 

Kayvon enlisted the help of classmate & best-friend Mike Lombardo to develop an iced tea that tasted nostalgic, but without all of the junk. Each can of Halfday has prebiotic fiber, with only 3-5g of sugar. 

Kayvon & Mike researched tirelessly the highest quality fibers & ingredients possible, knowing this needed to be something Kayvon could enjoy every single day.

title: Marketing Assistant, Performance
descriptionHtml:
We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Marketing Assistant to support the performance marketing team. This person must be proactive, solution-oriented, and extremely attentive to details. This is a junior or entry-level role ideal for someone excited about ecommerce or performance marketing.

Reporting to the Senior Director of Marketing, this role will support the execution of campaigns across channels like email, SMS, app, paid media, partnerships, and more. The Marketing Assistant will help with content creation, campaign setup, reporting, and coordination, gaining hands-on experience in performance marketing and data analysis within a fast-paced ecommerce environment.

Schedule: This is a hybrid role based in our Los Angeles, CA office, with in-office days on Tuesday through Thursday.

What You’ll Do

  • Support the marketing team with QA needs, market research, and administrative tasks
  • Assist in filling out campaign and creative briefs, collaborating with the design team to ensure all necessary information is clearly outlined
  • Help gather assets, product information, and messaging inputs needed for campaign execution
  • Pull and organize performance reports for various channels and KPIs
  • Build emails using templates and design systems; draft and edit basic marketing copy for emails, SMS, and push notifications
  • Help refresh and update content across channels including automated customer journeys to reflect new collections, promotions, and seasonal messaging
  • Maintain organized documentation related to campaigns, promotions, and automated journeys
  • Support ongoing updates to calendars, trackers, and workflow tools
  • Assist with ad hoc performance marketing projects as needed

What You’ll Need

  • 0–2 years of experience in marketing, ecommerce, or a related field (internships included)
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Comfort/proficiency in Excel including conditional formatting and basic formulas
  • Exceptional written skills with perfect grammar and spelling, and an interest in copywriting and brand storytelling
  • Comfortable working in a data-driven, fast-paced environment

Nice to Have - Bonus Points!

  • Familiarity with email or SMS marketing tools (e.g., Klaviyo, Attentive, or similar) is a plus but not required
  • Advanced Excel skills, like pivot tables and complex formulas
  • Eagerness to learn lifecycle marketing, retention strategies, and ecommerce best practices
  • Interest in fashion, women’s apparel, creative, or ecommerce marketing
  • Collaborative mindset and positive attitude

We’re ready to meet you! Come help us achieve our ambitious goals!

At Lulus, we believe our people are our greatest asset and we seek to structure competitive compensation offers to ensure that we can attract and retain the best talent. Our job postings include an annual base salary range for the role. The stated base salary range represents our good faith estimate as to what candidates are likely to expect, and we tailor our offers within the range based on several factors, including the selected candidate's educational and professional experience, industry knowledge, location, technical and communication skills, and other factors that may prove relevant during the selection process. Base salary is a part of a total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include an annual bonus and other benefit programs.

companyDescription: Headquartered in California and serving millions of customers worldwide, Lulus is an attainable luxury fashion brand for women, offering modern, unapologetically feminine designs at accessible prices for all of life’s fashionable moments. Our aim is to make every woman feel beautiful, celebrated and as if she’s the most special version of herself for every occasion – from work desk to dream date or cozied up on the couch to the spotlight of her wedding day. Founded in 1996, Lulus delivers fresh styles to consumers daily, using direct consumer feedback and insights to refine product offerings and elevate the customer experience. Follow @lulus on Instagram and @lulus on TikTok. Lulus is a registered trademark of Lulu’s Fashion Lounge, LLC. All rights reserved.

title: VP of Finance
descriptionHtml:
Silva-Lining HR is seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented VP of Finance﻿ for one of our clients to lead and manage all aspects of their financial operations, including accounting, financial reporting, internal controls, budgeting, cash management, and compliance. As a key member of the team, this role will also contribute to financial strategy and data-informed decision-making. At a more strategic level, the VP of Finance builds on these responsibilities by shaping the company’s overall financial direction, leading financial planning and capital strategy, and partnering closely with executive leadership to drive long-term growth and cross-functional business decisions.

﻿Key Responsibilities

Financial Management & Reporting

  • Oversee the integrity of financial reporting, ensuring alignment with GAAP and executive-level decision-making needs.
  • Provide high-level financial insights, KPIs, and dashboards to executive leadership and stakeholders.
  • Establish and enforce internal controls, governance frameworks, and financial policies at an organizational level.
  • Lead relationships with external auditors, tax advisors, and regulatory bodies.

Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Strategy

  • Own the company-wide financial planning strategy, including long-term forecasting and multi-year financial models.
  • Lead budgeting and forecasting processes, including rolling forecasts and variance analysis
  • Drive scenario planning, capital allocation, and investment strategy to support business growth.
  • Advise executive leadership on financial risks, opportunities, and business performance.

Operational & Strategic Leadership

  • Evaluate and guide pricing strategies, margin performance, and service line profitability to support market expansion and overall business growth.
  • Lead financial due diligence, structuring, and integration for mergers, acquisitions, or major investments, supported by financial modeling for capital investment and cost management decisions.
  • Drive organization-wide financial systems strategy, including scalability, automation, and data infrastructure.

Cash Management, Capital Strategy & Risk Oversight

  • Oversee cash flow strategy, capital structure, and funding requirements (debt, equity, or reinvestment).
  • Develop enterprise-level risk management strategies, including financial, operational, and market risks.
  • Ensure appropriate insurance coverage and risk mitigation frameworks across the business.

Executive Leadership & Team Development

  • Build and lead a high-performing finance function, including accounting, FP&A, and other finance roles as the company scales, through coaching, training, and continuous improvement.
  • Develop leadership within the finance team, ensuring succession planning and capability building.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with department heads to align financial and operational goals.

Soft Skills

  • Strategic and forward-thinking leader with strong business acumen.
  • High attention to detail with a strong sense of integrity, accountability, and ownership.
  • Effective communicator with the ability to influence at all levels.
  • Strong decision-making and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

﻿Compensation & Benefits

Salary: Competitive base salary + performance-based bonuses.

Insurance: Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.

Perks: Paid time off (PTO), company holidays, and professional development support.

This is a full-time, onsite employment opportunity in Hollywood, Florida.

Requirements

﻿Qualifications

Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
  • CPA or CMA designation preferred.
  • 7+ years of progressive experience in accounting/finance, with at least 2 years in a senior finance or equivalent role.
  • Strong understanding of GAAP, financial strategy and planning, financial systems, accounting operations, and business performance management.

Preferred

  • Proficiency with ERP/accounting systems
  • Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills.
  • Demonstrated ability to partner with technical teams (e.g., IT, operations, engineering) to improve financial reporting, project costing, and system automation
  • Experience leading or supporting financial system upgrades, integrations, or automation projects (e.g., integrating accounting software with CRM or PSA tools).
descriptionText:
Silva-Lining HR is seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented VP of Finance﻿ for one of our clients to lead and manage all aspects of their financial operations, including accounting, financial reporting, internal controls, budgeting, cash management, and compliance. As a key member of the team, this role will also contribute to financial strategy and data-informed decision-making. At a more strategic level, the VP of Finance builds on these responsibilities by shaping the company’s overall financial direction, leading financial planning and capital strategy, and partnering closely with executive leadership to drive long-term growth and cross-functional business decisions.

﻿Key Responsibilities

Financial Management & Reporting

Oversee the integrity of financial reporting, ensuring alignment with GAAP and executive-level decision-making needs.Provide high-level financial insights, KPIs, and dashboards to executive leadership and stakeholders.Establish and enforce internal controls, governance frameworks, and financial policies at an organizational level.Lead relationships with external auditors, tax advisors, and regulatory bodies.

Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Strategy

Own the company-wide financial planning strategy, including long-term forecasting and multi-year financial models.Lead budgeting and forecasting processes, including rolling forecasts and variance analysisDrive scenario planning, capital allocation, and investment strategy to support business growth.Advise executive leadership on financial risks, opportunities, and business performance.

Operational & Strategic Leadership

Evaluate and guide pricing strategies, margin performance, and service line profitability to support market expansion and overall business growth.Lead financial due diligence, structuring, and integration for mergers, acquisitions, or major investments, supported by financial modeling for capital investment and cost management decisions.Drive organization-wide financial systems strategy, including scalability, automation, and data infrastructure.

Cash Management, Capital Strategy & Risk Oversight

Oversee cash flow strategy, capital structure, and funding requirements (debt, equity, or reinvestment).Develop enterprise-level risk management strategies, including financial, operational, and market risks.Ensure appropriate insurance coverage and risk mitigation frameworks across the business.

Executive Leadership & Team Development

Build and lead a high-performing finance function, including accounting, FP&A, and other finance roles as the company scales, through coaching, training, and continuous improvement.Develop leadership within the finance team, ensuring succession planning and capability building.Collaborate cross-functionally with department heads to align financial and operational goals.

Soft Skills

Strategic and forward-thinking leader with strong business acumen.High attention to detail with a strong sense of integrity, accountability, and ownership.Effective communicator with the ability to influence at all levels.Strong decision-making and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

﻿Compensation & Benefits

Salary: Competitive base salary + performance-based bonuses.

Insurance: Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.

Perks: Paid time off (PTO), company holidays, and professional development support.

This is a full-time, onsite employment opportunity in Hollywood, Florida.

Requirements

﻿Qualifications

Required

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.CPA or CMA designation preferred.7+ years of progressive experience in accounting/finance, with at least 2 years in a senior finance or equivalent role.Strong understanding of GAAP, financial strategy and planning, financial systems, accounting operations, and business performance management.

Preferred

Proficiency with ERP/accounting systemsAdvanced Excel and financial modeling skills.Demonstrated ability to partner with technical teams (e.g., IT, operations, engineering) to improve financial reporting, project costing, and system automationExperience leading or supporting financial system upgrades, integrations, or automation projects (e.g., integrating accounting software with CRM or PSA tools).

title: DATA ANALYST (contract)
descriptionHtml:
Data Analyst (Contract)

Please note that this is a contract role providing services to Microsoft through external staffing partners of Allegis Global Solutions. If you are selected for this role, you will be employed by AGS and will not be an employee of Microsoft.

Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytically driven professional to join a global IT support organization focused on delivering end-user services to internal employees. In this role, you will play a critical part in ensuring data quality across dashboards and reports that empower business and service delivery teams to make informed, data-driven decisions. You will support a centralized reporting platform built on modern cloud and analytics technologies, integrating data from enterprise sources to enable operational performance tracking and continuous improvement. This position offers the opportunity to contribute to both traditional reporting and cutting-edge AI and machine learning initiatives that drive meaningful improvements in service outcomes.

Job Responsibilities

  • Maintain and update documentation for the centralized reporting platform
  • Design, build, and enhance reports and dashboards using single or multiple data sources
  • Develop pipelines and datasets to ingest, store, and visualize data from new enterprise sources
  • Build AI/ML solutions to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies that improve operational insights, including support for problem management
  • Perform data validation, testing, and user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure data accuracy and consistency
  • Investigate and determine root causes of data discrepancies and document defects with appropriate engineering and support teams
  • Improve report and dashboard usability and interactivity based on end-user feedback
  • Deliver recurring training sessions to help stakeholders understand and effectively use platform metrics
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to support new reporting solutions and enhancements

Requirements

  • 2 to 4 years of applicable experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field
  • Demonstrated experience building and maintaining dashboards and reports for operational or business use cases
  • Prior experience working within IT service management or help desk environments is a plus
  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, computer engineering, or a related technical field required
  • Strong experience with Power BI, including data visualization, data modeling, and DAX
  • Working knowledge of SQL and Kusto Query Language (KQL)
  • Experience using Azure Machine Learning to build low-code and no-code solutions
  • Hands-on experience with ServiceNow data and IT Service Management (ITSM) modules
  • Familiarity with Azure and Microsoft Fabric for data integration and analytics workflows
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality
  • Solid interpersonal and communication skills
  • Willingness to learn and adapt to evolving technologies and business needs

Additional Details

  • Location: Remote
  • Duration: 2 months
  • Pay Range*: $36 - $40.50 per hour
  • Weekly Schedule: 40 hours
  • Job Status: Non-Exempt
  • Application Deadline: Apply within 72 hours of the posting date to ensure consideration.

This Role Is Eligible For The Following Benefits

  • Medical, dental & vision
  • Hospital plans
  • 401(k) Retirement Plan – Pre-tax and Roth post-tax contributions available
  • Life Insurance (Company paid Basic Life and AD&D as well as voluntary Life & AD&D for the employee and dependents)
  • Company paid short and long-term disability
  • Health & Dependent Care Spending Accounts (HSA & DCFSA)
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Time Off/Leave(PTO, Allegis Group Paid Family Leave, Parental Leave

Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to specific elections, plan, or program terms.

AGS is an Equal Opportunity Employer and will consider all applications without regard to race, sex, age, color, religion, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information or any characteristic protected by law.

If you require a reasonable accommodation related to the application or interview process due to a disability, please email accommodation@allegisglobalsolutions.com. This inbox is monitored solely for accommodation requests. For questions about open roles or to apply, please submit your application through the job posting, as this inbox is not monitored by recruiters and applications sent here will not be reviewed.

In accordance with the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986, employment is contingent upon verification of identity and authorization to work in the United States. All persons hired will be required to complete Form I-9 and provide acceptable documentation as required by law.

﻿Please note that we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position. These tools may analyze application materials and assist our team in identifying candidates whose qualifications best match the requirements of the role. If you have questions about our use of AI in the hiring process, or would like more information, please contact us.

  • We reserve the right to pay above or below the posted wage based on factors unrelated to protected classifications.

Individual compensation offered for this position within this range will depend on many factors, including qualifications, skills, relevant experience, job knowledge, geographic location, internal equity, and other pertinent job-related factors.

San Francisco Candidates only: Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

City of Los Angeles Candidates only: We will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the City of Los Angeles’ Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring.

Every company has a mission. What's ours? To empower every person and every organization to achieve more. title: Retail Graphic Designer
descriptionHtml:
Overview

We are seeking a Graphic Designer to support all retailer accounts with high-impact, on-brand creative across secondary displays, display updates, and retail advertising. This role is responsible for bringing retail moments to life—translating brand storytelling into compelling in-store and retailer-specific creative that drives visibility, conversion, and shelf presence.

The ideal candidate understands both brand design and retail execution, with the ability to work within retailer guidelines while elevating creative impact.


Key ResponsibilitiesSecondary Displays & Visual Merchandising
  • Design secondary displays, endcaps, PDQs, clip strips, and seasonal merchandising units across all retail accounts
  • Update and refresh existing display graphics for new launches, resets, and promotional moments
  • Prepare print-ready mechanical files according to retailer and vendor specifications
  • Partner with external display vendors to ensure production accuracy and quality control


Retail Advertising & Account-Specific Creative
  • Design retail ads for digital, in-store, and co-op marketing placements
  • Adapt brand campaigns to retailer-specific formats and guidelines
  • Collaborate with Marketing and Retail teams to ensure messaging aligns with account strategies and launch priorities
  • Support retailer.com needs including banners, hero images, promotional assets, and content updates


Cross-Functional Collaboration
  • Work closely with Retail Sales to understand account priorities and timelines
  • Partner with Marketing to ensure brand consistency across all retail executions
  • Coordinate with Product Development to ensure new launches are visually supported on time
  • Manage multiple retailer timelines simultaneously and communicate status updates clearly


Production & Asset Management
  • Prepare final mechanicals and oversee print file preparation
  • Maintain organized asset libraries by retailer
  • Ensure all designs meet retailer compliance standards and specifications
  • Conduct quality checks on final proofs before production


Qualifications
  • 2–4 years of graphic design experience, preferably in beauty, CPG, or retail marketing
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating in-store displays, retail ads, and merchandising design
  • Expert in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign)
  • Strong understanding of print production and file setup
  • Detail-oriented with strong project management skills
  • Ability to balance creativity with retailer constraints
  • Experience working with major retailers (Ulta, Target, Walmart, Sephora, etc.) is a strong plus
  • Experience working with project management tools 
  • Photo retouching and color correcting images


Please note we are only able to hire in the following states: CA, TX, FL, WA, NV, WY, NH, PA

title: Videographer / Editor
location: Land O' Lakes, Flórida, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
About Paper Jacket

Paper Jacket is a media production company that helps businesses inspire, educate, and entertain with clear, well-crafted video content. We focus on producing work that is thoughtful, polished, and purposeful from start to finish.


We’re a small, collaborative team that cares about quality and doing work that holds up. Everyone here plays a role in shaping the final product, and we approach each project with a strong sense of ownership and attention to detail.


The Role

This role is designed for someone who wants to grow within the production process while taking on meaningful responsibility from the start. You’ll support projects from prep through delivery, helping ensure shoots are organized, efficient, and aligned with the creative vision. On set, you’ll play an active role in capturing content, supporting direction, and learning how to lead productions over time.


The day-to-day is hands-on and varied. You’ll be working with gear, managing footage, assisting in post-production, and stepping in wherever needed to keep projects moving forward. As you gain experience, you’ll take on more ownership in shaping shoots and guiding decisions. It’s a great fit for someone who is dependable, eager to learn, and interested in developing both technical skills and creative leadership within a collaborative team.


What You’ll Do
  • Prepare productions by organizing logistics, equipment, and assets based on direction from the Producer or Director
  • Support and occasionally lead on-set production, ensuring shoots stay on schedule and aligned with project goals
  • Communicate clearly with clients throughout the production process, both on set and off
  • Capture high-quality video and audio using a range of production equipment (cameras, phones, lighting, audio, etc.)
  • Manage footage and project files, maintaining organized and reliable workflows
  • Assist with editing, motion graphics, and post-production tasks, including revisions and final polish
  • Review and proof all deliverables to ensure quality and accuracy before client delivery
  • Coordinate production needs such as travel, scheduling, and on-set logistics
  • Maintain and organize production equipment and vehicles, ensuring readiness for all shoots
  • Collaborate with team members to problem-solve and adapt when plans change
  • Contribute creative ideas and support the development of each project from concept through completion
  • Participate in ongoing learning, team collaboration, and internal projects to improve skills and workflows


Who You Are
  • You have 2+ years of professional, on-the-job experience (agency or in-house creative environment preferred).
  • You’re fluent in Adobe Premiere (AE is a plus) and comfortable adopting new tools.
  • You have strong visual instincts paired with an understanding of process and client standards.
  • You’re a self-starter who values both independence and collaboration.
  • You’re detail-oriented, organized, and committed to delivering high-quality work consistently.
  • You know what to expect – there will be regular travel resulting in long days and/or overnight stays (we work to make it comfortable and fair).
  • You have no interest in ego battles. You simply love doing great work with good people.
  • REQUIRED: You can lift and move equipment up to 50 lbs, sometimes up and down stairs, to and from projects.
  • REQUIRED: You have a valid driver’s license and a clean driving record.


What Success Looks Like
  • You follow the process, interpret instructions accurately, and communicate proactively.
  • You maintain the quality of work across every deliverable.
  • You deliver on time and take initiative to improve both work and workflow.
  • You contribute new ideas that advance creative outcomes.
  • You help elevate the team around you through collaboration, consistency, and care.


Perks & Benefits
  • Salary: $45,000 (+ up to $5,000/yr in benefits)
  • Health/dental: generous employer allowance toward your costs (up to $3,600/yr)
  • Vacation: earn 1 week of vacation per year, up to 5 total weeks off
