companyDescription:
Paper Jacket is a media content creation agency that specializes in commercial video production, photography, and podcasts (Video. Photo. Podcasts.).

Unlike other production companies who will simply point-and-shoot, Paper Jacket understands the demands and flow of current marketing and proactively provides assets that keep media hungry websites, social feeds, and channels fed.

title: Assistant Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
The Assistant Project Manager (APM) supports the successful execution of electrical construction projects by assisting in planning, coordination, and day-to-day project management activities. This role works closely with Project Managers, field leadership, vendors, and internal teams to help ensure projects are completed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with company standards. The APM plays a key role in managing documentation, tracking project performance, supporting procurement and financial processes, and facilitating communication across all phases of the project lifecycle.

Essential Functions, Duties, And Responsibilities

Tasks may include, but are not limited to the following:

  • Assist the PM in managing day-to-day project operations and administrative tasks
  • Support the preparation, review, and tracking of RFIs, submittals, change orders, and project logs
  • Help organize weekly project schedules, manpower plans, material deliveries, and long-lead tracking
  • Attend project meetings (internal and external) and prepare meeting notes or action items
  • Maintain accurate project documentation and ensure timely distribution of updates to stakeholders
  • Conduct periodic site walks with the PM or Superintendent to observe progress and verify installation compliance
  • Assist with documenting field conditions, tracking percent of completion, and reporting potential delays
  • Coordinate with field leadership to ensure alignment on project goals, safety standards, and execution plans
  • Assist in monitoring labor hours, equipment usage, and material costs
  • Review purchase orders, delivery tickets, and subcontractor invoices for accuracy
  • Help prepare weekly project summaries and cost-to-complete updates for leadership
  • Support communication between clients, general contractors, engineers, and Enterprise Electrical teams
  • Maintain professionalism when interacting with external partners while representing Enterprise Electrical values
  • Reinforce Enterprise Electrical's Safety Standards during all site visits
  • Ensure project activities align with NEC, local codes, and company quality requirements
  • Assist in project closeout tasks including punch list documentation, as-builts coordination, and turnover packages

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

  • Basic understanding of electrical construction methods, materials, and project workflows
  • Ability to read and interpret blueprints, specifications, and construction documents
  • Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Effective communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Strong attention to detail and problem-solving capabilities
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook); experience with construction/project management software

Preferred

  • Ability to work collaboratively with field teams, project managers, and stakeholders
  • Working knowledge of safety practices and jobsite requirements
  • Bilingual (English/Spanish) communication skills plus

Supervisory Responsibilities

  • None directly; may assist in coordinating field personnel, subcontractors, and vendors as directed by the Project Manager

Work Environment/Physical Demands/Position Type and Expected Work Time

  • Combination of office and field work environment. Work is performed primarily on active construction sites with ongoing

electrical and general construction activities

  • Regular exposure to outdoor weather conditions, including heat, cold, wind, rain, and humidity
  • Exposure to typical construction site conditions such as noise, dust, vibration, and moving equipment
  • May work near energized equipment, elevated work areas, excavations, and confined or restricted spaces in compliance

with OSHA safety procedures

  • Occasional exposure to fumes, airborne particles, and other environmental conditions common to construction sites
  • Work may require occasional travel between multiple project sites

Physical Requirements:

  • Frequently walk active construction sites, including uneven surfaces and unfinished structures
  • Occasionally climb stairs, ladders, or temporary access systems in compliance with OSHA requirements
  • Ability to stand, bend, kneel, crouch, or reach as necessary to observe work activities and inspect conditions
  • Ability to communicate effectively with employees and subcontractors in person and over the phone or radio
  • Occasionally lift, carry, or move materials or equipment weighing up to 25 pounds, with or without assistance
  • Ability to wear required personal protective equipment (PPE), including hard hat, safety glasses, gloves, high-visibility

clothing, and fall protection equipment when required

Position Type:

  • Full-time position
  • Eligible for standard company benefits

Typical Schedule:

  • Monday through Friday standard hours vary based on job site and location
  • Actual hours may vary based on business needs

Shift Details:

  • Primarily a day-shift role
  • Occasional adjustments to start/end times may be required
  • Could include evenings and/or weekends based on workload or deadlines

Overtime Expectations:

  • None

Travel

  • Travel between sites and main office

Required Education And Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Construction Management, Engineering, Business or a related field preferred
  • Equivalent electrical or construction field experience may substitute for formal education

Certifications/Licenses:

  • Valid driver's license with the ability to travel to project sites
  • OSHA 10 or OSHA 30 Certification
  • First Aid / CPR / AED preferred

Experience Requirements:

  • 1-3 years of experience in construction project coordination or management (electrical construction preferred)
  • Experience working on commercial, industrial, or mission-critical projects is a plus
  • Experience supporting multiple projects or working in a fast-paced environment

Equipment or Machines Routinely Used in This Position

  • Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) -hard hat, safety glasses, gloves, high-visibility vest, fall protection equipment
  • Mobile devices (smartphone, tablet) for inspections, reporting, and communication
  • Laptop or desktop computer for documentation, reports, and safety tracking
  • Hand tools and basic measuring devices used for inspections (tape measure, flashlight, voltage tester)
  • Two-way radios for jobsite communication
  • Access equipment such as ladders, lifts, and scaffolding (for observation and inspection purposes

Enterprise Electricals's Core Values & Purpose

To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following Enterprise Electrical core values:

  • Safety First, Safety Always (Safety)
  • Committed to Excellence (Greatness)
  • Plan it, Do it, Own it (Accountability)
  • Learn it, Know it, Teach it (Mentorship)
  • One Team, One Goal (Teamwork)
  • Positive Attitude Required (Positivity)

Our Core Purpose: Building Relationships to Enrich Lives
descriptionText:
The Assistant Project Manager (APM) supports the successful execution of electrical construction projects by assisting in planning, coordination, and day-to-day project management activities. This role works closely with Project Managers, field leadership, vendors, and internal teams to help ensure projects are completed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with company standards. The APM plays a key role in managing documentation, tracking project performance, supporting procurement and financial processes, and facilitating communication across all phases of the project lifecycle.

Essential Functions, Duties, And Responsibilities

Tasks may include, but are not limited to the following:

 Assist the PM in managing day-to-day project operations and administrative tasks Support the preparation, review, and tracking of RFIs, submittals, change orders, and project logs Help organize weekly project schedules, manpower plans, material deliveries, and long-lead tracking Attend project meetings (internal and external) and prepare meeting notes or action items Maintain accurate project documentation and ensure timely distribution of updates to stakeholders Conduct periodic site walks with the PM or Superintendent to observe progress and verify installation compliance Assist with documenting field conditions, tracking percent of completion, and reporting potential delays Coordinate with field leadership to ensure alignment on project goals, safety standards, and execution plans Assist in monitoring labor hours, equipment usage, and material costs Review purchase orders, delivery tickets, and subcontractor invoices for accuracy Help prepare weekly project summaries and cost-to-complete updates for leadership Support communication between clients, general contractors, engineers, and Enterprise Electrical teams Maintain professionalism when interacting with external partners while representing Enterprise Electrical values Reinforce Enterprise Electrical's Safety Standards during all site visits Ensure project activities align with NEC, local codes, and company quality requirements Assist in project closeout tasks including punch list documentation, as-builts coordination, and turnover packages

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

 Basic understanding of electrical construction methods, materials, and project workflows Ability to read and interpret blueprints, specifications, and construction documents Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities Effective communication skills, both written and verbal Strong attention to detail and problem-solving capabilities Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook); experience with construction/project management software

Preferred

 Ability to work collaboratively with field teams, project managers, and stakeholders Working knowledge of safety practices and jobsite requirements Bilingual (English/Spanish) communication skills plus

Supervisory Responsibilities

 None directly; may assist in coordinating field personnel, subcontractors, and vendors as directed by the Project Manager

Work Environment/Physical Demands/Position Type and Expected Work Time

 Combination of office and field work environment. Work is performed primarily on active construction sites with ongoing

electrical and general construction activities

 Regular exposure to outdoor weather conditions, including heat, cold, wind, rain, and humidity Exposure to typical construction site conditions such as noise, dust, vibration, and moving equipment May work near energized equipment, elevated work areas, excavations, and confined or restricted spaces in compliance

with OSHA safety procedures

 Occasional exposure to fumes, airborne particles, and other environmental conditions common to construction sites Work may require occasional travel between multiple project sites

Physical Requirements:

 Frequently walk active construction sites, including uneven surfaces and unfinished structures Occasionally climb stairs, ladders, or temporary access systems in compliance with OSHA requirements Ability to stand, bend, kneel, crouch, or reach as necessary to observe work activities and inspect conditions Ability to communicate effectively with employees and subcontractors in person and over the phone or radio Occasionally lift, carry, or move materials or equipment weighing up to 25 pounds, with or without assistance Ability to wear required personal protective equipment (PPE), including hard hat, safety glasses, gloves, high-visibility

clothing, and fall protection equipment when required

Position Type:

 Full-time position Eligible for standard company benefits

Typical Schedule:

 Monday through Friday standard hours vary based on job site and location Actual hours may vary based on business needs

Shift Details:

 Primarily a day-shift role Occasional adjustments to start/end times may be required Could include evenings and/or weekends based on workload or deadlines

Overtime Expectations:

 None

Travel 

 Travel between sites and main office

Required Education And Experience

 Bachelor's degree in Construction Management, Engineering, Business or a related field preferred Equivalent electrical or construction field experience may substitute for formal education

Certifications/Licenses:

 Valid driver's license with the ability to travel to project sites OSHA 10 or OSHA 30 Certification First Aid / CPR / AED preferred

Experience Requirements:

 1-3 years of experience in construction project coordination or management (electrical construction preferred) Experience working on commercial, industrial, or mission-critical projects is a plus Experience supporting multiple projects or working in a fast-paced environment

Equipment or Machines Routinely Used in This Position

 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) -hard hat, safety glasses, gloves, high-visibility vest, fall protection equipment Mobile devices (smartphone, tablet) for inspections, reporting, and communication Laptop or desktop computer for documentation, reports, and safety tracking Hand tools and basic measuring devices used for inspections (tape measure, flashlight, voltage tester) Two-way radios for jobsite communication Access equipment such as ladders, lifts, and scaffolding (for observation and inspection purposes

Enterprise Electricals's Core Values & Purpose

To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following Enterprise Electrical core values:

 Safety First, Safety Always (Safety) Committed to Excellence (Greatness) Plan it, Do it, Own it (Accountability) Learn it, Know it, Teach it (Mentorship) One Team, One Goal (Teamwork) Positive Attitude Required (Positivity)

 Our Core Purpose: Building Relationships to Enrich Lives
companySlogan: Building Relationships to Enrich Lives

title: Packaging Graphic Designer
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Packaging Graphic Designer

About Phantom Brands

Phantom Brands is a forward-thinking consumer products company focused on delivering innovative, high-quality products with standout visual identities. We pride ourselves on blending creativity with strategic execution to create packaging that performs at retail and resonates with customers.

Position Overview

We are seeking an experienced Packaging Graphic Designer with a strong background in retail-focused design. This role requires a deep understanding of packaging systems, dielines, and production processes, along with the ability to translate brand strategy into compelling, shelf-ready designs. The ideal candidate has a proven track record of creating packaging that not only looks great but also meets retailer requirements and manufacturing constraints. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented designer who has hands-on experience across branding, packaging, social media content and web design, and is excited to work with both traditional design tools and AI-powered workflows.


Key Responsibilities

  • Design and execute packaging concepts from ideation through final production
  • Develop innovative, on-brand packaging that stands out in competitive retail environments
  • Work directly with dielines, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and print readiness
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, product development, and operations teams
  • Prepare and deliver production-ready files, ensuring compliance with vendor and printer specifications
  • Adapt designs based on retailer requirements, compliance standards, and feedback
  • Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting tight deadlines
  • Maintain consistency across product lines and brand touchpoints


Qualifications

  • Minimum 5–7 years of experience specifically in packaging graphic design (consumer products preferred)
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating packaging work across physical products and retail environments
  • Expert knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign)
  • Deep understanding of dielines, print production, materials, and packaging formats
  • Familiarity with retailer requirements (e.g., big-box, e-commerce packaging standards)
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Ability to balance creative vision with technical constraints
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience working with international vendors and printers
  • Knowledge of packaging compliance, labeling regulations, and sustainability considerations
  • Experience in fast-paced, high-growth consumer brands
  • Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop and Adobe Illustrator
  • Proficiency in Shopify
  • Packaging design experience (professional or portfolio-based)
  • Website design experience (UI/visual design experience required)
  • Experience creating social-first content for platforms like Instagram and TikTok
  • Experience using AI tools in a design or creative workflow
  • Strong eye for design, layout, color, and typography
  • Excellent attention to detail and visual precision
  • Ability to take direction, apply feedback, and iterate efficiently


Nice to Have

  • Experience with additional Adobe tools (InDesign, After Effects, etc.)
  • Familiarity with marketing or e-commerce brands
  • Understanding of responsive web design or UX fundamentals


What Else You’ll Do

  • Design and support graphics for physical products
  • Create and update website designs and digital assets
  • Develop visual content for digital and print marketing materials
  • Collaborate closely with senior designers, sales teams, and the marketing team in-house
  • Utilize AI tools to assist with ideation, design exploration, and production
  • Maintain brand consistency across all projects
  • Manage multiple design tasks while meeting deadlines and maintaining a high level of detail


Why Join Phantom Brands

  • Opportunity to shape packaging for innovative, high-impact products
  • Room for growth and creative ownership
  • Collaborative in-house creative environment
  • Opportunity to grow under experienced designers
  • Hands-on involvement in real product launches and brand development
  • Exposure to modern design tools and AI-enhanced workflows


To apply:

Please submit your resume and portfolio demonstrating packaging and website design work here.

And/OR send us your resume and portfolio to --- email: alicia@phantomchef.com

title: Chief Executive Advisor
descriptionHtml:
Position Title: Chief Executive Advisor


Reports to: CEO / Board of Directors

Position Summary

The Chief Executive Advisor serves as a trusted strategic partner to the Chief Executive Officer and senior leadership team. This role provides high-level counsel on corporate strategy, organizational development, major business decisions, and external stakeholder engagement. The Advisor leverages deep industry experience and executive judgment to support sustainable growth and long-term value creation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide strategic advice and independent perspectives to the CEO and Board on critical business initiatives and long-term planning
  • Support the development and execution of corporate strategy, transformation programs, and major investments
  • Act as a sounding board for executive decision-making on complex or high-risk matters
  • Advise on organizational structure, leadership effectiveness, and succession planning
  • Support mergers, acquisitions, partnerships, and other strategic transactions as needed
  • Assist in managing relationships with key stakeholders, including investors, regulators, and strategic partners
  • Represent the company externally when required, enhancing corporate reputation and influence
  • Monitor industry trends, competitive dynamics, and policy developments to inform executive decisions

Qualifications

  • Extensive senior executive experience (CEO, President, Partner, or equivalent)
  • Proven track record in strategic leadership, business growth, or organizational transformation
  • Strong understanding of corporate governance and board-level decision-making
  • Exceptional strategic thinking, judgment, and communication skills
  • High level of integrity, discretion, and professionalism

Preferred Experience

  • Experience advising CEOs or Boards in a complex or multinational environment
  • Background in [industry-specific field, e.g., finance, technology, manufacturing]
  • Experience with M&A, capital markets, or government and regulatory affairs

companySlogan: Courage. Committment. Perseverance. Integrity. These are the core values of the brave men and women who serve at TDCJ.
companyDescription: For more than 36,000 dedicated public servants, our mission is to provide public safety, promote positive change in offender behavior, reintegrate offenders into society and assist victims of crime. The Texas Department of Criminal Justice (TDCJ) is the Lone Star State’s largest state agency and manages more than 146,000 offenders in state prisons, state jails and private correctional facilities that contract with the TDCJ. The agency also provides funding and certain oversight of community supervision and is responsible for the supervision of offenders released from prison on parole or mandatory supervision.

title: Senior Manager of IT
descriptionHtml:
Location: Hybrid- Employees may be required to work out of the nearest office location for quarterly meetings 1-4 times annually. 

Aravo Solutions, Inc., provides leading third-party risk management (TPRM), ESG, and vendor lifecycle management solutions powered by intelligent automation software and designed to meet the needs of TPRM teams at Global 2000 enterprises.

At Aravo, we believe that in an increasingly interconnected world, managing risk is a critical pillar of global resilience. Traditional, manual approaches to TPRM can no longer keep pace with today's scale and speed of disruption. That's why we have pioneered an Intelligence-First™ approach.

Trusted by millions of third-party users in 195+ countries, Aravo provides guidance for the most complex third-party networks in the world, helping them mitigate risk, build business resilience, and protect their reputations. We are fundamentally redesigning how organizations see, understand, and act on risk by embedding AI directly into the core of their workflows.

Joining Aravo means working at the forefront of AI in TPRM and collaborating with a team that views AI as a partner, not a replacement. You will have the opportunity to work alongside industry experts, leverage the latest technologies, and contribute to shaping the future of third-party risk management!

Position Overview:

We are seeking a hands-on, strategic Senior Manager of IT to lead and scale our internal IT function. The ideal candidate will own corporate IT and business systems operations while partnering closely with Engineering, Security, Finance, and HR teams to support a growing, distributed SaaS organization. They will have exceptional communication and problem-solving skills, with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

This leader will balance day-to-day operational excellence with forward-looking improvements, ensuring our systems, tools, and processes scale efficiently as the company grows.

Key Responsibilities:

IT Leadership & Strategy

  • Define and execute Aravo's IT roadmap aligned with company growth and operational needs
  • Lead and mentor a small, high-impact IT team (helpdesk, systems, IT ops)
  • Establish and track SLAs, KPIs, and user satisfaction metrics
  • Rollout tool standardization, ensuring we are continuously consolidating toolsets
  • Own IT budgeting, forecasting, and vendor management

Corporate IT Operations

  • Manage core internal systems, including license management
  • Ensure reliable onboarding/offboarding processes and lifecycle management
  • Maintain high availability and performance of internal systems
  • Support a hybrid/remote workforce across multiple locations with effective endpoint and access strategies

Security & Compliance Partnership

  • Partner with Security and Engineering to support and customer-driven compliance requirements
  • Enforce identity, access control, and endpoint security policies
  • Onboard AI solutions and usage that adhere to proper security and data practices
  • Support audits, evidence collection, and vendor security reviews
  • Drive security awareness and best practices across employees

End-User Experience & Support

  • Own IT support function with a focus on responsiveness, quality and automation
  • Deliver a seamless employee experience across onboarding, support, and tooling
  • Continuously improve documentation, self-service, and internal processes

Vendor, Asset & SaaS Management

  • Manage SaaS application portfolio, licensing, and cost optimization
  • Track and manage IT assets and lifecycle (procurement → retirement)
  • Negotiate vendor contracts and manage third-party relationships

Requirements

Qualifications:

  • 8-12+ years of experience in IT, with 3-5+ years in a leadership role
  • Strong hands-on experience with modern IT stack, including AI tools
  • Automating user onboarding and offboarding with auditability and traceability
  • Experience supporting compliance frameworks such as Microsoft SSPA, SOC1, and SOC 2
  • Support FedRamprelated activities and compliance
  • Proven ability to operate both strategically and hands-on
  • Strong project management and cross-functional collaboration skills
  • Experience in a SaaS or cloud-first company (80-500 employees ideal)
  • Experience scaling IT processes in a high-growth environment

Preferred Qualifications/Skills/Soft Skills: (Optional)

  • Familiarity with zero trust security principles
  • Exposure to customer-facing security or audit requirements
  • Hands-on leadership with a "roll up your sleeves" mindset
  • Self-starter with entrepreneurial attitude towards managing everything IT
  • Strong operational discipline and attention to detail
  • Ability to prioritize effectively in a lean environment
  • Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Customer-first mindset

Benefits

Why Join Us:

At Aravo Solutions, Inc., we offer a dynamic and inclusive work environment where employees are encouraged to innovate, grow, and make a meaningful impact. We provide competitive compensation and benefits packages, opportunities for professional development, and the chance to work alongside industry experts on cutting-edge projects.

Benefits:

  • 100% Employer Paid Medical Insurance options for the Employee and Family
  • Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave
  • Life and AD&D Insurance
  • Long-Term Disability Insurance
  • 401K with Company Matching
  • Equity Participation
  • 4 Weeks of Vacation
  • Fully Stocked Kitchens
  • Company-Sponsored Charitable Day of Giving Events

Aravo Solutions Inc. is registered as an employer in many, but not all, states. If an applicant is not in or able to work from a state where Aravo Solutions Inc. is registered, they may not be eligible for employment. The eligible states include: FL, GA, MA, MO, NC, NH, NV, OR, PA, SC, TN, and TX.

companySlogan: Delivering the market’s smartest risk and resilience solutions, powered by intelligent automation.
companyDescription:
Aravo delivers the market’s smartest supplier, vendor, and third-party risk and resilience solutions, powered by intelligent automation.

For more than 20 years now, Aravo’s combination of award-winning technology, premium services, and unrivaled domain expertise has helped the world’s most respected brands accelerate and optimize their third-party management programs, delivering better business outcomes faster and ensuring the agility to adapt as programs evolve.

With solutions built on technology designed for usability, agility, and scale, and powered by trusted, native AI, even the most complex organizations can keep pace with the high velocity of regulatory change. As a centralized, AI‑enhanced system of record for all data related to third-party risk, Aravo helps organizations achieve a complete view of their third-party ecosystem throughout the lifecycle of the relationship, from intake through off-boarding and all stages in between and across all risk domains.

Aravo is trusted by the world’s leading brands, helping them manage their risk and improve the performance of millions of third-party users, with hundreds of thousands of corporate users across 195 countries. 

Only Aravo is positioned as a Leader across all the following key market evaluations:

2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Third-Party Risk Management Tools for Assurance Leaders; 2025 Chartis RiskTech Quadrant™ for GRC Digital Resilience Solutions; and 2020-2024 Chartis RiskTech Quadrant™ for Third-Party Risk Management

title: Product Designer
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Rent the Runway (RTR) is transforming the way we get dressed by pioneering the world’s first Closet in the Cloud. Founded in 2009, RTR has disrupted the $2.4 trillion fashion industry by inspiring women with a more joyful, sustainable and financially-savvy way to feel their best every day. As the ultimate destination for circular fashion, the brand now offers infinite points of access to its shared closet via a fully customizable subscription to fashion, one-time rental or ownership. RTR offers designer apparel, accessories and home decor from 700+ brand partners and has built in-house proprietary technology and a one-of-a-kind reverse logistics operation. Under CEO and Co-Founder Jennifer Hyman’s leadership, RTR has been named to CNBC’s “Disruptor 50” five times in ten years, and has been placed on Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies list multiple times, while Hyman herself has been named to the “TIME 100” most influential people in the world and as one of People magazine’s “Women Changing the World.”

About The Job

We’re looking for a smart and curious Product Designer, with a solid foundation across the fundamentals of UX, UI, interaction design, and research—paired with a proactive curiosity for leveraging AI to enhance both the user experience and design efficiency. As part of our pod model, our Product Designers own the end-to-end design process for specific features and product areas within their dedicated squad, advocating for the customer through discovery, design execution, and user testing. You’ll work closely with your squad to deliver high-quality solutions across our desktop and mobile websites, our iOS App

What You’ll Do

  • Own the end-to-end design process for specific features or small product areas within a cross-functional squad, delivering user-centric solutions with moderate autonomy.
  • Deliver all stages of the user experience, from supporting product discovery and participating in vision-setting to delivering high-quality wireframes and prototypes.
  • Conduct usability tests and competitive analysis to deeply understand user goals, using those insights to iterate on designs and drive improvements for your features.
  • Design functional and inspirational customer experiences that prioritize user needs while meeting defined business demands and milestones.
  • Speak clearly to tradeoffs and the “Why” behind design decisions in terms non-designers can understand and participate in
  • Utilize our existing design system and collaborate with the Design System designer to ensure a consistent, intuitive user experience across your feature set
  • Proactively participate in design critiques, providing constructive feedback to peers and supporting a culture of inclusive collaboration.

About You

  • 3+ years of experience working in a mobile consumer-focused business/product
  • Strong understanding of experience principles and customer needs such as motivations and behaviors, with the ability to translate them into engaging and actionable deliverables
  • Ability to create and present strategic experiences that ladder up into business goals and opportunities
  • Excellent verbal and written skills to be able to communicate to both technical and non-technical savvy audience members
  • Ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines under pressure with little management
  • Ability to critique the quality of both UX and visual designs to improve the final product experience creating immersive websites, mobile websites, mobile applications, experiential elements and paid media campaigns
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Strong sense of accountability and ownership of projects
  • Experience presenting to executives and guiding decisions for new enhancements, features or redesigns
  • Experience working in an agile environment
  • Strong working knowledge of Figma (including prototyping) and UserTesting

Benefits

At Rent the Runway, we’re committed to the wellbeing of our employees, and aim to create a workplace that fosters both personal and professional growth. Our inclusive benefits include, but are not limited to:

  • Paid Time Off including vacation, paid bereavement, and family sick leave - every employee needs time to take care of themselves and their family.
  • Universal Paid Parental Leave for both parents + flexible return to work program - because we know your newest family member(s) deserve your undivided attention.
  • Paid Sabbatical after 5 years of continuous service - Unplug, recharge, and have some fun!
  • Exclusive employee subscription and rental discounts - to ensure you experience the magic of renting the runway (and give us valued feedback!).
  • Comprehensive health, vision, dental, FSA and dependent care from day 1 of employment - Your health comes first and we’ve got you covered.
  • 401k match - an investment in your future.
  • Company wide events and outings - our team spirit is no joke - we know how to have fun!
  • Hybrid Work - our corporate employees have the option to work remotely 1 day a week, in accordance with Company policies.

Rent the Runway is an equal opportunity employer. In accordance with applicable law, we prohibit discrimination against any applicant or employee based on any legally-recognized basis, including, but not limited to: race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, childbirth or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, age (40 and over), national origin or ancestry, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, genetic information (including testing and characteristics), veteran status, uniformed service member status or any other status protected by federal, state or local law.

_________

The anticipated base salary for this position is $116K to $140K. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held. This position is also eligible for equity compensation.

By submitting your application below, you agree that you have read and acknowledge Rent the Runway's Candidate Privacy Policy, found here.

title: Project Scheduler
descriptionHtml:
Scheduler – Data Center Construction

Full-time | TX, IA, NE (Relocation or 2-week rotation)


About the Opportunity

One of the top general contractors in the data center space is hiring a Scheduler to support large-scale hyperscale builds across the U.S.

With a strong pipeline of mission-critical projects and long-term client partnerships, this is a chance to join a contractor consistently trusted to deliver some of the most complex data center programs in the market.


The Role

You’ll take ownership of project scheduling across high-value data center builds — working closely with project teams, field leadership, and stakeholders to ensure projects stay on track from preconstruction through delivery.

This is a high-impact role where your work directly influences execution, efficiency, and project success on multi-million (often billion-dollar) builds.


Key Responsibilities

  • Lead all planning and scheduling activities across projects
  • Develop, maintain, and analyze project schedules
  • Coordinate with field teams, subcontractors, and leadership
  • Ensure alignment with construction sequencing and project milestones
  • Support and mentor field personnel on scheduling best practices
  • Identify risks, delays, and opportunities to optimize delivery


What We’re Looking For

  • 4+ years of construction experience (data center or large-scale builds preferred)
  • Strong knowledge of scheduling tools and methodologies
  • Experience working closely with field teams and project leadership
  • Ability to manage complex schedules across fast-paced environments
  • Willingness to relocate or travel on a 2-week rotation (with long weekends)


Why This Role

  • Work on some of the largest data center builds in the U.S.
  • Join a top-tier GC with a proven track record in mission-critical delivery
  • Clear career progression on a rapidly growing pipeline
  • Strong compensation, benefits, and travel support

jobPosterTitle: Associate at OP Renewables - Expert Headhunter Supporting Growth in the Renewable Energy & Infrastructure Space
companySlogan: Search-driven recruitment solutions for the renewable energy sector
companyDescription:
OP Renewables, part of the Oliver Parks Group, is a global search firm focused exclusively on the renewable energy sector.
 
Our consultants work in precisely defined market segments, developing deep expertise in the people, companies, and trends shaping each area we cover.
 
Every organization faces unique hiring challenges. That’s why we begin with a detailed consultation to understand your business context, role requirements, timelines, growth stage, budget, and market dynamics, then recommend the best approach, whether it’s a single service or a tailored combination, including:

1. Retained Search
2. Contingent Search
3. Contract/Interim
4. RAAS (Recruitment as a Service).
 
We take a relationship-driven, consultative approach, tailoring our solutions to meet each client’s unique needs. Whether on a permanent or interim basis, we connect experienced professionals with opportunities at companies ranging from high-growth SMEs to global corporations.
 
From our offices in New York, Dallas, Fort Lauderdale, London, Dublin, Munich, and Newcastle, we provide both local insight and global reach.
 
If you want to explore career opportunities or strengthen your leadership team, speak to one of our consultants who understands your specific market.

title: Data & AI Specialist/ Data Scientist / Remote
descriptionHtml:
Job Title : Data & AI Specialist/ Data Scientist / Remote

Location : Remote

Summary

We are seeking a skilled Data & AI Specialist to design, develop, and deploy data-driven solutions that leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning. This role bridges data engineering, analytics, and AI model development to deliver actionable insights and scalable intelligent systems that support business objectives.

  • Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and infrastructure to support analytics and AI initiatives
  • Develop, train, and deploy machine learning and AI models for predictive and prescriptive use cases
  • Analyze large, complex datasets to extract insights and communicate findings to stakeholders
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities for AI-driven improvements
  • Implement data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model evaluation techniques
  • Monitor model performance and retrain models as needed to ensure accuracy and relevance
  • Ensure data quality, integrity, and governance across systems
  • Stay up to date with emerging trends in AI, machine learning, and data technologies
  • Data Analysis Experience, Quality Analysis Experience, Software and Programming

title: Chief of Staff to the Founder
descriptionHtml:
Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details.


About the Opportunity

The organization is scaling rapidly and has a growing pipeline of opportunities that demand exceptional talent across disciplines. The mission is to bring on individuals, from creative producers to technical experts to entrepreneurial leaders, who can help realize this next chapter of growth.


This is a company of doers. Leaders roll up their sleeves, teams work flat, and everyone contributes to what ships. Titles don't insulate individuals from feedback or basics. Critique is invited, learning happens quickly, and the bar is continuously raised. The best ideas win here, no matter where they come from, because clients trust the organization to deliver the strongest outcomes every time.


Clients' missions, products, and bottom lines are sacred. The organization immerses itself in their world, becoming stewards of their goals and partners in solving big problems. Every product, strategy, or asset created must be both beautiful and functional; practical, usable, and designed for real-world impact.


Humans are the most valuable resource, and growth only happens by hiring people who push the organization forward. Across strategy, engineering, design, data, and operations, the organization seeks out teammates who raise the bar and make everyone better. Always hire up, never down.


The organization partners with organizations of all sizes to explore, design, and implement AI strategies that are secure, scalable, and human-centered. The belief is that AI should amplify human potential, not replace it, and this conviction guides every engagement. From advisory and tooling to implementation and education, the organization meets clients where they are at and helps them integrate AI in ways that align with their mission and values. The goal is to empower teams to work smarter, move faster, and unlock new possibilities through thoughtful, responsible innovation.


And through it all, the organization leads with purpose, love, and adventure. Meaningful work is done with people who care about each other, and the ride is made an adventure worth taking. Because at this organization, who we are and how we work are one and the same.


The Opportunity

The organization is seeking an exceptional Chief of Staff to serve as a force multiplier to the founder. This role will be the engine that translates the founder's vision and initiatives into executable plans, builds the systems that allow the leadership team to operate at their highest level, and ensures nothing critical falls through the cracks as the organization scales rapidly.

Rather than being hired for a predetermined set of tasks, the individual will be brought on to solve complex, evolving challenges. Success in this role requires self-direction, proactive value creation, and the ability to thrive without constant oversight.


Over the coming year, responsibilities will be tailored to both organizational needs and individual strengths, potentially including:

  • Leading AI transformation projects with clients
  • Managing CEOs at client organizations
  • Driving sales initiatives for the organization
  • Partnering with Recruiting to help find and close top-tier talent
  • Managing client project delivery
  • Driving value through data and analytics for clients
  • Leading go-to-market initiatives for clients


This role will own special projects, drive organizational initiatives from concept to completion, and build the infrastructure (GTM motions, resource allocation, operational rhythms) that allows the organization to grow while maximizing its greatest strength – its people. If the candidate thrives on turning ambitious ideas into reality and building the scaffolding that makes a fast-growing startup actually work, this is the role.


Key Responsibilities

Strategic Execution & Founder Partnership

  • Partner with the founder to translate priorities, initiatives, and strategic vision into sequenced plans with clear owners, timelines, and success metrics
  • Own end-to-end execution of founder-level special projects and organizational initiatives, from scoping and planning through delivery and measurement
  • Maintain focus and accountability across the founder's portfolio of priorities; proactively identify conflicts, dependencies, and risks before they become problems
  • Prepare high-quality executive materials (strategy briefs, decision memos, board materials, organizational roadmaps) that drive alignment and enable confident decision-making
  • Serve as a strategic thought partner to the founder, pressure-testing ideas, surfacing options and trade-offs, and ensuring initiatives align with company strategy and capacity


Internal Systems & Operations

  • Build and continuously improve the internal operating systems that allow the organization to scale: hiring infrastructure, resource allocation processes, team capacity planning, and cross-functional coordination mechanisms
  • Design and implement hiring systems that support the organization's growth target without overwhelming the existing team, including interview frameworks, candidate evaluation rubrics, and onboarding processes
  • Own resource allocation and capacity planning across the organization, ensuring the right people are working on the right priorities at the right time, and that no one is stretched unsustainably thin
  • Establish organizational rhythms that create alignment without bureaucracy: leadership meeting cadences, decision-making frameworks, communication protocols, and reporting structures
  • Identify operational bottlenecks and inefficiencies; design and implement solutions that increase organizational velocity while maintaining quality


Cross-Functional Leadership & Coordination

  • Orchestrate complex, cross-functional initiatives that require coordination across leadership and multiple teams
  • Serve as a neutral coordinator and problem-solver when initiatives span multiple departments or require difficult trade-offs between competing priorities
  • Represent the founder's office in key internal discussions, ensuring the founder's priorities and perspective are understood even when they cannot be in every meeting
  • Build strong working relationships across the organization; earn trust as someone who gets things done without ego and acts in the organization's best interest


Communications & Organizational Transparency

  • Ensure crisp internal communication on company strategy, priorities, and decisions—translating founder-level thinking into clear, actionable guidance for the broader team
  • Prepare the founder for key meetings, presentations, and decisions; provide relevant context, background materials, and recommended talking points
  • Maintain visibility on organizational health metrics and key initiatives; proactively surface issues and opportunities to leadership


Who You Are

Experience & Skills

  • 5-7+ years in high-performing environments such as Chief of Staff roles, strategic operations, management consulting, venture capital/private equity, business development, or startup leadership positions
  • AI-native, not AI-curious. Before doing almost anything manually, the individual has already asked themselves whether AI can do it better. Constantly tinkering, building small tools, automating personal workflows, and sharing findings.
  • Demonstrated success in taking ambiguous, complex initiatives and driving them to completion; the person who makes hard things happen
  • Strong systems thinking and process design capability; not just executing tasks, but building the infrastructure that makes future execution easier and more scalable
  • Exceptional project management and coordination skills across multiple concurrent work-streams with competing priorities and timelines
  • High proficiency with modern collaboration tools (Google Workspace, Asana/Notion, Slack) and intermediate to advanced comfort with AI-powered productivity tools
  • Outstanding written and verbal communication; ability to synthesize complex information into clear, actionable briefs for different audiences
  • Bonus: Experience in high-growth startups, venture studios, or AI/tech companies; background in talent operations or organizational design; proven success driving business development in B2B environments; exposure to board-level strategy and operations


Mindset & Traits

  • Mission-driven operator with deep commitment to the organization's values and vision
  • Systems builder, not just task executor, thinking about sustainable processes, not one-off solutions
  • High agency and bias for action, seeing what needs to happen and making it happen without waiting for permission
  • Calm under pressure with excellent judgment about when to escalate vs. solve independently
  • Ego-free collaborator who earns trust through delivery, not title or authority
  • Intellectually curious with strong learning agility, comfortable moving between strategic thinking and tactical execution
  • Comfortable operating in ambiguity, thriving when there's no playbook and creating clarity from complexity


Considerations

  • Education: Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; an advanced degree in a relevant field is a plus
  • Ethics: Commitment to ethical practices and responsible business operations
  • Travel: Occasional (10–25%) for company offsites, key events, and leadership meetings
  • Location: Remote-friendly with preference for candidates in major tech hubs and strong preference for overlap with EST business hours


The hiring organization is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

This role will own special projects, drive organizational initiatives from concept to completion, and build the infrastructure (GTM motions, resource allocation, operational rhythms) that allows the organization to grow while maximizing its greatest strength – its people. If the candidate thrives on turning ambitious ideas into reality and building the scaffolding that makes a fast-growing startup actually work, this is the role.
Key ResponsibilitiesStrategic Execution & Founder PartnershipPartner with the founder to translate priorities, initiatives, and strategic vision into sequenced plans with clear owners, timelines, and success metricsOwn end-to-end execution of founder-level special projects and organizational initiatives, from scoping and planning through delivery and measurementMaintain focus and accountability across the founder's portfolio of priorities; proactively identify conflicts, dependencies, and risks before they become problemsPrepare high-quality executive materials (strategy briefs, decision memos, board materials, organizational roadmaps) that drive alignment and enable confident decision-makingServe as a strategic thought partner to the founder, pressure-testing ideas, surfacing options and trade-offs, and ensuring initiatives align with company strategy and capacity
Internal Systems & OperationsBuild and continuously improve the internal operating systems that allow the organization to scale: hiring infrastructure, resource allocation processes, team capacity planning, and cross-functional coordination mechanismsDesign and implement hiring systems that support the organization's growth target without overwhelming the existing team, including interview frameworks, candidate evaluation rubrics, and onboarding processesOwn resource allocation and capacity planning across the organization, ensuring the right people are working on the right priorities at the right time, and that no one is stretched unsustainably thinEstablish organizational rhythms that create alignment without bureaucracy: leadership meeting cadences, decision-making frameworks, communication protocols, and reporting structuresIdentify operational bottlenecks and inefficiencies; design and implement solutions that increase organizational velocity while maintaining quality
Cross-Functional Leadership & CoordinationOrchestrate complex, cross-functional initiatives that require coordination across leadership and multiple teamsServe as a neutral coordinator and problem-solver when initiatives span multiple departments or require difficult trade-offs between competing prioritiesRepresent the founder's office in key internal discussions, ensuring the founder's priorities and perspective are understood even when they cannot be in every meetingBuild strong working relationships across the organization; earn trust as someone who gets things done without ego and acts in the organization's best interest
Communications & Organizational TransparencyEnsure crisp internal communication on company strategy, priorities, and decisions—translating founder-level thinking into clear, actionable guidance for the broader teamPrepare the founder for key meetings, presentations, and decisions; provide relevant context, background materials, and recommended talking pointsMaintain visibility on organizational health metrics and key initiatives; proactively surface issues and opportunities to leadership
Who You AreExperience & Skills5-7+ years in high-performing environments such as Chief of Staff roles, strategic operations, management consulting, venture capital/private equity, business development, or startup leadership positionsAI-native, not AI-curious. Before doing almost anything manually, the individual has already asked themselves whether AI can do it better. Constantly tinkering, building small tools, automating personal workflows, and sharing findings.Demonstrated success in taking ambiguous, complex initiatives and driving them to completion; the person who makes hard things happenStrong systems thinking and process design capability; not just executing tasks, but building the infrastructure that makes future execution easier and more scalableExceptional project management and coordination skills across multiple concurrent work-streams with competing priorities and timelinesHigh proficiency with modern collaboration tools (Google Workspace, Asana/Notion, Slack) and intermediate to advanced comfort with AI-powered productivity toolsOutstanding written and verbal communication; ability to synthesize complex information into clear, actionable briefs for different audiencesBonus: Experience in high-growth startups, venture studios, or AI/tech companies; background in talent operations or organizational design; proven success driving business development in B2B environments; exposure to board-level strategy and operations
Mindset & TraitsMission-driven operator with deep commitment to the organization's values and visionSystems builder, not just task executor, thinking about sustainable processes, not one-off solutionsHigh agency and bias for action, seeing what needs to happen and making it happen without waiting for permissionCalm under pressure with excellent judgment about when to escalate vs. solve independentlyEgo-free collaborator who earns trust through delivery, not title or authorityIntellectually curious with strong learning agility, comfortable moving between strategic thinking and tactical executionComfortable operating in ambiguity, thriving when there's no playbook and creating clarity from complexity
ConsiderationsEducation: Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; an advanced degree in a relevant field is a plusEthics: Commitment to ethical practices and responsible business operationsTravel: Occasional (10–25%) for company offsites, key events, and leadership meetingsLocation: Remote-friendly with preference for candidates in major tech hubs and strong preference for overlap with EST business hours
The hiring organization is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

title: Investment Banking Analyst (Class of 2026)
descriptionHtml:
Title: Investment Banking Analyst (Class of 2026)

Start Date: Summer of 2026

Locations: Dallas, TX

STEPHENS INVESTMENT BANKING ANALYST PROGRAM

The Stephens Investment Banking Analyst Program is one-of-a-kind in the financial services industry. Our two-year, generalist program begins with four weeks of intensive training taught by Stephens bankers who have gone through the process themselves, and evolves into continuous on-the-job experience. Analysts assist junior and senior bankers in advising the firm’s clients across multiple products and industries, while also receiving exposure to private equity firms and family offices.

Our program offers analysts the best opportunity to learn and prepare for a rewarding career in finance, while making significant contributions to our team. Analysts work on deal structure, modeling, presentations, client communications and more.

About Stephens

In operation since 1933, we are among a small group of the nation’s largest family-owned diversified financial services firms, privately held by the Stephens family. This distinction allows us to prioritize doing what is right for our clients and concentrate on a long-term strategy. In addition to investment banking services, Stephens provides wealth and investment management, fixed income and equities trading and sales, insurance and risk management, public finance and private capital. We have a national presence with strategic locations across the U.S. and in Europe.

Our Investment Banking team has a superb track record of adding value for small and middle market companies, as well as global organizations, across all major sectors and with deep expertise on the subsector level. Our investment banking professionals have a collaborative approach to addressing the M&A and capital needs of our clients and regularly coordinate with transatlantic colleagues on active and prospective projects. These include some of the most successful public and private companies in business today.

OUR CULTURE

We draw from a team of expert bankers with extensive knowledge in the areas they serve. The consistency of the advice that we provide stems from the tenure of this highly experienced team. On average, our senior bankers have more than a decade of service at Stephens. This reflects the supportive and stable atmosphere at Stephens, which exists alongside a rigorous commitment to achieving excellent client service and maintaining a reputation for focusing on relationships over transactions. We think like owners, on behalf of our clients.

Senior bankers at Stephens take an active and authentic interest in cultivating positive experiences for analysts during the program. Numerous former analysts have become senior team members in Investment Banking and other lines of business at the firm. Whether our Investment Banking analysts stay at Stephens or move on to other opportunities, we know firsthand the unique value of this mutually beneficial program.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Analysts have a broad array of responsibilities that are executed alongside our veteran bankers, as well as the senior management teams of our clients. These tasks include:

  • Develop detailed financial analysis for various types of transactions
  • Compose presentation materials including: tailored pitch books, Confidential Information Memorandums and other marketing materials
  • Conducting quantitative and qualitative industry research and analysis
  • Perform other operational and administrative tasks as needed

The two year program puts motivated Analysts on track for an Associate role at our firm.

EDUCATION

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related field preferred

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

  • Strong interpersonal skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, effective team player and a commitment to excellence
  • Analytical, critical thinking and problem solving skills with the ability to determine the appropriate action
  • Self-starter with the ability to manage time effectively, prioritize work to balance multiple projects and meet deadlines with minimal supervision
  • Attention to detail with emphasis on accuracy and quality
  • Outstanding record of academic achievement
  • Demonstrated interest in accounting and finance
  • Math and analytical aptitude must be exceptional
  • High level of personal integrity and work ethic
  • Ability to maintain confidentiality
  • Advanced computer skills in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Access
  • Ability to accurately read, interpret and communicate large volumes of numbers and data

CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS

The following registrations will be required within 6 months of employment:

  • SIE – Securities Industry Essentials
  • Series 7 – General Securities Representative
  • Series 63 – Uniform State Law Exam
  • Series 79 – Investment Banking Representative Exam

stephens.com/investment-banking

  • Little Rock
  • Atlanta
  • Boston
  • Chicago
  • Dallas
  • Frankfurt
  • London
  • New York
  • Washington, DC

For benefits information, please visit: https://www.stephens.com/careers/benefits

companySlogan: Stephens is a privately held financial services firm focused on building long-term relationships and long-term results.
companyDescription:
Founded in 1933, Stephens is a family of privately-held, independent financial services firms focused on building value for companies, state and local governments, institutions and high-net-worth investors. 

We are headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas, with offices in leading cities across the country and overseas. Since our founding, Stephens has pursued an independent course. We’ve built our firm on long-term relationships and enduring values, establishing an international reputation for vision, integrity and innovation. Free from herd mentality, short-term thinking and quarter-to-quarter imperatives, we’ve always stayed focused on the people who matter most: our clients. As investors and business owners ourselves, we have a unique perspective on the world. We understand the needs and concerns of individual investors, industry leaders and public interest stewards. Because we sit on the same side of the table as our clients, we are able to understand their goals and help build their future in partnership. 

© 2026 Stephens Inc. | Member NYSE, SIPC

title: Software Engineer
descriptionHtml:
About Hilo Aviation

Hilo Aviation is building the AI-powered operating system for flight schools. Our platform touches student safety, regulatory compliance, and the daily operations of flight training programs across the country. We’re a small, serious technical team solving problems where “good enough” isn’t acceptable — and we’re growing deliberately.



The Role

We’re hiring a Software Engineer to build and own features across our full-stack platform. You’ll work primarily in Next.js, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL, taking ownership of feature development from design through deployment. This is a high-trust, high-output role on a team that moves fast and cares deeply about what it ships.

We’re not looking for someone to fill tickets. We’re looking for an engineer who takes initiative, thinks about the downstream effects of what they build, and brings enough technical ownership to push back when a design or approach needs reconsidering.

We want the best engineer we can find at any career stage. That might be a recent graduate whose academic work, open-source contributions, or personal projects are demonstrably exceptional. It might be an engineer already in the industry who has a body of delivered work they can speak to in depth. In either case, you need to be able to prove it.



What You’ll Work On

•      Build product features across our Next.js/TypeScript stack, from UI to API to data layer.

•      Collaborate with our AI Dev team on integrating intelligent features into the product experience.

•      Participate in code review, architectural decisions, and the ongoing evolution of our engineering standards.

•      Write tests alongside features — not as an afterthought — and maintain quality gates through our CI/CD pipeline.

•      Document your work in a way that serves the team, not just your memory.



What We’re Looking For

Strong fundamentals in:

·      TypeScript

·      React

·      Next.js

·      PostgreSQL

·      GitHub

·      Vercel, AWS

We also value experience working in a cloud-deployed production environment is highly valued. We’re less focused on years of experience and more focused on the quality and depth of what you’ve built.

We’re open to candidates from two backgrounds:

•      Graduates or researchers with standout academic records, published work, or personal/open-source projects that reflect a genuine engineering standard — not tutorials, but real systems with real design decisions you can articulate and defend.

•      Working engineers who can walk through their deliveries in technical depth: what they built, why it was designed that way, what they’d do differently, and what the outcomes were.


Beyond the technical baseline: we want someone who communicates clearly, handles ambiguity without freezing, and takes quality seriously. Aviation is a safety-critical domain, and the software we build reflects that.


We move fast and we ship often. You're someone who drives toward delivery rather than getting comfortable in research mode — but you also know that speed without discipline creates the kind of debt that slows everything down. We build things right because we plan to build on top of them.



How We Hire

We don’t make offers based on résumés alone. Every candidate will complete a structured technical assessment, submit a portfolio or project review, and participate in an in-depth delivery walkthrough with our engineering team. We’re looking for evidence of how you think and what you ship — not just where you’ve been.

The Structure

Six-month contract with an optional path to full-time employment for the right person. We’re investing in the right people, and we expect that investment to convert. If you’re performing, we want you permanent — this structure gives both sides the confidence to move forward.


Hours: Full-time equivalent (40 hrs/week)

Rate: Competitive — based on demonstrated capability

companySlogan: Making Aviation Intelligent, with the power of AI.
companyDescription: Hilo Aviation provides Aviation Intelligence through a revolutionary AI-driven platform offering a comprehensive integrated suite of solutions and offerings that bring unprecedented value to empower flight schools, students, pilots, financial lending institutions and global airlines. Hilo’s leadership combines experienced industry pilots, flight school owners, and airline influencers to provide a brand-new, innovative AI-driven technology application. Hilo Aviation has a clear vision and is focused on reducing costs, improving operational efficiencies and mitigating risks to revolutionize aviation education and safety.  AI data-driven actionable insights ensure that every aspect of flight training, student placement and pilot safety is efficient, affordable, insightful and accessible.  The Hilo AI Platform streamlines aviation operations, leverages student performance, tracks training milestones, optimizes resource utilization and automates scheduling from an “all-in-one” easy to use application to provide real time predictive analytics and personalized progress tracking.

title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:

  • Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
  • Label or classify content based on project guidelines
  • Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material

Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $23 per hour equivalent.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in any discipline
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
  • Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
  • Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
  • Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence

Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?

  • Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
  • Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
  • Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

title: Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
We are partnering with a general contractor to hire a Project Manager to oversee construction projects from preconstruction through closeout. This role offers the opportunity to take ownership of projects - managing budgets, schedules, subcontractors, and client communication while helping drive successful, safe, and high-quality delivery.


If you’re a builder who enjoys responsibility, problem-solving, and being involved in all phases of a project, we’d love to connect.


Responsibilities

  • Manage all phases of construction from preconstruction through closeout, ensuring projects are delivered safely, on schedule, and within budget
  • Support and enforce safety and quality standards; participate in site visits and inspections
  • Coordinate daily project execution, assist in resolving field issues, and ensure alignment with plans, specifications, and client expectations
  • Assist with estimates, takeoffs, proposals, and subcontractor bids; support buyout and contract administration
  • Manage budgets, forecasts, change orders, vendor invoices, and owner billing
  • Help secure permits and ensure compliance with codes, regulations, and contract requirements
  • Maintain accurate project documentation including RFIs, submittals, schedules, and closeout materials
  • Collaborate with Superintendents and field teams to ensure smooth project execution
  • Participate in OAC, subcontractor, and internal meetings to track progress and maintain alignment
  • Build and maintain relationships with clients, design teams, and subcontractors


Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Engineering, or equivalent experience
  • 4–8+ years of experience in construction project management
  • Experience supporting or managing projects from preconstruction through completion
  • Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills
  • Proficient with construction software (Procore, Microsoft Office, etc.)


Why Join

  • Opportunity to grow with a reputable general contractor
  • Collaborative team environment focused on quality and long-term relationships
  • Competitive compensation, benefits, and career development opportunities


Compensation

  • Competitive salary + vehicle allowance or company vehicle + gas card + performance-based bonus + full benefits (ESOP, 401K, PTO) + additional perks (continued education, gym, referral programs, and more)

companySlogan: Redefined Recruitment - Reimagined Success
companyDescription:
Scott Humphrey empowers companies and people by redefining recruitment. We prioritize quality over speed, authenticity over volume, and long-term partnerships over transactions.
 
We recruit midlevel professionals across the construction, energy, manufacturing, and shared services industries. Our recruitment consultants are regional specialists in their verticals, giving us a national reach with a local focus.
 
For candidates and job seekers, we are your career partner. We build proactive relationships before you are ready to make a move so that when the right opportunity comes along, we can connect you with employers who value your skills and experience. Whether you are seeking career advancement, a better fit, or your next leadership role, we help you navigate the market with confidence.
 
For clients, we understand your industry’s challenges, from tight project timelines to evolving workforce needs and the demand for skilled professionals who align with your culture and standards Therefore, we engage with top candidates in the market so that when you are ready to hire, we are ready with talent that fits your needs. We work closely alongside your team to provide real-time insights into labor markets, helping you stay competitive and build a workforce for the future.
 
Let us help you build a stronger team or take the next step in your career.
 
Empowering people. Redefining recruitment.

title: Cultural Strategist – Art, Fashion & Capital Influence
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

LPP Media is a venture-scale creative studio operating at the intersection of storytelling, innovation, and cultural impact. We collaborate with founders and funds seeking not just to scale, but to fundamentally shape the way the world engages with their vision. With a proven track record of raising over $100M in capital, achieving 600M+ organic views, and building brand systems for global leaders like Google, Uber Eats, Microsoft, Mercedes, and PepsiCo, we specialize in transforming purpose-driven ideas into powerful narratives that resonate deeply with the market.


LPP Media is a cinematic venture studio trusted by global brands like Google, Uber Eats, Microsoft, and the NFL.

We don’t just make content.

We craft belief.

Our storytelling systems have powered $200M+ raised and 600M+ organic views—helping visionary founders raise $4M–$40M through campaigns that turn brands into movements and moments into mythology.


The Opportunity

We’re assembling an invite‑only circle of directors, designers, illustrators, editors, and creative visionaries to partner with us on projects that touch every corner of the world.

This isn’t a standard in‑house role.

It’s a chance to attach your name, art, and vision to campaigns trusted by elite founders and brands.


What You’ll Do

✨ Create capital‑grade visuals and films for founders and brands raising millions

✨ Co‑engineer campaigns that elevate companies into cultural icons

✨ Collaborate with a studio that blends art, business, and belief


What You Get

💵 Commission‑only with no cap

💵 $5K–$25K+ per introduced client

🎨 Co‑branding opportunities

🔑 Access to private decks, pitch systems, and cinematic frameworks


Who You Are

✔️ A visionary, designer, or artist who lives at the intersection of high ticket sales, art, fashion, and story

✔️ Someone who moves people with taste and presence—not hard sells

✔️ A creative who believes the right story, told at the right moment, changes everything

LPP Media is a venture-scale creative studio forged at the intersection of myth, money, and meaning. We partner with founders and funds who don’t just want to scale — they want to change the way the world moves.

title: Radiology Recruiter
descriptionHtml:
About Expert Radiology

Expert Radiology is a founder-led teleradiology practice built by radiologists, for radiologists. We offer top-tier compensation, cutting-edge technology, and genuine flexibility — and we're growing fast. We need a recruiter who can help us find the best radiologists in the country.


The Role

You'll be our boots-on-the-ground recruiter — sourcing, screening, and moving radiologist candidates through the pipeline every day. This is a high-volume, high-impact role. You won't be building strategy decks; you'll be on the phone, in inboxes, and at conferences finding talented radiologists and getting them excited about Expert Radiology.


You'll initially report to the CEO and transition to our Head of Radiology Recruiting once that role is filled.


What You'll Do
  • Source radiologist candidates through LinkedIn, job boards, professional networks, conferences, and direct outreach
  • Screen candidates for fit: credentials, availability, subspecialty, and interest level
  • Manage candidate pipeline from first touch through onboarding handoff
  • Coordinate with credentialing and operations to keep the hiring process moving
  • Track pipeline metrics and report on sourcing activity weekly
  • Represent Expert Radiology at radiology conferences and recruiting events
  • Build and maintain a candidate database for ongoing outreach
  • Draft and post job ads on physician-specific boards (AAPPR, PracticeLink, PracticeMatch, etc.)
Who We're Looking For
  • 1–3+ years of physician or healthcare recruiting experience (radiology experience is a major plus)
  • Comfortable with high-volume outreach — phone, email, LinkedIn
  • Organized and detail-oriented — you track every candidate and never drop a thread
  • Self-starter who doesn't need daily direction
  • Familiarity with physician credentialing timelines and licensing basics
  • Experience with ATS platforms and recruiting tools
Nice to Have
  • Prior experience recruiting radiologists or other diagnostic specialists
  • Relationships with radiology residency programs or professional associations
  • Experience at a locums or physician staffing agency
  • Familiarity with teleradiology or remote physician practice models
What We Offer
  • $70,000–$90,000 base salary
  • Placement bonuses for each radiologist hired
  • Fully remote — work from anywhere in the US
  • Ground-floor opportunity at a fast-growing practice
  • Direct access to leadership — no layers of bureaucracy
  • Potential to grow into a senior recruiting role as the team scales
How to Apply

Send your resume and a brief note on your physician recruiting experience to avery@expertradiology.com. Subject line: "Radiology Recruiter — [Your Name]"
