companySlogan: Accuracy Matters
companyDescription:
At Expert Radiology it is our goal to improve patient care by providing the highest quality medical imaging evaluations and reports every time. Our dedicated team of physicians provides specialized expertise in all areas of imaging for hospitals, medical groups, imaging centers, physicians, and patients who require accurate, fast, and reliable off-site image interpretations. Our mission is to be more than readers of imaging studies, but instead to serve as valuable consultants to the patients we serve.

About our PrecisionPlus v3 Imaging Reports:
v1 radiology reports have been around since 1895 when Dr. Rontgen, the grandfather of radiology,  wrote his first radiology report.  99.9% of radiology reports are still text only. 
With v2 reports, we are seeing some people incorporating images. We've been adding images to our v2 reports for nearly a decade. In 2024, Expert Radiology ushered in v3 reports: detailed sensitive report, colorization, illustration, patient friendly report summary. With Expert Radiology, you get v3 accurate and detailed reports every time.

title: Apartment Leasing Manager
descriptionHtml:
Location: Summit Ridge

Property Size: 216 Units

Learn more about our community at: Spacious, Pet-Friendly Apartments in Temple, TX - Summit Ridge

Why Join Adara Communities?

At Adara Communities, we blend 40 years of industry experience with a forward-thinking approach to technology, customer service, and community living. Our success is driven by people - our residents, our investors, and especially our team members. When passionate individuals work together with shared commitment, anything is possible. Join us and be part of a company where your impact truly matters.

What You'll Do As An Apartment Leasing Manager

We're seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Leasing Manager who can oversee leasing operations while supporting the daily functions of the community. In this role, you will:

  • Lease apartments to maintain strong occupancy and contribute to revenue goals
  • Manage rent collection, posting of rent, and issuance of late notices
  • Assist with accepting move out notices, service requests, and resident transfer requests
  • Support managerial duties and help ensure smooth daily operations
  • Maintain confidentiality of all resident information and documentation
  • Prepare reports, administrative paperwork, and required leasing documentation
  • Assist in maintaining an active social media presence to increase visibility and generate traffic
  • Support, coach, and help develop the Leasing Agent to strengthen leasing performance
  • Provide exceptional customer service to residents and prospective renters
  • Work collaboratively with the Property Manager and onsite team to support community success

What We're Looking For

To be successful in this role, you MUST have:

  • Minimum 2 Year(s) previous experience as an Assistant Manager in the apartment industry with strong leasing experience (REQUIRED)
  • Strong sales and leasing skills with a proven ability to close
  • Excellent customer service and communication abilities
  • Professional demeanor, appearance, and work ethic
  • Flexible schedule – some weekends are required
  • Social media or marketing content creation experience strongly preferred
  • Stable work history
  • Valid driver's license and reliable transportation
  • Bilingual English/Spanish is a plus, but not required

Important: Candidates must live within a 20-mile radius of the property or be willing to relocate.

What Adara Offers You

  • Competitive pay
  • Excellent commission opportunities
  • Medical, Dental, Vision, Life & Disability Insurance
  • Generous PTO
  • Rental housing discounts
  • A supportive, people-first culture
  • Salary Range: $20/hour

If you're an experienced leasing professional with exceptional sales and customer service skills looking for a community and company that values your expertise, we'd love to hear from you.

companySlogan: This is Adara living.
companyDescription:
Headquartered in Houston, Texas, Adara Communities is a fully integrated real estate firm providing services in multifamily property management. With its launch in 2010, Adara brings with it over 105 combined years of professional management experience.

The Adara Communities management professionals bring a wealth of managerial knowledge and experience to the table and are dedicated to providing each resident with a place they can call home.  Our attention to detail and ability to access the most modern and up to date operating tools makes us one of the premier management companies in the country. 

We are focused on maintaining a high-quality, geographically diverse portfolio of apartment homes located in the highest growth markets across the United States. Adara is equipped with ample liquidity and financial flexibility and strives to maintain a positive, dynamic work environment with a team that is truly committed to being the best.

Adara owns a portfolio of apartment communities across the United States and are experts at leasing, managing, marketing, and maintaining apartment homes. We recognize that our people are a key factor in Adara's formula to provide excellent value, service, and operating performance. Our communities regularly exceed market expectations and deliver excellent residential services, which makes us one of the best multifamily companies in the industry.

title: Scrum Master
descriptionHtml:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.

Job Description

Job Title: Scrum Master (Devices/Hardware OR Software)

Location: Remote (Travel required to Mendon, UT on need basis)

Fulltime Permanent with Capgemini Engineering

About The Job You’re Considering

The Scrum Master coordinates the work of an Agile development team and oversees the software development lifecycle. This role involves planning and facilitating scrum activities such as sprint planning, daily stand-ups, demos, board reviews, and retrospectives. The Scrum Master collaborates with clients or end users to understand business requirements and acts as a liaison between development teams and product management to ensure appropriate project processes are followed. Additionally, they identify and resolve impediments that may slow development or prevent the team from meeting established goals or milestones.

Ensure their team is able to organize and follow the scrum and Client processes (Product Development Process). Help ensure all their team members have taken the appropriate step on stories so their team is a well-oiled machine. Remove Impediments, encourage the team to work only on task, and encourage Dev Manager to plan. Conduct Retrospectives, and schedule Backlog Grooming.

Your Role

  • Plan and facilitate scrum activities, including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, demos, board reviews, and retrospectives.
  • Ensuring the breakdown of work. Force the breakdown of stories into substories, tasks, spikes, etc.
  • Help build the schedule for deliverables. (Development timeline, PMO would be ultimately responsible for the schedule).
  • Resource mapping/loading, what is our capacity? (Project is what we need to do, team should be focused on who needs to do what).
  • Strategically identify if the schedule requires more resources than the team currently has. Resource balancing.
  • Collaborate with clients or end users to understand business requirements.
  • Act as a liaison between development teams and product management to ensure appropriate project processes are followed.
  • Identify and resolve impediments that may slow development or prevent the team from meeting established goals or milestones.
  • Work with team members on backlog and processes.
  • Understand and remove impediments to the scrum process.
  • Escalation of risk issues outside of the development group to other management levels. (if we fall behind during the sprint, we don’t just accept that, we try to push it and identify what can be done).

Your Skills And Experience

  • 7+ Years of related experience engineering field.
  • Bachelor’s degree in related technical field.
  • Scrum Master with hands-on experience working with cross functional Agile teams across the following engineering disciplines:
  • Mechanical / Electrical Engineering
  • Electronics, Embedded Systems & Mechatronics
  • Software Development & Validation / Testing
  • Functional Safety (ISO 26262 or equivalent standards
  • Proficiency in scrum tools (e.g. JIRA)
  • Certification (e.g., Certified ScrumMaster - CSM) is often preferred.
  • Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and Scrum framework.

The base compensation range for this role in the posted location is: $65,200 - $169,000/Year

Capgemini provides compensation range information in accordance with applicable national, state, provincial, and local pay transparency laws. The base compensation range listed for this position reflects the minimum and maximum target compensation Capgemini, in good faith, believes it may pay for the role at the time of this posting. This range may be subject to change as permitted by law.

The actual compensation offered to any candidate may fall outside of the posted range and will be determined based on multiple factors legally permitted in the applicable jurisdiction.

These may include, but are not limited to: Geographic location, Education and qualifications, Certifications and licenses, Relevant experience and skills, Seniority and performance, Market and business consideration, Internal pay equity.

It is not typical for candidates to be hired at or near the top of the posted compensation range.

In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for additional compensation such as variable incentives, bonuses, or commissions, depending on the position and applicable laws.

Benefits

Capgemini offers a comprehensive, non-negotiable benefits package to all regular, full-time employees. In the U.S. and Canada, available benefits are determined by local policy and eligibility and may include:

  • Paid time off based on employee grade (A-F), defined by policy: Vacation: 12-25 days, depending on grade, Company paid holidays, Personal Days, Sick Leave
  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage (or provincial healthcare coordination in Canada)
  • Retirement savings plans (e.g., 401(k) in the U.S., RRSP in Canada)
  • Life and disability insurance
  • Employee assistance programs
  • Other benefits as provided by local policy and eligibility

Important Notice: Compensation (including bonuses, commissions, or other forms of incentive pay) is not considered earned, vested, or payable until it becomes due under the terms of applicable plans or agreements and is subject to Capgemini’s discretion, consistent with applicable laws. The Company reserves the right to amend or withdraw compensation programs at any time, within the limits of applicable legislation.

Disclaimers

Capgemini is an Equal Opportunity Employer encouraging inclusion in the workplace. Capgemini also participates in the Partnership Accreditation in Indigenous Relations (PAIR) program which supports meaningful engagement with Indigenous communities across Canada by promoting fairness, accessibility, inclusion and respect. We value the rich cultural heritage and contributions of Indigenous Peoples and actively work to create a welcoming and respectful environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, gender identity/expression, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, genetics, veteran status, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law.

This is a general description of the Duties, Responsibilities and Qualifications required for this position. Physical, mental, sensory or environmental demands may be referenced in an attempt to communicate the manner in which this position traditionally is performed. Whenever necessary to provide individuals with disabilities an equal employment opportunity, Capgemini will consider reasonable accommodations that might involve varying job requirements and/or changing the way this job is performed, provided that such accommodation does not pose an undue hardship. Capgemini is committed to providing reasonable accommodation during our recruitment process. If you need assistance or accommodation, please reach out to your recruiting contact.

Please be aware that Capgemini may capture your image (video or screenshot) during the interview process and that image may be used for verification, including during the hiring and onboarding process.

Click the following link for more information on your rights as an Applicant in the United States. http://www.capgemini.com/resources/equal-employment-opportunity-is-the-law

It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

Capgemini Engineering is the engineering and R&D services powerhouse of the Capgemini Group, combining its broad industry knowledge and cutting-edge technologies in digital and software to support the convergence of the physical and digital worlds. Coupled with the capabilities of the rest of the Group, Capgemini Engineering helps clients to accelerate their journey towards the Intelligent Industry with more than 55,000 engineer and scientist team members in over 30 countries across sectors including Aeronautics, Space, Defense, Naval, Automotive, Rail, Infrastructure & Transportation, Energy, Utilities & Chemicals, Life Sciences, Communications, Semiconductor & Electronics, Industrial & Consumer, Software & Internet.

Capgemini Engineering is an integral part of the Capgemini Group, an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. The Group reported 2025 global revenues of €22.5 billion.

title: Chief Growth and Brand Officer
descriptionHtml:
Chief Growth & Brand Officer -** Full Time** - Not Considering Fractional Positions

Location: Remote within US

Company: An Emerging Wellness Collective (Parent of PHD Weight Loss, Dr. Ashley Wellness, OsteoCollective, and Founder Brands)


Role Overview

We are building a next-generation wellness company—one that goes far beyond weight loss to encompass longevity, metabolic health, functional medicine, and high-performance living.

This role will architect and lead the marketing strategy across a multi-brand ecosystem, including:

  • PHD Weight Loss (core revenue engine)
  • Dr. Ashley Wellness (supplements / clinical authority)
  • OsteoCollective (specialized care offering)
  • Personal brands of Dr. Doug Lucas & Dr. Ashley Lucas (audience growth + authority platforms)


The Chief Growth and Brand Officer will be responsible for driving scalable growth, building a cohesive brand architecture, and maximizing lifetime value across all business lines.

This is a builder role, not a maintainer.


Key Outcomes


The CGBO will be accountable for:

Revenue Growth

  • Increased lead volume, conversion rates, and LTV across all brands
  • Clear attribution of marketing performance to revenue

Unified Brand Ecosystem

  • Defined relationship between all brands (who serves what, when, and how they connect)
  • Strategic cross-selling and client journey mapping

Audience Expansion

  • Significant growth of founder-led audiences (social, email, content platforms)
  • Strong top-of-funnel authority positioning in wellness & longevity

Marketing Infrastructure

  • Scalable systems for paid media, organic content, CRM, and funnel optimization
  • Clean reporting dashboards tied to business KPIs

Team & Vendor Leadership

  • High-performing internal team and/or agency ecosystem
  • Clear accountability and execution cadence


Core Responsibilities


1. Multi-Brand Strategy & Positioning

  • Define the brand architecture across all companies
  • Clarify:
  • Target audiences per brand
  • Unique positioning and offers
  • Customer journey between brands
  • Prevent brand dilution while maximizing cross-brand synergy

2. Growth Strategy (Full Funnel)

  • Own strategy across:
  • Paid media (Meta, YouTube, Google, etc.)
  • Organic/social content
  • Email/SMS lifecycle marketing
  • Funnel optimization (landing pages, conversion paths)
  • Build a predictable lead to consult to client acquisition engine

3. Founder Brand Monetization & Scale

  • Turn Dr. Doug & Dr. Ashley’s personal brands into:
  • Consistent audience growth engines
  • Lead generation channels
  • Authority platforms that feed all business units
  • Align content strategy with revenue goals (not just engagement)
  • Create and direct workflow for Drs. Doug and Ashley speaking engagements

4. Data, Analytics & KPI Ownership

  • Establish and track key metrics:
  • CAC by channel & brand
  • LTV by product/service line
  • Funnel conversion rates
  • ROI on marketing spend
  • Build dashboards and reporting cadence for leadership

5. Offer & Campaign Development

  • Collaborate with clinical and operations leaders to:
  • Package new services
  • Launch new offerings
  • Test and iterate messaging
  • Lead high-impact campaigns (product launches, promos, brand expansions)

6. Team Leadership & Structure

  • Build and lead:
  • Internal marketing teams for each entity (content, paid media, CRM, etc.)
  • External partners (agencies, freelancers)
  • Define roles, KPIs, and accountability systems

7. Technology & Systems

  • Oversee marketing tech stack:
  • CRM (lead tracking, attribution)
  • Email/SMS platforms
  • Analytics tools
  • Ensure systems support scale and visibility


Ideal Candidate Profile

Experience

  • 8–12+ years in marketing, with senior leadership experience
  • Proven success scaling multi-brand or multi-offer businesses
  • Experience in one or more:
  • Health/wellness
  • Direct-to-consumer
  • E-commerce offerings
  • Community and Membership based offerings
  • High-ticket sales organizations
  • Strong background in performance marketing AND brand building


Capabilities

  • Strategic thinker who can, and will, also execute
  • Deep understanding of:
  • Funnels & conversion optimization
  • Paid acquisition & organic growth
  • Personal brand monetization
  • Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building from scratch


Leadership Style

  • High ownership and accountability
  • Data-driven but creatively sharp
  • Can partner effectively with:
  • Founders (strong personalities, strong visions)
  • Sales teams
  • Clinical/operations teams


Why This Role is Unique

  • You are not inheriting a clean, finished system—you are designing it
  • You will help define the future of a multi-entity wellness brand
  • You will directly influence how:
  • Clients enter and move through the ecosystem
  • Founders scale their impact and authority
  • Revenue is generated across multiple verticals


Compensation & Structure

  • Base Salary: $150K–$220K+
  • Performance Bonus tied to: Revenue growth, CAC/LTV improvements
  • Potential long-term incentive

Benefits

  • Medical (ICHRA)
  • Life
  • Voluntary Supplemental benefits including vision, dental, additional life, hospital indemnity, etc.
  • Unlimited PTO
  • 401K with company match

companyDescription:
PHD Weight Loss is a rapidly growing, doctor and dietitian developed, weight loss company with 4 clinic locations and a booming nationwide program. Since creating our very first life change back in 2016, the team at PHD Weight Loss has worked tirelessly to build, perfect, and continue our unique approach to customized, successful long-term weight loss, while being committed to the authenticity and integrity that separates us from others. We care to our very core. We care about changing lives and shifting mindsets. And we care about our clients while they are re-learning to care for themselves. 

We are looking for individuals whose values align with what we’re doing here at PHD Weight Loss. People who operate with a radically open mind. Who practice acceptance over judgement. We are a high energy group who choose grit when faced with opportunities to improve someone's life. With vulnerable hearts, we embrace our clients, our teammates, and every challenge that presents itself as a potential for positive impact. If all of this sounds like you and you’re looking to join a fun-loving, motivated, and inspiring team, let’s talk!

title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:

  • Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
  • Label or classify content based on project guidelines
  • Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material

Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $23 per hour equivalent.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in any discipline
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
  • Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
  • Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
  • Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence

Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?

  • Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
  • Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
  • Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

title: Quantitative Researcher
descriptionHtml:
India

Versor Investments (“Versor”) is a pioneer in applying AI and alternative data to global equity markets. As a quantitative equities boutique, we focus on systematically delivering uncorrelated alpha across single stocks, equity index futures, and corporate events. Founded in 2014 and headquartered in New York, Versor manages assets on behalf of a global client base. Our edge is defined by four core pillars that underpin how we operate and how we continue to stay at the frontier of quantitative investing. These include the use of alternative data across both developed and emerging markets, a disciplined integration of artificial intelligence with human judgment and domain expertise, deep experience in systematic investing, and an embedded approach to risk management that informs research, portfolio construction, and implementation.

Role Summary

The Quantitative Researcher position will be based in Mumbai and be a part of the Portfolio Enhancements team. They will collaborate closely with senior researchers to develop rigorous scientific methods and analytics for a sophisticated quantitative investment process covering forecast models, risk management models, and portfolio optimization tools.

We are seeking candidates who have excelled in quantitative research and have demonstrated the ability to conduct independent research utilizing large datasets. Quantitative research experience could be in the fields of mathematics, statistics, pure sciences, engineering, etc. Prior experience in investments and finance is beneficial but not mandatory. This role is ideal for candidates who have a strong research background in experimental sciences and are interested in quantitative investment research.

Responsibilities

  • Apply advanced statistical techniques to develop forecast models utilizing large datasets
  • Develop and refine risk management and portfolio optimization models
  • Identify potential enhancements to quantitative investment processes
  • Review research papers and journals as a part of the research process
  • Manage different aspects of the research process including methodology selection, data collection and analysis, prototyping, and back-testing
  • Build systems for implementation of quantitative investment processes
  • Contribute towards white papers and thought leadership articles published by the firm

Qualifications

  • PhD, M.Tech. or M.Sc. in computer science, economics, statistics, physics or similar quantitative disciplines from a top tier institute
  • Demonstrated ability to conduct independent in-depth research utilizing large datasets
  • Validate prior research experience through research publications
  • Excellent mathematical and statistical skills
  • Working knowledge of Python will be beneficial
  • Good written and oral communication skills
  • Good coordination skills and ability to work in a team

title: Controller
location: Washington, Distrito de Colúmbia, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Position Type: Remote (Must be located in the Washington DC Area)

Department: Finance

Contact Government Services (CGS) is seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Controller to lead our accounting operations and financial management functions. This role is critical to ensuring financial accuracy, compliance, and strategic insight as we continue to grow. The ideal candidate brings a strong background in services-based organizations (legal, IT, or government contracting) and thrives in a fast-paced, multi-project environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Oversee all accounting operations, including general ledger, A/R, A/P, and monthly close
  • Maintain and ensure accuracy of P&L and Balance Sheet reporting
  • Manage both cash and accrual accounting processes
  • Support annual audits and ensure compliance with GAAP standards
  • Lead end-of-year tax preparation and coordination
  • Maintain detailed financial records, including gross and net profit at global, project, and resource levels
  • Oversee invoice generation (approximately 300 invoices/month)
  • Manage treasury functions, including banking relationships, line of credit, and credit card programs
  • Develop and maintain cash flow and accrual projections for executive leadership
  • Provide financial analysis, identify trends, and highlight potential risks
  • Support multiple teams, projects, and customers simultaneously
  • Supervise and mentor accounting staff




Qualifications

  • CPA required
  • 10+ years of progressive accounting experience
  • Prior experience as a Controller
  • Background in services industries (legal, IT, or government contracting preferred)
  • Strong experience with QuickBooks and Unanet
  • Proven experience supporting audits and ensuring GAAP compliance
  • Hands-on experience with A/R and A/P functions
  • Demonstrated ability to manage complex, multi-project financial environments
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent leadership and communication abilities




Additional Requirements

  • Must be located in the DMV area
  • Ability to go in-office at least once per week (mail handling and administrative needs)




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Business Analyst
descriptionHtml:
🚀 We’re Hiring: Business Process Analyst (Dallas, TX | Full-Time)

Base : $60-70K


No Visa support for this position


Are you passionate about solving complex problems and driving real business impact? We’re looking for a Business Process Analyst to help transform operations, optimize workflows, and unlock efficiency across the organization.

This is a high-impact role where you’ll collaborate with cross-functional teams, leverage data insights, and take ownership of meaningful process improvements that directly contribute to business growth.

🔍 What You’ll Do

  • Analyze and map end-to-end business processes to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities
  • Use data to uncover trends, diagnose issues, and build actionable solutions
  • Translate business needs into clear system and process requirements
  • Design scalable workflows and maintain structured documentation
  • Proactively track project progress, flag risks, and communicate with stakeholders
  • Contribute to operational initiatives and cross-functional projects

💡 What We’re Looking For

  • Strong analytical mindset with advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, data analysis, visualization)
  • Ability to thrive in ambiguity and independently drive solutions
  • Detail-oriented with a strong commitment to accuracy and quality
  • Excellent communication skills across technical and non-technical teams
  • Adaptability and eagerness to learn new tools and technologies
  • Experience leveraging AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini) for problem-solving

⭐ Nice to Have

  • 3+ years in application development or system engineering (Python, JavaScript, etc.)
  • Experience with Google Apps Script (GAS) for automation
  • SQL skills (BigQuery is a plus)
  • Familiarity with CRM/ERP systems (e.g., Salesforce)
  • Background in business process mapping and documentation

🌟 Why This Role?

  • Opportunity to drive real operational transformation
  • High visibility and ownership of impactful projects
  • Collaborative, forward-thinking environment
  • Exposure to cutting-edge tools, including AI-driven solutions

📍 Location: Dallas, TX

🕒 Employment Type: Full-Time

title: Adaptive Skills and Career Pathway Specialist
descriptionHtml:
Are you ready to make a difference in someone's life?

THEN WE ARE LOOKING FOR YOU!!!

New Horizons, a nonprofit serving special needs adults in the San Fernando Valley, Santa Clarita, Simi Valley, Moorpark, Thousand Oaks, and Ventura, for more than 60 years, seeks a self-starter to be a Job Coach.

This will be a very rewarding experience for reliable people who have a passion to help others.

A perfect part-time job!

Flexible availability. 20 - 30 hours a week (may include evenings and weekends)

Rate: $25 - $27 Per Hour

SUMMARY: Adaptive Skills Training/Career Pathway Specialist is a hybrid role that is responsible for designing and implementing adaptive skills training plans under the supervision of an Adaptive Skills Training Professional as well as supporting New Horizon's Youth Navigation Center programs. This role involves working directly with individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities to address skill deficits in communication, social functioning, and other adaptive skills areas. Guided by student preferences, services may be delivered in school settings, NH Campus, within the community, at their home, and/or remotely. The AST/ Career Pathway Specialist fosters a welcoming, engaging, and outcome-oriented environment for adults with disabilities.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES include the following: By Program Area

  • Coordinated Career Pathways: Career Pathway Navigator partners with Members to develop and implement a Person-Centered Career Plan (PCCP) that reflects each individual's strengths, interests, and goals
  • Adaptive Skills Training: Collaborate with the supervising Adaptive Skills Training Professional to develop individualized adaptive skills training plans
  • Pre-Employment Services: Support Job Exploration, Postsecondary Counseling, Workplace Readiness Training, Self-Advocacy Training, and Paid Student Work-Experience
  • Digital Literacy Playbook: Deliver engaging and accessible training modules: Privacy & Safety, Communication Skills, and Relationship Building that equip participants with the knowledge and skills to safely, responsibly, and effectively navigate the digital world
  • Travel Training: Build Independent travel skills for safe and confident community access

WORK ENVIRONMENT

  • This position may involve working in member's homes, community settings or training facilities. Travel may be required to support members in various locations
  • Flexible work hours to accommodate program schedules

Requirements

  • Must speak English
  • Bachelor's Degree or equivalent
  • Registered Behavior Technician Certification
  • ACRE Certified in Customized Employment (New Horizons will train if applicant does not have)
  • Experience with special needs / developmental disabilities (preferred)
  • Driver's License for 3+ years required. Must be at least 21 years of age
  • Must be willing to drive an 8-passanger van
  • Authorized to work in the United States

New Horizons offers a supportive, team environment. TOGETHER WE MAKE A DIFFERENCE!!!

Benefits

  • 30+ work hours eligible for part-time benefits (Medical, Vision & Dental)
  • Paid sick leave
  • Differential rates for Holidays
  • All paid trainings including CPR/First Aide
  • Discounts Program
  • Referral Program
  • FSA
  • And more..

 Coordinated Career Pathways: Career Pathway Navigator partners with Members to develop and implement a Person-Centered Career Plan (PCCP) that reflects each individual's strengths, interests, and goals Adaptive Skills Training: Collaborate with the supervising Adaptive Skills Training Professional to develop individualized adaptive skills training plans Pre-Employment Services: Support Job Exploration, Postsecondary Counseling, Workplace Readiness Training, Self-Advocacy Training, and Paid Student Work-Experience Digital Literacy Playbook: Deliver engaging and accessible training modules: Privacy & Safety, Communication Skills, and Relationship Building that equip participants with the knowledge and skills to safely, responsibly, and effectively navigate the digital world Travel Training: Build Independent travel skills for safe and confident community access

WORK ENVIRONMENT

 This position may involve working in member's homes, community settings or training facilities. Travel may be required to support members in various locations Flexible work hours to accommodate program schedules

Requirements

Must speak EnglishBachelor's Degree or equivalentRegistered Behavior Technician Certification ACRE Certified in Customized Employment (New Horizons will train if applicant does not have)Experience with special needs / developmental disabilities (preferred)Driver's License for 3+ years required. Must be at least 21 years of ageMust be willing to drive an 8-passanger vanAuthorized to work in the United States

New Horizons offers a supportive, team environment. TOGETHER WE MAKE A DIFFERENCE!!!

Benefits

30+ work hours eligible for part-time benefits (Medical, Vision & Dental)Paid sick leaveDifferential rates for HolidaysAll paid trainings including CPR/First AideDiscounts ProgramReferral ProgramFSAAnd more..

companyDescription: New Horizons empowers individuals with disabilities to reach their full potential.

title: Sr. Key Account Representative
descriptionHtml:
Vuori is re-defining what athletic apparel looks like: built to move and sweat in but designed with a casual aesthetic to transition into everyday life. We draw inspiration from an active coastal California lifestyle; an integration of fitness, creative expression and life. Our high energy fast paced retail environment is reflected in the clothes we make. We aim to inspire others to take on all aspects of their lives with clarity, enthusiasm and purpose…while having a lot of fun along the way. We are proud to be an outlet for opportunity and for personal growth and success.



Job Description

We are looking for a commercially driven and brand-focused Senior Key Account Representative to support and elevate performance across our international franchise network. Reporting to the International Franchise Manager, this role is responsible for working with partners to ensure best-in-class execution across all franchise markets and supporting partners to deliver premium and consistent brand experiences in-store and across consumer touchpoints.

This individual will partner closely with franchise accounts and cross-functional teams to ensure excellence in product assortment, visual merchandising, retail marketing, training, and operational execution, while also leveraging data and analytics to monitor performance, identify trends, and drive profitable growth. The ideal candidate is equally passionate about retail execution and business performance, with the ability to translate insights into action.

What you’ll get to do:

  • Ensure strong in-store execution across all franchise markets, including assortment, visual merchandising, product storytelling, and brand presentation
  • Partner with franchise accounts to ensure retail teams are trained and equipped to deliver a premium, brand-aligned customer experience
  • Drive visual merchandising excellence, including regular photo reporting and close alignment with the International Visual Manager
  • Collaborate with Brand Marketing to ensure retail marketing campaigns, activations, and events are executed to brand expectations
  • Support new store openings (NSOs) to ensure operational readiness, strong visual execution, and successful launch strategies
  • Coordinate cross-functional efforts to ensure all NSO deliverables are completed on time and to standard
  • Maintain consistent communication with franchise partners to drive alignment, accountability, and executional excellence across all markets
  • Analyze weekly and seasonal sales performance using retail KPIs to identify trends, risks, and growth opportunities
  • Develop and maintain standardized KPI reporting tools to drive visibility and data-driven decision-making across markets
  • Provide actionable competitive intelligence by analyzing local market dynamics, monitoring regional promotional activity and holidays, and identifying consumer trends to inform merchandising and business strategies
  • Partner closely with Sales Operations and Logistics to ensure timely product delivery, order fulfillment, and issue resolution
  • Assist with seasonal market coordination, sample line management and organization, F&B arrangements, and other showroom needs

Qualifications

Who you are:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent years of experience preferred
  • 5+ years of experience in retail management, wholesale, or franchise management, preferably in international markets
  • Passionate about retail environments, brand storytelling, and delivering premium customer experiences
  • Highly organized with strong project management and execution skills
  • Excellent communication and relationship-building abilities across cross-functional teams and external partners
  • Strong understanding of retail math, KPIs, and performance analytics
  • Resourceful, flexible, and excellent team player; ability to work cross-functionally and contribute beyond defined responsibilities
  • Comfortable working across multiple time zones and traveling as needed

Additional Information

Our investment in you:

At Vuori, we’re proud to offer the following to our employees:

  • Health Insurance
  • Savings and Retirement Plan
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Generous Vuori Discount & Industry Perks
  • Paid Time Off
  • Wellness & Fitness benefits

The salary range for this role is $93,000 per year - $120,000 per year. This role is bonus eligible.

Vuori is proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Director Talent Management
companyName: The Christopher Group, Agile HR Business Solutions
descriptionHtml:
Director of Talent & Workforce Planning

Overview

A dynamic organization is seeking a Director of Talent & Workforce Planning to lead enterprise workforce strategy, talent acquisition, and retention efforts. This role ensures the business has the right talent in place to meet current and future demands while improving workforce stability and reducing turnover.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead enterprise workforce planning strategy, aligning labor forecasts with business growth and operational needs
  • Oversee and optimize recruiting operations, processes, and technology
  • Build and manage talent pipelines across critical roles, reducing reliance on external agencies
  • Develop analytics and reporting to drive insights on turnover, hiring, and workforce trends
  • Partner with leadership to strengthen succession planning and internal mobility
  • Implement strategies to improve early-tenure retention and overall employee experience
  • Drive a skills-based workforce approach, including upskilling and reskilling initiatives
  • Ensure compliance with employment practices and promote inclusive hiring

Success Measures

  • Reduced turnover, particularly in early tenure
  • Improved hiring efficiency and workforce readiness
  • Increased internal mobility and bench strength
  • Lower cost-per-hire and agency utilization

Qualifications

  • Experience in workforce planning, talent acquisition, or enterprise talent leadership
  • Background supporting large, distributed, or hourly workforces
  • Experience in both union and non-union environments preferred
  • Strong analytical and operational mindset with a data-driven approach
  • Proven ability to partner with cross-functional leaders to drive business outcomes

companyDescription:
The Christopher Group (TCG) is the nation’s premier specialized boutique for Agile HR Business Solutions: HR Executive Search, Interim HR Leaders, and Expert Consultants. Named America’s Best HR Executive Recruiting Firm by Forbes and staffed by former HR professionals and highly trained search practitioners, TCG’s Recruiting Services Division and Consulting Services Division provides customized HR Business Solutions for clients, across all industries, locations, company sizes, and verticals.

Our comprehensive understanding and deep relationships within HR have allowed us to become the firm of choice for many clients like Kraft, Whirlpool Corporation, Pepsi, St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital, Welch’s, Nationwide, SC Johnson, Walgreens, USAA, Verizon, Penn State University, Honeywell, Hanes, Allstate, Delta Dental, BP, Capital One, Comcast, Dish, Exxon Mobil, First Energy, Goodyear, Johnson & Johnson, Goodyear, Parker Hannifin, Connecticut Children’s Medical Center, Lamb Weston, Meijer, Petco, Air Products and many more.

title: Executive Assistant, People & Culture (Temp)
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Welcome to Warner Bros. Discovery… the stuff dreams are made of.

Who We Are…

When we say, “the stuff dreams are made of,” we’re not just referring to the world of wizards, dragons and superheroes, or even to the wonders of Planet Earth. Behind WBD’s vast portfolio of iconic content and beloved brands, are the storytellers bringing our characters to life, the creators bringing them to your living rooms and the dreamers creating what’s next…

From brilliant creatives, to technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive.

Your New Role

Provides administrative and clerical support to a People & Culture leader. This position will be located in New York. 


This is a temporary position until the end of the year.

Your Role Accountabilities

  • Arranges executive calendar and travel, and schedules meetings, independently or as directed.

  • Coordinates events, travel, and all associated reservations and accommodations.

  • Creates and submits expense reports.

  • Tracks ongoing projects and is well-informed of related activities and associated materials.

  • Assists with creating decks, taking notes, conducting research, and executing special projects as needed. 

  • Maintains a strong organizational system for all materials and information.

  • Oversees general office operations, including but not limited to keeping inventory, ordering supplies, and coordinating with building personnel. 

Qualifications & Experience

  • Minimum 1-3 years experience providing administrative support required, preferably within the TV or entertainment industry or for an HR Team.

  • High School diploma or equivalent required.

  • BA/BS degree. 

  • Must have proficiency with MS Word & Excel, PowerPoint, & the internet.

  • Must have excellent proofreading & writing skills.

  • Must have excellent organizational skills & possess ability to prioritize tasks.

  • Must have excellent communication skills.

  • Must have ability to create & maintain databases & other resources.

  • Must be able to interface with all levels of personnel, including senior management, talent, producers and journalists.

  • General Office Activities – including, but not limited to walking, standing, sitting, climbing stairs, typing, filing, lifting, bending, etc.

  • Must be able to work in extremely fast-paced environment.

  • Must be able to handle multiple tasks simultaneously.

  • Must have excellent follow-through skills.

  • Must have ability to self-direct and take initiative to pursue projects in the office with minimal direction.

  • Must be willing to collaborate with other staff within the office on common tasks or projects impacting the larger department.

  • Must be able to successfully see projects/tasks through to completion.

  • Must be able to work under minimal supervision.

  • Must be detail-oriented and maintain confidentiality.

  • Must be able to work under strict time constraints.

  • Must be able to resolve conflicts within the scope of responsibility.

  • Must be able to work overtime, as required.


How We Get Things Done…

This last bit is probably the most important! Here at WBD, our guiding principles are the core values by which we operate and are central to how we get things done. You can find them at www.wbd.com/guiding-principles/ along with some insights from the team on what they mean and how they show up in their day to day. We hope they resonate with you and look forward to discussing them during your interview.

Championing Inclusion at WBD

Warner Bros. Discovery embraces the opportunity to build a workforce that reflects a wide array of perspectives, backgrounds and experiences. Being an equal opportunity employer means that we take seriously our responsibility to consider qualified candidates on the basis of merit, without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, mental or physical disability, and genetic information, marital status, citizenship status, military status, protected veteran status or any other category protected by law.

If you’re a qualified candidate with a disability and you require adjustments or accommodations during the job application and/or recruitment process, please visit our accessibility page for instructions to submit your request.

In compliance with local law, we are disclosing the compensation, or a range thereof, for roles in locations where legally required. Actual salaries will vary based on several factors, including but not limited to external market data, internal equity, location, skill set, experience, and/or performance. Base pay is just one component of Warner Bros. Discovery’s total compensation package for employees. Pay Range: $50,756.00 - $94,261.00 salary per year. Other rewards may include annual bonuses, short- and long-term incentives, and program-specific awards. In addition, Warner Bros. Discovery provides a variety of benefits to employees, including health insurance coverage, an employee wellness program, life and disability insurance, a retirement savings plan, paid holidays and sick time and vacation.

companyDescription:
Warner Bros. Discovery, a premier global media and entertainment company, offers audiences the world’s most differentiated and complete portfolio of content, brands and franchises across television, film, streaming and gaming. The new company combines WarnerMedia’s premium entertainment, sports and news assets with Discovery’s leading non-fiction and international entertainment and sports businesses.

****Please be aware of recruitment scams by individuals posing as employers and encouraging candidates to apply for, interview and/or accept nonexistent job opportunities as a means to solicit personal information or money. The online scammers have become much more sophisticated in their attempts to lure victims.
 
Employment opportunities and job offers at Warner Bros. Discovery will always come from our Talent Acquisition and hiring teams. Never provide sensitive, personal information to someone unless you’re confident who the recipient is. WBD does not extend job offers via email or on any other messaging tools to individuals to whom we have not made prior contact. Our email domain is @wbd.com. A valid link for employment with Warner Bros. Discovery can be found at https://careers.wbd.com/global/en.

If you believe you have been contacted by a scammer and may be the victim of fraud or identity theft, you should report details to the police where you live. You can also report job scams to the FTC. Learn more at https://consumer.ftc.gov/articles/job-scams. *****

title: Data Analyst
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
BC Forward is currently seeking for a highly motivated Data Analyst for a Onsite Role in New York, NY


Position Title: Data Analyst

Location: New York, NY

Anticipated Start Date: 5/4/2026

Please note this is the target date and is subject to change. BCforward will send official notice ahead of a

confirmed start date.

Expected Duration: 03+ Months (W2 contract)

Job Type: Contract

Primary Skill Required for the Role: Tableau

Data Analysis Advanced

Microsoft - Excel

We are looking for Alteryx, Snowflake and Data Analysis skills/experience.

  • Data Analytics Contractor, you will analyze and interpret complex data sets to provide actionable insights and support decision-making processes.
  • Collect, process, and analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
  • Develop and maintain data models, dashboards, and reports to visualize data findings.
  • Utilize statistical and analytical tools to perform data analysis and generate insights.
  • Collaborate with project teams to understand data requirements and deliver relevant analytical solutions.
  • Ensure data accuracy and integrity by performing data validation and quality checks.

Benefits:

BCforward offers all eligible employees a comprehensive benefits package including, but not limited to major medical, HSA, dental, vision, employer-provided group life, voluntary life insurance, short-term disability, long-term disability, and 401k.


About BCforward:

Founded in 1998 on the idea that industry leaders needed a professional service, and workforce management expert, to

fuel the development and execution of core business and technology strategies, BCforward is a Black-owned firm providing

unique solutions supporting value capture and digital product delivery needs for organizations around the world.

Headquartered in Indianapolis, IN with an Offshore Development Center in Hyderabad, India, BCforward’s 6,000

consultants support more than 225 clients globally.

BCforward champions the power of human potential to help companies transform, accelerate, and scale. Guided by our core values of People-Centric, Optimism, Excellence, Diversity, and Accountability, our professionals have helped our clients achieve their strategic goals for more than 25 years. Our strong culture and clear values have enabled BCforward to become a market leader and best in class place to work.

BCforward is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against based on disability.

To learn more about how BCforward collects and uses personal information as part of the recruiting process, view our Privacy Notice and CCPA Addendum. As part of the recruitment process, we may ask for you to disclose and provide us with various categories of personal information, including identifiers, professional information, commercial information, education information, and other related information. BCforward will only use this information to complete the recruitment process.

This posting is not an offer of employment. All applicants applying for positions in the United States must be legally authorized to work in the United States. The submission of intentionally false or fraudulent information in response to this posting may render the applicant ineligible for the position. Any subsequent offer of employment will be considered employment at-will regardless of the anticipated assignment duration.

companySlogan: Our global consulting and workforce fulfillment empower you to innovate, scale and accomplish the extraordinary
companyDescription:
BCforward is a global talent and technology solutions partner helping organizations accelerate outcomes through people, platforms, and expertise.

Founded in 1998 and headquartered in Indianapolis, BCforward supports enterprise and public sector clients across the United States, Canada, and India. For more than 25 years, we have helped organizations navigate change, scale operations, and deliver-mission critical initiatives by connecting the right talent with the right solutions. 

Our capabilities span: 
- Talent Solutions
- Professional + Project Services
- Workforce Management + Payroll
- Technology + AI-Enabled Solutions

Through a unified delivery model, BCforward helps organizations build high-performing teams, modernize operations, and execute complex programs with speed and confidence. 

We partner with leading organizations across industries including financial services, healthcare, government, life sciences, technology, energy + utilities, manufacturing, and retail.

As one of the largest minority-owned consulting and workforce solutions firms in the United States, BCforward is committed to creating opportunity, delivering measurable value for clients, and building careers for talented professionals who power the future of work.

descriptionHtml:

Full-time babysitter opportunity in Chicago for a newborn. The ideal candidate will have experience in caring for infants and providing a safe and nurturing environment. Duties include feeding, changing diapers, soothing, and engaging in age-appropriate activities. The candidate must have excellent communication skills, patience, and be reliable. title: Graphic Designer
descriptionHtml:
Role Description

The Graphic Designer will be responsible for the development and execution of campaign assets and client deliverables. This person will collaborate with the Creative Manager, copywriters, social media managers, accounts/project managers, and media team as needed to craft designs that represent the brand and are strategic, effective and impactful. Primary functions will include graphic design for campaign development and brand identity.


Key Responsibilities

· Conceptualize and design creative assets for campaigns across digital, social, out-of-home, and overall brand identity.

· Translate creative direction into polished, high-quality visuals for marketing materials and presentations.

· Collaborate with creative and account team members to concept new ideas and ensure design aligns with brand voice and campaign goals.

· Maintain visual consistency across all deliverables.

· Execute all design assets based on an approved concept in a timely and efficient manner.

· Contribute to the development of brand guidelines and visual systems.

· Ensure all designs adhere to brand guidelines and project requirements.

· Revise and refine designs based on feedback from internal and external stakeholders.

· Stay up to date on design trends and tools, including the use of AI


What We’re Looking For

· 2-5 years of professional experience in graphic design. Bonus points if experience is in an agency or marketing environment!

· Experience creating social-first content including paid social ads

· Knowledge of graphic design principles including typography, layout and hierarchy.

· Curious learner who can embrace AI and incorporate into their workflow.

· Proficiency in Adobe Creative Cloud suite

· Bonus points for experience in photography, web design (UX/UI), or motion design & animation using Adobe After Effects, and Premiere Pro.

title: Executive Assistant to President
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Executive Assistant to President

A top-tier, mid-sized hedge fund is seeking an experienced Executive Assistant to support a highly dynamic President in a fast-paced, high-performance environment. This is a unique opportunity to step into a well-established role supporting a senior leader.


Location: Midtown East, New York City

Schedule: In-office, 5 days/week (Monday–Friday)


Typical office hours are approximately 7:45 AM to 6:00/6:30 PM. While the role does not require constant weekend work, the ideal candidate maintains a responsive, “24/7 mindset” for urgent matters.


Compensation & Benefits

  • Salary: around $150,000 – $250,000 (commensurate with experience)
  • Discretionary bonus
  • Fully paid health benefits
  • 401(k) (no match)
  • 25 days PTO
  • Daily breakfast and lunch and other perks


Role Overview

This role requires a highly proactive, detail-oriented Executive Assistant who thrives in a demanding, high-touch environment. The President operates at a rapid pace with constant calendar changes and high-volume communication, requiring exceptional organizational skills, sound judgment, and adaptability.


Key Responsibilities

  • Manage a complex, high-volume, ever-changing calendar with precision and foresight
  • Ensure the President is consistently prepared, on time, and optimized for all meetings
  • Act as a strategic gatekeeper for internal and external scheduling requests
  • Coordinate global travel arrangements and process expense reports
  • Partner closely with a Personal Assistant on select personal matters
  • Anticipate needs and proactively solve problems in real time


Qualifications

  • 3+ years supporting C-suite executives (or 5+ years supporting senior leadership)
  • Experience in financial services strongly preferred; hedge fund experience is a plus
  • Proven track record managing complex scheduling for a high-demand executive
  • Bachelor’s degree preferred (not required with relevant experience)
  • Strong preference for candidates with in-office, high-touch support experience


Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Highly proactive and solutions-oriented, with a demonstrated ability to drive outcomes
  • Strong judgment, intellectual flexibility, and adaptability under pressure
  • Confident, assertive, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced environment
  • Collaborative, team-oriented, and personable with a warm, professional demeanor
  • Process-driven but not rigid—able to pivot seamlessly when priorities shift
  • High level of integrity, self-awareness, and accountability


If you are a dedicated Executive Assistant who thrives in a fast-paced, high-impact role and enjoys being a true partner to senior leadership, we encourage you to apply.


You can use WorkGrades to collect and manage your references for free and share them with us or anyone else you choose by visiting workgrades.com/home/candidate. Candidates with references are always preferred by our clients. Now is the most important time to stand out from the crowd. We suggest that you ensure you have updated your LinkedIn profile and that you start collecting your references early.

title: Employee Relations Partner
descriptionHtml:
At Accendra Health, we understand that healthcare is complex, and we’re here to make it easier. We help deliver care beyond traditional settings, making essential products and services more accessible through every stage of life. As part of the care team, our teammates play a critical role in delivering personalized, long-term care for the patients we serve.

With deep expertise promoting health outside the hospital and a presence in communities nationwide through our Apria and Byram Healthcare brands, Accendra Health does more than just deliver the essentials.

If you’re interested in meaningful work with impact, explore our career opportunities and join us in our purpose of Bringing Care To Life™.

Job Description

The compensation range for this role is $74,900-$90,000/year based on experience.

Position Summary

The Employee Relations Partner is responsible for supporting a healthy and productive work environment by managing employee relations matters, conducting investigations, and advising on policy interpretation across the organization. This role provides case management, guidance, and support to managers and teammates on employee relations issues, ensuring compliance with policies and employment laws. The Employee Relations Partner will assist in investigations, documentation, and the implementation of ER programs and tools to promote a positive and compliant workplace.

Essential Job Functions

  • Serve as trusted advisor to managers, HRBP’s and employees on ER policies, procedures, performance management, and conflict resolution.
  • Independently manage employee relations cases including performance issues, disciplinary actions, investigations, and conflict resolution. Ensure thorough documentation and timely resolution.
  • Partner with Legal on specific cases requiring review or escalation. Ensure alignment with legal guidance and contribute case insights to broader risk assessments.
  • Assist in the development and maintenance of ER documentation, templates, and knowledge resources.
  • Maintain accurate case records and contribute to ER reporting efforts. Identify patterns and flag emerging trends to the ER Lead for strategic review.
  • Ensure compliance with employment laws and internal policies in all ER activities.
  • Ensure consistent interpretation and application of HR policies. Provide feedback to the ER Lead on policy gaps or areas for improvement.
  • Support ER training initiatives and participate in continuous improvement efforts.
  • Execute assigned involuntary separation cases with precision and professionalism, following established ER protocols.
  • Prepare and review termination documentation, ensuring completeness and compliance with company standards.
  • Provide direct support to managers during termination conversations, including logistics coordination and post-conversation follow-up.
  • Collaborate with HRBPs to gather necessary context and ensure alignment on impacted employee selections.
  • Maintain accurate records of separation cases in HR systems, ensuring confidentiality and data accuracy.
  • Flag inconsistencies or potential compliance issues in separation packets for review by ER Leads or Sr. Manager.
  • Participate in ER team meetings and contribute feedback to improve processes and enhance employee experience during transitions.

Supplemental Job Functions

Perform additional duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education & Experience Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
  • PHR certification or equivalent preferred.
  • 4+ years of HR experience with a focus on employee relations or HR generalist responsibilities.
  • Experience supporting workplace investigations and managing ER cases.
  • Working knowledge of employment law and HR practices.
  • Experience in a collaborative or matrixed environment is a plus.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

  • Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.
  • Detail-oriented with strong organizational and documentation skills.
  • Pushes for transparency and cultural integrity
  • Advocates for equity, inclusion, and fairness
  • Communicates neutrally and coaches with credibility
  • Navigates ambiguity and evolving org structures with clarity
  • Brings cultural fluency across global and distributed teams
  • Familiarity with tiered ER COE models and scalable service delivery
  • Connects ER work to broader business and leadership priorities
  • Leads adoption of new models and ways of working
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HRIS systems (e.g., Workday).
  • Comfortable with AI and emerging ER technologies

Teammate Benefits

As an Accendra Health employee, you have choices to fit your life. Our comprehensive benefits program is designed to meet you where you are — through all of life’s stages. We’ve got you and your family covered with benefits that support your health, finances, and overall wellness.

Our Benefits Program Includes

  • Medical, dental, and vision care coverage
  • Paid time off plan
  • 401(k) Plan
  • Flexible Spending Accounts
  • Basic life insurance
  • Short-and long-term disability coverage
  • Accident insurance
  • Teammate Assistance Program
  • Paid parental leave
  • Domestic partner benefits
  • Mental, physical, and financial well-being programs

If you feel this opportunity could be the next step in your career, we encourage you to apply.

Accendra is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, genetic information, religion, disability, age, status as a veteran, or any other status prohibited by applicable national, federal, state or local law.

Note: Accendra is not accepting unsolicited assistance from search firms for this employment opportunity. Please, no phone calls or emails. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our Company via email, the Internet, or in any form and/or method without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our Company. No fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by our Company as a result of the referral or through other means.

#AccendraHealth
companyDescription: Accendra Health (formerly Owens & Minor, Inc.) is a leading nationwide provider of critical products, technology, and services that support health beyond the hospital for millions of Americans each year. Backed by the industry-leading expertise of our Apria and Byram Healthcare brands, Accendra Health is reimagining the future of home-based care with one of the broadest portfolios of essentials for diabetes, sleep health, wound care, respiratory care, urology, and ostomy. At Accendra Health, we are Bringing Care To Life™: connecting patients, providers, and insurers, and delivering innovative solutions that help promote peace of mind and improve quality of life for people living with chronic, complex, and acute health conditions.

title: Membership & Marketing Assistant
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Established in 1989, Jacksonville Golf & Country Club is a prestigious private, member-owned club located centrally on Florida’s First Coast, near the beaches and downtown Jacksonville. The Club offers a vibrant lifestyle with a wide range of recreational and social opportunities in a welcoming, family-friendly environment. Its 25,000-square-foot clubhouse overlooks a championship 18-hole golf course and provides diverse amenities, including dining spaces, banquet areas, a fitness center, spa, tennis courts, a heated Olympic-size pool, and more. The Club prides itself on exceptional service, a sense of community, and a high-quality experience for all members and guests.


Job Title: Membership & Marketing Assistant                                                     

Employment Type: Full-Time


Position Summary

We are seeking a dynamic and detail-oriented Membership & Marketing Assistant to support membership growth, engagement, and brand visibility. This role blends hospitality, marketing, and administrative support, with a strong focus on member experience and digital content creation.

                                       

Key Responsibilities

Membership & Member Experience

  • Assist with membership inquiries, tours, and onboarding
  • Serve as a responsive, professional point of contact for members
  • Support engagement and retention initiatives

                                      

Marketing & Content Creation

  • Create and manage social media content (Instagram, Facebook, etc.)
  • Capture photography and video during events and daily club activities
  • Assist with content calendars, email newsletters, and promotions
  • Maintain a polished, consistent digital presence

 

Advertising & Analytics

  • Support digital advertising campaigns (Meta platforms preferred)
  • Track performance metrics and assist with reporting
  • Contribute to marketing strategies and campaign optimization

 

Events & Promotions

  • Assist in promoting club events, tournaments, and social functions
  • Capture live content and help develop promotional materials
  • Collaborate across departments to highlight club offerings

                      

Qualifications

·        Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, business, hospitality, or related field preferred

·        1–3 years of experience in marketing, social media, hospitality, or customer service

·        Familiarity with CRM systems and social media tools (Canva, Adobe, etc.)

·        Strong communication and organizational skills

                                          

Skills & Attributes

·        Creative, detail-oriented, and highly organized

·        Strong understanding of social media trends and content creation

·        Professional, friendly, and service-focused

·        Ability to balance administrative accuracy with creative marketing

 

Additional Expectations

  • Availability for occasional evenings/weekends for event coverage
  • Consistent time dedicated to content creation and planning

 

Benefits

  • Competitive pay
  • Health, dental, and vision benefits
  • Paid time off
  • Complimentary meals while at work

Its 25,000-square-foot clubhouse overlooks a championship 18-hole golf course and provides diverse amenities, including dining spaces, banquet areas, a fitness center, spa, tennis courts, a heated Olympic-size pool, and more. The Club prides itself on exceptional service, a sense of community, and a high-quality experience for all members and guests.

companyDescription: Established in 1989, Jacksonville Golf and Country Club is a private, member-owned country club centrally located on Florida's First Coast between shimmering beaches just a few minutes to our east and downtown Jacksonville to the west. Over the years it has become one of the most desirable gated communities and country clubs in the area. Our members enjoy a lifestyle with limitless recreational and social opportunities. We invite you to visit the Club for a guided tour and to experience what our members have been enjoying for a quarter century - an active, family-friendly private club that provides service second to none in a relaxed, comfortable, and safe environment. Our beautiful 25,000 square foot Clubhouse boasts sweeping views of our signature 18-hole championship golf course and of our Garden Terrace waterfall where blushing brides instantly envision saying their "I do's"​. Once inside the Clubhouse you'll discover 3 separate dining rooms (including an adults only dining room), ample banquet and private meeting space, a fitness center and group exercise room, locker rooms, spa services, golf pro shop and more. Outdoors, those seeking an active lifestyle won't be disappointed with 10 clay tennis courts, a junior Olympic size heated lap and recreation pool, playground, and golf practice facilities. At the centerpiece of the Club, our golf course, designed by Clyde Johnston and Fuzzy Zoeller, is highly regarded for its impeccable course conditions and renowned for being challenging and enjoyable for players of all handicap levels. Whether you are planning a wedding or special event or considering enhancing your lifestyle by becoming a member, we are confident that Jacksonville Golf and Country Club will exceed your expectations. We look forward to serving you!

companyDescription:
At ProPharma, we help pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies bring life-changing therapies to market. From early development through clinical, regulatory approval, and commercialization, we provide expert guidance to navigate complex challenges and accelerate success.

Our mission is simple: improving patient health and safety by delivering the highest-quality regulatory, compliance, clinical research, pharmacovigilance, and medical information services throughout the entire product lifecycle.

With over 20 years of expertise in regulatory sciences, clinical research solutions, quality & compliance, pharmacovigilance, medical information, and R&D technology, ProPharma partners with clients across the complete product lifecycle through an advise-build-operate model. Our end-to-end suite of consulting solutions de-risks and accelerates high-profile drug and device programs, ultimately improving patient health and safety. Discover how ProPharma can help you inspire the future of science!

title: Video Editor
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Outsiders Media is a full-service marketing company specializing in social media marketing for brands in the outdoor lifestyle niche.


Role Description

This is a full-time remote role for a Video Editor. The Video Editor will be responsible for producing and editing high-quality video content tailored to the outdoor lifestyle niche. This includes color grading footage, creating motion graphics, and working with graphics to enhance visual presentations. The Video Editor will collaborate closely with the team to meet client goals and deliver visually appealing and engaging content.


Qualifications

  • Proficient in Video Production and Video Editing
  • Strong skills in Video Color Grading and Motion Graphics
  • Ability to work with and integrate Graphics to elevate video content
  • Excellent attention to detail and creative problem-solving skills
  • Strong time management and ability to meet deadlines in a remote work setting
