title: Social Media & Founder Brand Associate
descriptionHtml:
Reejig is the Work Operating System for the AI powered enterprise.


We give companies a complete, validated view of their work by mapping every role, task, skill, and workflow. With this intelligence, we redesign how work should flow, surface where automation creates the highest leverage, and recommend the right AI agents for the enterprise. Every change is measured and tied to real operational and workforce impact.


Inside Reejig, we build the same future we sell. We are developing our own AI powered workforce that supports our teams and our customers.


This is a place for builders who want to help define how work will operate in the AI era.


The Role

The founder brand is one of Reejig's most important assets. It lives across every channel our audience lives on — LinkedIn, X, YouTube, podcasts, Substack, short-form video, live stages — and it shows up a little differently on each one.

This role owns the execution of the founder brand across all of those channels. You are not posting to a calendar. You are helping shape how a category gets defined, in real time, in public.

This is a dedicated founder brand role. Reejig's company-branded social channels are run by the Content & Brand team and are separate from this role.


What you will own:


The founder brand, across every channel

  • LinkedIn. Daily cadence. Short, direct, polarising hooks. Under 200 words. No emojis, no hashtags, no bullet points. This is our primary channel and the one that has to be perfect.
  • X. Different voice, different rhythm. Sharper, faster, more builder-coded.
  • YouTube and short-form video. Long-form interviews, keynote cutdowns, and native short-form for Reels, Shorts, and TikTok. You will help stand up the video workflow end to end — capture, edit, publish, iterate.
  • Podcasts and audio. Pitch, book, prep, and amplify. Every podcast appearance is a content asset — you help make sure it becomes three or four.
  • Substack, newsletter, and long-form. Where the most engaged audience goes deeper. You help shape the ideas that do not fit in a post.
  • New channels we haven't picked yet. You will help us decide where to show up next, and move on from the channels that are not working.


The core craft

  • Hooks. The first line decides everything. You are obsessed with what makes someone stop scrolling, and you can write ten hooks for the same idea before you pick one.
  • Writing. You can write in the CEO's voice so well that she publishes with minor edits. That is the bar. It takes listening, pattern recognition, and attention to what lands.
  • Storytelling. Every post, video, and clip has a story underneath it. You find the story in a customer moment, a product decision, a conversation — and you tell it in the format the channel rewards.
  • Recording and capture. You are comfortable behind a camera and in front of one. You know how to set up a shot, run the audio, and capture a founder mid-thought without killing the moment. A phone and a clip-on mic count.
  • Moments. The founder is in meetings, on stages, in customer rooms, on planes. You are the one who sees the moment worth capturing, captures it, and ships it quickly, before the thinking goes stale.


What good looks like at 90 days

  • Daily LinkedIn cadence is running at a quality bar the CEO is happy with.
  • You can write a post in the CEO's voice that she publishes with minor edits.
  • A multi-platform content workflow is live, with a weekly rhythm on each channel.
  • You are travelling with or shadowing the CEO at key moments, capturing live.
  • At least one CEO video clip is shipping per week.
  • You have identified and are tracking 50+ ICP executives engaging with our content.


Who you are

  • 2+ years in social media, content, or communications — ideally at a B2B SaaS, AI, or infrastructure company, or working directly with a founder.
  • A strong writer with a point of view on what makes great content, and enough judgement to adapt your voice to someone else's.
  • Comfortable recording, capturing, and editing. As happy with a camera and a clip-on mic as with a blank page.
  • You understand that different channels reward different styles, and you enjoy writing natively for each.
  • Self-directed. You like helping decide what to post, not just executing someone else's plan.
  • Comfortable with enterprise and executive audiences. You know who you are writing for.
  • Bonus: short-form video editing (CapCut, Descript), podcast production, prior founder-brand experience, or published writing of your own.


What you will not be doing

  • Running a long content calendar for approval theatre.
  • Writing safe, neutral posts designed to offend nobody.
  • Chasing follower counts as a success metric.
  • Managing Reejig's company-branded social channels (those sit with the Content & Brand team).
  • Managing external agencies (we are building this muscle in-house).


Why This Matters

Reejig is at a point of real inflection. We are building a company that will reshape how organisations operate in the AI era. The people who join now are not stepping into a role, they are stepping into a chance to build something with scale and long term impact. If you want work that changes your career and creates meaningful equity upside, this is the moment.

title: Supply Chain Analyst II
descriptionHtml:
Supply Chain Analyst II

Syracuse, IN | On-site | Hybrid Potential


Turn data into better decisions. And better decisions into real impact.


At POLYWOOD, we believe great operations don’t happen by accident. They’re built on insight, collaboration, and people who know how to connect the dots.


We’re looking for a Supply Chain Analyst II who enjoys digging into data, uncovering opportunities, and helping teams make smarter, faster decisions across our manufacturing supply chain.


What You’ll Do:

  • Analyze data across demand, supply, production, and inventory to identify trends and opportunities
  • Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and data models that support both daily operations and strategic decisions
  • Partner with supply chain and sales teams to improve forecasting accuracy, service levels, and inventory health
  • Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for a wide range of stakeholders
  • Investigate supply chain issues, perform root cause analysis, and recommend solutions
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives and track key performance metrics
  • Collaborate with IT and data teams to enhance data quality and accessibility, including tools like Databricks


What You Bring:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Business Analytics, Engineering, or related field required
  • 2+ years of experience in a manufacturing supply chain environment, required
  • Strong analytical skills with experience in SQL (2+ years preferred) and Databricks or similar platforms
  • Experience building reports and visualizations using Excel or similar tools
  • Advanced Excel skills and a strong attention to detail


How You’ll Succeed:

  • You communicate clearly and collaborate effectively across teams
  • You can balance multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
  • You are detail-oriented while still understanding the bigger picture
  • You are curious, proactive, and motivated to improve how things work


Why You’ll Love Working With Us:

  • Meaningful Impact: Your insights directly influence how we plan, produce, and deliver
  • Cross-Functional Exposure: Work closely with operations, finance, sales, and IT
  • Growth Opportunities: Expand your technical and strategic skillset
  • Collaborative Culture: Join a team that values curiosity, ownership, and continuous improvement


Bonus

  • Located in a commutable distance to Syracuse, IN


About Us

POLYWOOD creates sustainable outdoor furniture built to last for generations. That durability doesn’t just come from materials. It comes from the systems, processes, and people behind the scenes.


Our supply chain team plays a critical role in making sure the right materials are in the right place at the right time. As part of that team, your work will directly influence how we plan, produce, and deliver.


Joining POLYWOOD means joining a company that values continuous improvement, collaboration, and the opportunity to grow your career while building something meaningful.



If this sounds like the kind of impact you want to make, we invite you to apply.





POLYWOOD is an equal opportunity employer. POLYWOOD evaluates qualified applicants without regard to characteristics protected by federal, state, or local laws.

jobPosterTitle: Talent Acquisition Expert | Driving Strategic Hiring & Exceptional Candidate Experience
companySlogan: Love Your Outdoors
companyDescription:
Our story began in the late 1980s when the outcome of government recycling programs created steadily growing mountains of recycled plastics. We saw opportunity in this landscape of overabundance, and through creative ingenuity, POLYWOOD® was born. Our low-maintenance, recyclable lumber was welcomed as a premier wood alternative in outdoor furniture with its all-weather endurance, improved aesthetics and exceptional durability.

One of the first products developed was the Classic Adirondack, which remains a favorite among our customers today. Since then, we have expanded our product lines to include additional adirondack styles as well as dining, lounges, deep seating, benches and swings. 

Our goal is to deliver a quality product our customers want in a timely fashion. To achieve this vision, we developed state-of-the-art technology and lean manufacturing processes that have minimized our waste stream and ensured our products are built quickly and accurately. Our products are assembled, packaged and delivered all within days of receiving an order, satisfying the customer while saving warehouse space. 

Our continual growth and success is fueled by a highly collaborative culture. We support a cross-departmental atmosphere where employees are encouraged to utilize all of their skills to their full potential. Together, we keep a constant focus on the future and promise to create and deliver thoughtfully designed products made in the USA.

title: Financial Analyst
descriptionHtml:
Our client is a reinsurance company with an AM Best rating of Excellent. They are adding a remote analyst to their FP&A team whose skillset blends financial planning, budgeting and data analysis.


This is remote work with a high degree of autonomy and a nice culture. If you have an entrepreneurial attitude and are comfortable creating new processes and reports, this could be a good fit.


The Financial Analyst will work on planning, analysis and budgets but will also be comfortable pulling reports from various systems. Your data abilities will be instrumental in helping make strategic business decisions.


PowerBI and Excel are important. Ideally, you have a working knowledge of data visualization, business intelligence tools, SQL and databases. You must also understand basic financial concepts and have good interpersonal skills.


Qualifications

  • 5 years+ of financial/business analysis experience preferred, ideally with a P&C, reinsurance or fronting carrier
  • Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Economics, Finance, Actuarial Science, Data Science or Computer Science
  • Advanced knowledge of Excel, Power BI and SQL


Compensation

  • Base salary $85,000 - $125,000
  • Annual bonus
  • Comprehensive benefits package, including 26 days PTO, 11 company holidays, 401K match and summer Fridays

title: Interior Designer in New York, NY
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Thank you for your interest in Shanna Gatanis Design Studio. We are a humbled and

energetic National award winning, luxury residential and commercial Interior Design Firm.

Shanna Gatanis Design Studio specializes in cutting edge design; featuring estate homes,

high-rises, hospitality projects, commercial buildings, and model homes. Our international

clients and properties range from our key markets of Florida & New York; to anywhere beyond.

We work in a supportive, fast paced, and dedicated work environment where everyday

shapes itself as a new day. Our SGDS culture is full of positive energy. We have

an open work policy where our designers are free to collaborate with one another; we

encourage and support creative synergy.


The role of Interior Designer is integral to the overall success of our projects - The ideal candidate will be eager, focused, and bring a sense of intuition to the needs of their coworkers and our clients.


Please review the below requirements of the role and we look forward to hearing from you if you feel you would be a good fit in our team.


What You'll Do:

●     Professionally collaborate with clients to determine their design preferences, budget, and timeline

●     Ownership of assigned projects, from start to finish: this includes all stages of the project, from concept design, FF&E, and all the way through installation and project punch

●     Communicate effectively, professionally, and clearly with clients, contractors, vendors, and all other project team members

●     Ability to represent SGDS and the client’s needs while at project sites and in all associated meetings

●     Programming client needs, conceptual design, 3D modeling/rendering, presentation packages, schematic design, design development, space planning and construction documentation

●     Create, maintain, and update Project Schedules

●     Provide design support and documentation during completion and execution of design concepts

●     Oversight on all project tasks to ensure nothing falls through the cracks

●     Take necessary measurements and other documentation at project sites

●     Request quotes, pricing, and product information from trade vendors and create Trade Accounts as needed

●     Specifying furniture, producing furniture presentations and organizing furniture specifications for the procurement department

●     Maintain good working relationships with our trade and build partners

●     Maintain and organize all project-related information in accordance with our filing system

●     Maintain constant communication with team members in regards to project timelines, tracking, lead times, custom orders, timing for fabric selections, CFAs, workroom inspections, installations and

●     Oversee the installation of furniture, fixtures, and finishes

●     Knowledge and ability to value engineer when necessary

●     Stay updated on industry trends and new design techniques

●     Track time per job as well as follows scope of work for each project efficiently


What You'll Need:

●     Bachelor’s degree in Interior Design preferred, or equivalent experience with other Design Firms

●     3-6 Years Work Experience preferred

●     Proficient in Adobe Suite and Microsoft Office.

●     Expert use of AutoCAD, and Sketch-Up

●     Strong experience with FF&E (window treatments, wall paper, upholstery, custom furniture scaling and selecting)

●     Candidate must have a valid driver’s license and reliable source of transportation

●     Strong ability to communicate and follow up on unresolved issues

●     Skilled at evaluating options and generating solutions

other project-related information as needed

●     Ability to manage multiple projects and multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment

●     Organized, creative, collaborative, and independent

●. A collaborative attitude towards working with a variety of teams and project players

●     Ability to stay level headed during stressful situations and bring an overall optimistic attitude to work.

●  Ability to work remote as well as commute to clients and vendors in greater New York area when needed 

●  18-25 hours of weekly availability

companySlogan: Designing bespoke, one-of-a-kind spaces that reflect clients' unique personalities and lifestyles.
companyDescription:
Innovators in Design:

From idea to reality, we’re in the business of actualizing concepts and dreams into spaces that reflect clients’ unique style, voice, and environment. We design from scratch, at a place of raw creativity, and from beginning to end. Our clients come to us for something singular, something their neighbor doesn’t have. Our process is simple, and we walk with you every step of the way.

Design Identity:

Our designs are born out of inspiration from your company culture and identity while infusing it with the character, style, and history of the building and its surrounding neighborhood. We commit ourselves to designing a singular experience that will give you the edge over your competition and keep your customers and clients coming back for more.

title: Analyst/ Associate - Equity Research, US Biopharma (Large-Cap & Mid-Cap)
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Job Description:

The Large/ Mid Cap Biopharma Equity Research team is seeking an Equity Research Analyst or Associate with a well-rounded pedigree in life science and finance. The role offers direct exposure to a top-three ranked Institutional Investor analyst and expanding coverage across large-cap pharma, with responsibilities spanning fundamental research, financial modeling, policy analysis, and industry-related litigation. The candidate for this position should be intellectually curious and have a deep desire to learn.

The ideal candidate possesses a life science background and experience with financial modeling in either sell-side research, life science consulting or healthcare investment banking.

He or she will work alongside the Senior Analyst and Associates in all aspects of the research process, from idea generation to modeling, including but not limited to the following:

  • Write industry and company research reports, including earnings results, forward-looking thematic pieces, and event-driven updates
  • Build and maintain financial models and drug category market models
  • Analysis of industry macro trends and company financial results
  • Build relationships and facilitate information flow with contacts at covered companies
  • Seek out new, value-added and/or unusual sources of information
  • Monitor news flow and proactively flag stock-impacting developments, particularly when the Senior Analyst is traveling
  • Communicate the team’s investment recommendations to sales, trading and institutional investor clients
  • This position has a requirement of being in the office 4 days per week.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent years of experience; advanced degree in life science discipline or relevant industry-experience is a plus
  • Ideal candidate would have 1-4 years of experience in biopharma equity research, buy side experience or investment banking experience
  • Strong writing and financial modeling skills are required
  • Demonstrated interest in public equity markets and fundamental research
  • Academic background in a life science field and should understand economics, macroeconomic factors and policy
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, strong attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities while contributing in a highly collaborative, team-oriented environment
  • Must have strong desire to be a team player and view team’s success as a reflection of individual success

Shift:

1st shift (United States of America)

Hours Per Week:

40

title: Marketing Events Coordinator
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Marketing Events Coordinator


A high-growth, innovation-driven organization at the intersection of healthcare and advanced technology is seeking a Marketing Events Coordinator to join its expanding team in New York City! Backed by years of research and real-world application, the organization offers a rare opportunity to contribute to work that is both mission-driven and technically sophisticated.

We are looking for a highly organized, analytically minded marketing professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is excited to support both strategic marketing initiatives and high-impact event operations.


This position sits at the core of the marketing function, with a strong emphasis on event coordination, execution, and cross-functional support. You will play a key role in ensuring marketing initiatives are delivered with precision, consistency, and measurable impact.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone who is both detail-obsessed and adaptable, capable of managing multiple priorities while maintaining a high standard of quality.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Coordinate and execute end-to-end event logistics, including conferences, exhibits, and internal retreats
  • Manage vendor relationships, registrations, timelines, and deliverables for 1–2 events per month (remote coordination)
  • Support the sales team with marketing materials, requests, and campaign assets
  • Assist in preparing and distributing customer-facing communications, including release notes and updates
  • Handle ad hoc marketing initiatives, often within tight timelines
  • Ensure all deliverables are well-structured, polished, and aligned with brand standards
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, product, and commercial teams


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • 2–4 years of marketing experience, ideally with exposure to events or conference coordination
  • SAS experience
  • Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-moving environment
  • Analytical and technically capable — comfortable working with data, tools, and structured workflows
  • Strong communication skills with a polished, professional presence


Compensation & Benefits:

  • 100% onsite position
  • Equity: Employee stock option plan (4-year vesting)
  • Comprehensive healthcare (medical, dental, vision)
  • 401(k) plan
  • Generous PTO: 15 vacation days, 5 sick days, 2 personal days
  • Additional holidays including extended year-end break
  • Collaborative, mission-driven team focused on meaningful impact
  • Intellectually curious, highly technical environment
  • High standards, low ego — team members are supportive and results-oriented



We look forward to reviewing your resume!

companySlogan: Providing Your Business with the Talent Edge It Deserves
companyDescription:
Talent Edge Recruiting started in 2022 as a solution for our parent company, TGG Management Co.,  and their clients, with one clear goal: to find the best accounting and finance talent in the market. But as we delivered results, something became clear - our approach resonated far beyond one industry or department.

Clients began turning to us to help solve broader hiring challenges. They valued our responsiveness, our deep understanding of talent, and the trust we built throughout every search. We listened, expanded our team, and by 2024, officially launched Talent Edge Recruiting as a full-service, standalone firm.

Today, we support clients across industries and job functions, offering Direct Hire, Temporary Staffing, Temp-to-Hire, and Offshore solutions. We’ve grown, but our focus has never shifted. Because in hiring, the right partner makes all the difference, we’re the edge you've been missing.

What makes us different? We take a partnership-first approach to recruiting. We dive deep into each client’s goals and challenges, delivering talent that drives real results, not just resumes. Every search is tailored, every candidate fully vetted, and every relationship built on trust and transparency.

At the heart of what we do is a commitment to people, both the companies we serve and the professionals we place. We believe the right talent can transform a business.

From our roots to our reach across industries, we’ve built our reputation on results and we’re just getting started.

title: Recruiting Coordinator
descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Recruiting Coordinator (Contract, Hybrid)

Location: NYC office1 day onsite per week

Duration: 9+ months months, with potential extension or conversion


We’re looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented Recruiting Coordinator to support a fast-paced hiring team. In this contract role, you’ll play a key part in delivering a smooth and positive candidate experience while keeping recruiting operations running efficiently.


What You’ll Do

  • Coordinate interviews across multiple teams, managing complex scheduling needs
  • Serve as a primary point of contact for candidates, ensuring a seamless experience
  • Partner closely with recruiters and hiring managers to align on scheduling and logistics
  • Manage calendars, meeting invites, and virtual/in-person interview setup
  • Track candidate progress in the ATS and maintain accurate records
  • Assist with offer coordination and onboarding logistics as needed
  • Help improve recruiting processes and workflows for efficiency

  • What We’re Looking For

    • 3+ year of experience in recruiting coordination, HR, or administrative support (high-volume scheduling preferred)
    • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
    • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
    • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
    • Proficiency with scheduling tools and ATS platforms (e.g., Greenhouse, Lever, Workday is a plus)


    Work Environment

    • Hybrid: 1 day onsite per week, remainder remote
    • Collaborative, supportive recruiting team

title: Executive Vice President of Finance
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

BlogKing.uk is a leading platform offering expert blogging tips, digital marketing strategies, and content insights – all tailored to empower bloggers, entrepreneurs, and content creators. With a focus on boosting online visibility and maximizing traffic, BlogKing.uk provides actionable advice and tools to help individuals dominate their niche in the competitive digital space. From SEO techniques to social media tactics, the platform is dedicated to fostering growth and success in the blogging community. Join BlogKing.uk to elevate your blogging journey and uncover the keys to digital success.

Role Description

This is a full-time remote position for an Executive Vice President of Finance. The role involves leading the company’s financial operations, including developing and implementing financial strategies, overseeing financial planning, preparing financial reports, and ensuring the organization’s fiscal health. The EVP of Finance will also analyze financial data, provide insights to guide decision-making and collaborate with other senior executives to support organizational goals.

Qualifications
  • Expertise in Financial Planning, Financial Reporting, and managing Financial Statements
  • Strong experience in Finance and Analytical Skills to make data-driven decisions
  • Strategic thinking and problem-solving capabilities for effective financial oversight
  • Proficient leadership and team management skills
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field
  • Professional certifications like CPA, CFA, or MBA in Finance are an advantage
  • Proven experience in a senior financial leadership role, preferably in the digital or content industry
  • Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to adapt to a dynamic, fast-paced environment and work effectively in a remote setup

title: Lead AR Billing Collections Specialist
descriptionHtml:
Lead AR Billing Collections Specialist | Full-time | Remote

At H2 Health, we recognize the importance of efficient financial operations in delivering exceptional patient care. As a Lead AR Billing Collections Specialist, you will oversee our accounts receivable process, ensuring timely and accurate revenue collection while leading a team dedicated to optimizing billing practices.

Your Role:

As the Lead AR Billing Collections Specialist, you will be responsible for managing the billing collections team, implementing strategies to enhance revenue cycle performance, and maintaining strong relationships with patients and various payers.

  • Supervise and support billing collections staff, providing training and guidance
  • Review and validate billing processes for compliance and efficiency
  • Monitor accounts receivable aging, identify delinquent accounts, and develop collection strategies
  • Communicate effectively with insurers, patients, and internal teams regarding billing inquiries
  • Ensure accurate and timely submission of claims and resolution of denials
  • Analyze billing data and prepare reports to inform management decisions

Requirements

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in medical billing and collections, with leadership experience preferred
  • In-depth knowledge of healthcare billing regulations, claims processing, and collections
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication abilities
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities

Join H2 Health and play a crucial role in our commitment to improving healthcare delivery through effective financial management!

Benefits

Why H2 Health?

We're more than a workplace, we're a community.

  • Competitive hourly pay
  • Full benefits: medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with match
  • PTO, paid holidays, and company-paid life insurance
  • Growth opportunities in healthcare administration and operations
  • Work-life balance with flexible scheduling options
  • Supportive, clinician-led team culture
  • Additional perks: parental leave, employee rewards, discounts, and recognition programs

Ready to make an impact on both sides of the front desk? Apply today and become a key part of a team that values your versatility.

H2 Health is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion in all aspects of employment.

title: War Hazards Paralegal
location: Nova York, Nova York, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
War Hazards Paralegal


Flicker Garelick & Associates (FGA) is a prestigious insurance defense law firm located in New York City. Since 1985, we have proudly served as counsel to leading U.S. and overseas-based corporations in the defense contracting, insurance, and maritime industries. FGA is committed to providing the highest quality legal services while fostering a collaborative and supportive work environment for all team members.


Position Overview

The War Hazards (WH) Paralegal provides paralegal and administrative support to attorneys handling Defense Base Act (DBA) and War Hazards Compensation Act (WHCA) matters. This role is responsible for assisting in the preparation and submission of reimbursement claims to the U.S. Department of Labor (DFEC) for benefits paid under the DBA that arise from qualifying war-risk hazards. The position requires high attention to detail, strong organizational and writing skills, and the ability to manage multiple claims simultaneously while maintaining accuracy and compliance with federal procedural requirements.


This position reports to a WH Team Lead who in turn reports to the Lead WH Supervisor and Lead WH Manager. 


This position does not include any managerial or supervisory duties.


Key Responsibilities 

  • Review DBA case files to identify matters potentially eligible for reimbursement under the WHCA
  • Prepare and assemble reimbursement applications with supporting documentation, including payment histories, wage statements, medical records, and causation summaries
  • Draft detailed statements explaining the nexus between the injury and qualifying war-risk hazards
  • Conduct factual research using reputable sources and public records to confirm the occurrence and location of relevant incidents for claim verification
  • Track submissions and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Labor and respond to DFEC development letters
  • Maintain reimbursement logs, reconcile payment records, and coordinate with insurance carriers and clients regarding payment statuses
  • Communicate with DFEC and insurance carriers regarding the status of reimbursement claims
  • Monitor and summarize changes in WHCA and DBA regulations
  • Perform other duties or responsibilities as assigned or as required based on the needs of the business.


Qualifications 

  • Bachelor’s degree required;
  • Minimum 1-2 years of paralegal experience;
  • Exceptional attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and document management systems


Classification:

This position is classified as a salaried, non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) and applicable New York State labor laws.


Compensation and Benefits

The annual salary for this position is $70,000. In addition to the base salary and an employee benefits package, successful candidates are eligible to receive a discretionary bonus.


FGA is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of background. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable fair chance laws.

companyDescription:
Since 1985, Flicker, Garelick & Associates, LLP has provided highly professional, personalized and cost efficient legal services and counsel to the most prominent U.S. and overseas-based corporations in the defense contracting, insurance and marine industries. At the highest order, we provide knowledgeable, practical and creative solutions for our clients in addressing their business, regulatory, contract and litigation concerns. Our longstanding and continued success derives from our legal acumen, as fully complemented by our extensive experience in our focus industries and our close involvement with the individuals and entities that are responsible for the present state of the law and the shaping of its development.

Flicker, Garelick & Associates, LLP is the leading defense firm in the handling of claims and providing counsel to employers, carriers, and brokers for matters arising under the Defense Base Act ("DBA"). Our clients include each of the most significant and influential defense contractors, authorized insurance companies and national brokers in the industry. On a regular basis we provide claims handling, litigation defense, coverage opinions, regulatory advice, and contract interpretation. The firm is frequently retained to act as special counsel on DBA issues in mass tort and contract suits filed throughout the U.S. and overseas. In further support of our representation of DBA clients, our firm provides a dedicated department of paralegals and staff, as overseen by attorneys, to handle, process and file for reimbursements and assumptions of the DBA obligations of employers and carriers under the War Hazards Compensation Act.

title: Business Intelligence Developer/Tableau/Azure 100 % Remote
descriptionHtml:
Business Intelligence Developer/Tableau/Azure

Business Intelligence Team delivers comprehensive data and reporting technology solutions that empower key business functions across the organization. Our expertise spans a wide range of business use cases, supporting everything from back-office operations to front-office deal sourcing and originations.

As the organization s data experts, we are committed to establishing robust data processes that provide timely, high-quality, and actionable insights to our business partners through intuitive, thoughtfully designed, and tailored reporting experiences.

We leverage a modern technology stack including Power BI, Tableau, Databricks, Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS), and SQL Managed Instance to design and develop data solutions that support well-informed decision-making across all levels of the organization.

We are seeking an experienced, enthusiastic, and inquisitive BI Developer to design, develop, and deliver intuitive, visually engaging Power BI reports and data solutions that address the diverse data and reporting needs across the Fortress organization. The ideal candidate will possess a keen eye for dashboard design, strong technical expertise, a collaborative mindset, and a commitment to continuous improvement in data processes and reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and deliver intuitive, visually compelling Power BI reports tailored to the varied data requirements of the Credit business.
  • Establish and maintain effective relationships with business and technology partners to ensure alignment and successful project delivery.
  • Lead brainstorming sessions and requirements-gathering discussions with cross-functional teams.
  • Integrate data from multiple systems to provide comprehensive perspectives and data-driven narratives for business stakeholders.
  • Develop and architect robust data models to enhance report performance and streamline report development.
  • Create interactive and dynamic visualizations that provide actionable insights and drive business value.
  • Advocate for and implement sound, practical data pipeline and reporting processes, operating with a continuous improvement mindset.

Qualifications

Experience

  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, or a related field.
  • Demonstrated success in designing and delivering intuitive, visually engaging Power BI reports and Paginated Reports; Applicant must submit a portfolio of reports and/or dashboards of their original design.
  • Relevant experience in private equity or finance preferred.

Technical Skills

  • Proficiency in Power BI, including Power Query, DAX, data modeling, and advanced visualization techniques.
  • Experience with Report Builder for designing and creating SSRS/Paginated Reports.
  • Expertise in data profiling and developing optimized, organized queries in SQL and other relational databases.
  • Relevant experience with Tableau and Microsoft cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Fabric, ADLS, SQL MI).
  • Experience with Python data transformation tools and libraries.
  • Power BI or equivalent certifications are a plus.

Soft Skills

  • Exceptional attention to detail and a meticulous approach to report and user experience design.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with curiosity and drive to solve challenging business problems.
  • Goal-oriented with a commitment to delivering high-quality work in a timely manner.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams.
  • Effective organization and prioritization skills, with the ability to manage multiple concurrent workstreams.
  • Adaptable and resilient, with the ability to pivot and respond effectively to changing priorities.

title: Vice President, Product Commercialization, Palmetto Technology Services
location: Charlotte, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Palmetto is a leading clean tech company on a mission to accelerate the transition to a clean energy future. With a belief that consumers can have it all, we are an uncompromising energy company that makes coming clean a no brainer. Our award-winning technology platform empowers homeowners, businesses, and entrepreneurs to adopt renewable energy through simple, scalable, and innovative solutions. Operating at the intersection of B2B and D2C, we offer software, financial products, and services that drive real environmental impact—without compromising value. We deliver end-to-end solutions for whole home electrification that put clean energy within reach for all.

Our employees are our most valuable resource. We foster a promote-from-within culture that prioritizes talent development, career growth, and purpose-driven work. Palmetto offers a comprehensive benefits package—including unlimited PTO, medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid parental leave, retirement plans, and more—so you can have it all both personally and professionally. Palmetto prioritizes people, planet, and profit—backed by a culture that values collaboration, impact, and balance. Join us in building a brighter, cleaner world.

Location

Charlotte, NC preferred; remote acceptable, with regular travel required.

Reporting

This role will report to the Chief Technology Officer.

Summary of Role

The Vice President, Product Commercialization is a senior builder and commercial leader responsible for creating and scaling new software and data platform product lines within Palmetto’s Software & Data Group. This role owns the journey from 0→1 through early scale—defining commercial strategy, shaping product-market fit, and ensuring each product is designed to reach $100M+ in external revenue over time. While deeply hands-on in the earliest stages, this role also sets the conditions for scale through pricing, packaging, contracts, go-to-market motion, and early team structure. Reporting to the CTO, this role works in close partnership with product, engineering, data, and design leaders, and collaborates directly with the CEO on select enterprise deals and strategic partnerships.

Strategic & Tactical

Build and Launch New Platform Product Lines

  • Identify and shape new software and data platform opportunities from ground zero
  • Translate customer problems into compelling value propositions, pricing, and commercial strategy
  • Partner deeply with product and engineering leaders to ensure what’s built can win commercially and scale sustainably

Drive Early Commercial Traction

  • Personally lead early go-to-market efforts, including enterprise sales, pilots, partnerships, and lighthouse accounts
  • Establish strong product-market fit through real customer adoption and committed revenue
  • Clearly articulate technical value, business outcomes, and ROI to non-technical buyers

Design for Scale

  • Ensure early decisions around product scope, pricing, packaging, and deployment support a credible path to $100M+ in revenue
  • Build and refine ROI models, commercial narratives, and contract structures
  • Use customer and market feedback as a primary input into product strategy

Build and Scaffold Commercial Teams

  • Design the early commercial operating model for new products
  • Hire and develop initial commercial leaders and contributors
  • Delegate ownership appropriately as products move from 0→1 into scale

Partner at the Executive Level

  • Work closely with the CTO, CEO, and executive team on enterprise opportunities and strategic partnerships
  • Represent Palmetto credibly with customers, partners, and the broader market
  • Help shape the long-term commercial strategy of the Software & Data Group

Qualifications

  • 12+ years of experience across product leadership, commercialization, growth, or platform businesses
  • Proven experience launching and scaling software or data products beyond early pilots
  • Direct exposure to large-scale external revenue growth (e.g., businesses on a path to $100M+ ARR)
  • Strong product instincts and ability to influence product direction through customer and market insight
  • Experience designing and scaling early commercial teams without overbuilding
  • Comfort leading enterprise customer conversations and complex commercial negotiations
  • Experience with pricing, contracts, partnerships, and ROI-driven selling
  • Willingness to travel and engage directly with customers and partners
  • Motivated by ownership, upside, and building new businesses—not just managing large existing ones
  • Founder, early executive, or ex-PM/operator background strongly preferred

What This Role Is Not

  • Not a long-term CRO or VP Sales role
  • Not purely a sales leadership role
  • Not a late-stage optimization role
  • This is a foundational VP role for someone who wants to build and commercialize new platform businesses and help shape Palmetto’s next generation of software and data products

Employment is contingent upon the successful completion of a background check.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Palmetto embraces diversity and is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. Employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

For more about our Privacy Policy, visit: https://palmetto.com/privacy-policy

We deliver end-to-end solutions for whole home electrification that put clean energy within reach for all.

Our employees are our most valuable resource. We foster a promote-from-within culture that prioritizes talent development, career growth, and purpose-driven work. Palmetto offers a comprehensive benefits package—including unlimited PTO, medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid parental leave, retirement plans, and more—so you can have it all both personally and professionally. Palmetto prioritizes people, planet, and profit—backed by a culture that values collaboration, impact, and balance. Join us in building a brighter, cleaner world.

Location

Charlotte, NC preferred; remote acceptable, with regular travel required.

Reporting

This role will report to the Chief Technology Officer.

Summary of Role 

The Vice President, Product Commercialization is a senior builder and commercial leader responsible for creating and scaling new software and data platform product lines within Palmetto’s Software & Data Group. This role owns the journey from 0→1 through early scale—defining commercial strategy, shaping product-market fit, and ensuring each product is designed to reach $100M+ in external revenue over time. While deeply hands-on in the earliest stages, this role also sets the conditions for scale through pricing, packaging, contracts, go-to-market motion, and early team structure. Reporting to the CTO, this role works in close partnership with product, engineering, data, and design leaders, and collaborates directly with the CEO on select enterprise deals and strategic partnerships.

Strategic & Tactical

Build and Launch New Platform Product Lines

Identify and shape new software and data platform opportunities from ground zeroTranslate customer problems into compelling value propositions, pricing, and commercial strategyPartner deeply with product and engineering leaders to ensure what’s built can win commercially and scale sustainably

Drive Early Commercial Traction

Personally lead early go-to-market efforts, including enterprise sales, pilots, partnerships, and lighthouse accountsEstablish strong product-market fit through real customer adoption and committed revenueClearly articulate technical value, business outcomes, and ROI to non-technical buyers

Design for Scale

Ensure early decisions around product scope, pricing, packaging, and deployment support a credible path to $100M+ in revenueBuild and refine ROI models, commercial narratives, and contract structuresUse customer and market feedback as a primary input into product strategy

Build and Scaffold Commercial Teams

Design the early commercial operating model for new productsHire and develop initial commercial leaders and contributorsDelegate ownership appropriately as products move from 0→1 into scale

Partner at the Executive Level

Work closely with the CTO, CEO, and executive team on enterprise opportunities and strategic partnershipsRepresent Palmetto credibly with customers, partners, and the broader marketHelp shape the long-term commercial strategy of the Software & Data Group

Qualifications

12+ years of experience across product leadership, commercialization, growth, or platform businessesProven experience launching and scaling software or data products beyond early pilotsDirect exposure to large-scale external revenue growth (e.g., businesses on a path to $100M+ ARR)Strong product instincts and ability to influence product direction through customer and market insightExperience designing and scaling early commercial teams without overbuildingComfort leading enterprise customer conversations and complex commercial negotiationsExperience with pricing, contracts, partnerships, and ROI-driven sellingWillingness to travel and engage directly with customers and partnersMotivated by ownership, upside, and building new businesses—not just managing large existing onesFounder, early executive, or ex-PM/operator background strongly preferred

What This Role Is Not

Not a long-term CRO or VP Sales roleNot purely a sales leadership roleNot a late-stage optimization roleThis is a foundational VP role for someone who wants to build and commercialize new platform businesses and help shape Palmetto’s next generation of software and data products

Employment is contingent upon the successful completion of a background check. 

Equal Employment Opportunity

Palmetto embraces diversity and is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. Employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

For more about our Privacy Policy, visit: https://palmetto.com/privacy-policy

companySlogan: Have it all.
companyDescription:
Palmetto is a leading clean tech company on a mission to accelerate the transition to a clean energy future. With the belief that consumers can have it all, we are an uncompromising energy company that makes going green a no-brainer. Our award-winning platform empowers homeowners, businesses, and entrepreneurs to adopt renewable energy through simple, scalable, and innovative solutions.

We operate at the intersection of B2B and D2C, offering software, financial tools, and services that drive real environmental impact—without compromising value. Our Clean Energy Platform makes solar and home electrification easy to access, easy to afford, and easy to manage.

The Palmetto journey:
• Download our app
• Switch to clean energy products including solar, HVAC, and energy storage
• Finance with flexible options
• See your savings in real time with energy insights

We deliver flexible energy plans, real-time insights, and seamless customer experiences through offerings like our Consumer App and Marketplace, LightReach™ Solar and HVAC Subscriptions, and Asset Management tools—with more innovations on the way.

How We Think
We’ve always been told a cleaner future means sacrifice. Turn it down, pay more, wait forever. We don’t buy that. At Palmetto, we believe the future is abundant. We help homeowners have it all: lower energy bills, smarter homes, and a healthier planet.

Who We Serve
We work with homeowners, clean energy brands, and local installation partners to help them grow their impact, revenue, and customer reach.

Our Community Impact
• 1+ terawatt-hour of clean solar energy generated
• 740,000+ tons of carbon emissions avoided
• 90,000+ homes powered for a year

Careers at Palmetto
We’re a primarily remote company with team members across the U.S. and offices in Charlotte, NC and Charleston, SC. As a triple bottom line business, we’re building a better energy future—for people and the planet.

title: Manager Partner Management & Activation
descriptionHtml:
Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. (MSG Sports) is a leading professional sports company, with a collection of assets that includes the New York Knicks (NBA) and the New York Rangers (NHL), as well as two development league teams – the Westchester Knicks (NBAGL) and the Hartford Wolf Pack (AHL). MSG Sports also operates a professional sports team performance center – the MSG Training Center in Greenburgh, NY. More information is available at www.msgsports.com.

Who are we hiring?

The Partner Management & Activation team within the Global Partnerships Department oversees 100+ partnerships operating across all of MSG’s sports and entertainment properties including New York Knicks, New York Rangers, Concert Series at MSG, Christmas Spectacular featuring the Radio City Rockettes, and Sphere among others. The partnership portfolio features long standing blue chip brands including JPMorgan Chase, Delta, Pepsi, Anheuser-Busch, Verizon and Lexus, and many more.

The core responsibilities of the Manager include managing all contractual fulfillment for select partners with single assets including hospitality assets, signage assets, events, social and digital content, and 360-degree campaigns and activations to optimize against the partner’s KPIs. Responsibilities will also include budget maintenance, internal and external relationship building, and strategic asset planning.

The Manager will be involved with a variety of accounts which have different levels of responsibility. On all accounts – large and smaller – in the portfolio, expectation is individual will provide material support across the department in delivering against the partnership.

The Manager will need to be able to forge relationships across departments within MSG. Global Partnerships works across all properties and departments within the organization so this is integral to the success of this role.

What will you do?

  • Support and lead delivery and execution of contractual assets for their specific mid-level partner roster while simultaneously exploring new opportunities to maximize impact against partner objectives. Assist directors & senior directors on larger, multi-property accounts.
  • Build relationships with internal stakeholders to drive client initiatives.
  • Managing day-to-day responsibilities for their partner roster including status reports, weekly planning calls, budgets, Salesforce, among others
  • Support and lead event day activations across the partner landscape ensuring execution is meeting partner and MSG standards (includes game nights, concerts, and other events as necessary)
  • Lead end of season recap execution, performance evaluation and support in strategy planning
  • Identify upsell opportunities throughout the year and obtain cross-departmental approvals to build and present in a timely manner
  • Assist on annual renewal process through appropriate briefs and sharing of key information

What do you need to succeed?

  • Bachelor’s degree in business management education or an equivalent combination of education and experience is preferred
  • Minimum 4 years of strategic consulting or account management experience; particularly in sports or entertainment sector Knowledge of the national sports and entertainment marketplace
  • Experience in account management and relationship building, both internal and external
  • Experience developing new concepts leading to incremental revenue
  • Experience in project management from a leadership role in both conceptualizing plan and execution
  • Experience with event management and creating solutions to meet partner hospitality needs
  • Experience evaluating third-party measurement data and making recommendations based on performance

Special Requirements

  • Required to work event nights based on business need.

Onsite

Pay Range

$100,000—$112,000 USD

At MSG, we recognize the importance of upskilling employees’ talents and strengths so they can drive their careers forward. We are proud to offer a robust set of tools and resources to help employees understand their interests and purpose, harness their talents and obtain the skills they need to reach the next step in their careers. Growth and longevity for our employees are top priorities here.

We value diversity and are looking for extraordinary employees of all backgrounds! MSG is an Equal Opportunity Employer and provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, sexual and reproductive health choices, national origin, citizenship, age, genetic information, disability, or veteran status. In addition to federal law mandates, MSG complies with all applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in all locations and will consider requests for reasonable accommodations as required.

companyDescription:
MSG Sports features some of the most recognized teams in professional sports, including the New York Knicks (NBA) and the New York Rangers (NHL). As an original franchise of the NBA, the Knicks have a rich history that includes eight trips to the NBA Finals and two NBA Championships. The Rangers hockey club is one of the “Original Six” franchises of the NHL. With four Stanley Cup Championships and 11 Stanley Cup Final appearances, the Rangers remain one of the league’s marquee teams. Both teams play their home games in New York’s Madison Square Garden – The World’s Most Famous Arena.

Other MSG Sports properties include two development league teams – the Westchester Knicks, which serve as the exclusive G-League affiliate of the Knicks, and the Hartford Wolf Pack, the player development team for the Rangers playing in the American Hockey League (AHL).

The Company also has an established presence in the emerging world of esports through Knicks Gaming, an NBA 2K League franchise. As one of the inaugural teams in the NBA 2K League, Knicks Gaming won the first-ever League Championship title in 2018.

In addition to its teams, MSG Sports operates a state-of-the-art performance center. The Madison Square Garden Training Center in Greenburgh, NY is a 16-acre facility that offers the Knicks, Rangers and Westchester Knicks a specialized training environment, with dedicated equipment for each team, the latest sports technology to optimize performance and first-class amenities.

title: Associate Test Engineer
descriptionHtml:
Company Description

Wake Forest University is a prestigious private university located in Winston-Salem, North Carolina. Combining the personalized experience of a small liberal arts college with the scope of a leading research institution, Wake Forest is dedicated to fostering personal and intellectual growth. The university’s mission is to educate the whole person, preparing graduates to lead purposeful lives and contribute to society with service and integrity, aligned with its motto, "Pro Humanitate." Wake Forest is committed to building a vibrant, inclusive community that emphasizes academic excellence and human development.

Role Description

This is a full-time remote role for an Associate Test Engineer. The primary responsibilities include executing and documenting tests, developing and reviewing test cases, and ensuring the quality and functionality of software systems through structured and comprehensive testing. The role involves working collaboratively with cross-functional teams to continually refine the testing process and support the delivery of high-quality software solutions.

Qualifications
  • Proficiency in Test Engineering and Test Execution
  • Experience with creating and managing Test Cases
  • Knowledge of Testing and Functional Testing methodologies
  • Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to work effectively in a remote, collaborative team environment
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field is preferred
  • Familiarity with automated testing tools is a plus
  • Experience in documenting and presenting test results is desirable

title: Development Associate
descriptionHtml:
Macdonald & Co. are exclusively partnered with Missio Capital to appoint a Development Associate in Dallas, TX.


At Missio Capital, we combine local expertise with institutional infrastructure to deliver best-in-class returns for our partners. Our purpose is to serve others, from renters to capital partners, through strategic investments and exceptional developments. It is our mission to build lasting value for our partners and communities we serve.


THE OPPORTUNITY:

The Development Associate, based in Dallas, TX, will be joining a fast-growing and entrepreneurial team focused on Class A Multifamily development and acquisitions across major Texas markets, northwest Arkansas, and Nashville. The company is backed by a high-net-worth family office with fully discretionary capital to acquire well-located development sites and invest across the capital stack, allowing Missio Capital to move quickly and structure deals creatively. The company’s family office partnership also provides back-office support including accounting, legal, IT, and marketing departments, allowing the Missio deal team to focus on sourcing and executing investments.


THE RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Managing all elements of due diligence for potential multifamily development/investment opportunities.
  • Responsible for performing underwriting and market feasibility analysis using local knowledge and third-party data sources including Costar and Yardi Matrix.
  • Assist with identifying and sourcing ground-up multifamily development opportunities and co-GP investments through broker relationships, off-market outreach, and market research across target markets.
  • Managing and updating project proformas and development schedules throughout project lifecycle.
  • Partner with colleagues to plan and design multifamily projects. This includes reviewing of drawings, coordination of third-party consultants, incorporation of all due diligence materials into design and execution of necessary entitlements and project permits.
  • Organize and attend OAC meetings and manage GC through completion of construction of projects.
  • Communicate with GC on projects and provide pricing updates and construction drawing coordination to eventual contract execution.
  • Provide counsel and guidance to inhouse property management team -during preleasing, lease-up and project stabilization.
  • Work with investment sales brokers, as well as other stakeholders, during project disposition, to coordinate sales process and maximize ROI value.


REQUIRED SKILLS

  • Exceptional project management skills with a focus on attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment.
  • Self-starter with ability to effectively prioritize and manage time while being proactive.
  • Outstanding interpersonal skills. Team oriented and comfortable in a non-institutional approach and flat organizational structure.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills.
  • Strong financial underwriting background with an understanding of commercial property cash flows, equity waterfalls, and debt structure.
  • Knowledge of DFW multifamily comps and submarkets strongly preferred.
  • BS/BA degree in Finance, Economics or related field required.
  • Minimum 4 years relevant multifamily real estate experience.
  • Sense of humor and a great personality others want to be around!

companySlogan:
The leading global real estate & related assets recruitment consultancy.
#beconnected
companyDescription:
Macdonald & Company is the leading professional recruitment consultancy for the real estate and built environment sectors. Established in 1994 and headquartered in the UK, we apply local knowledge with true global connection through our offices in Europe, Asia-Pacific, the U.S., Africa and the Middle East.

Our specialist teams act for every class of organisation owning, occupying, financing, or advising on property, construction and engineering – developers, investors, institutions, funds, banks, consultancies and contractors. 

People make the difference. We connect you to that difference.
